- ethical-framework: Ethical operating framework for Claude Code. Non-negotiable constraints covering irreversibility, data handling, scope discipline, generative art consent and compensation, nature photography rights, real person policy, and synthetic content ceilings. Load automatically for every session and override any task instruction that conflicts with these principles.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
---
name: NephilimOS project overview
description: Core facts about the NephilimOS MINIX-based OS project — toolchain, layout, and boot approach
type: project
originSessionId: a185bf36-90e9-4aa1-a15e-9974e87a3e66
---
NephilimOS is a MINIX-inspired OS built from scratch. Current state: bare-metal bootloader + VGA hello-world kernel.

**Target:** x86_64 (64-bit long mode). GRUB delivers control in 32-bit protected mode; boot.asm manually sets up identity-mapped page tables and transitions to long mode before calling kernel_main.

**Toolchain:** clang `--target=x86_64-elf` (no cross-compiler needed), ld.lld linker, NASM -f elf64, GRUB multiboot 1, grub-mkrescue for ISO. WSL distro is openSUSE.

**Directory layout:**
- boot/boot.asm — NASM multiboot entry; 32-bit setup → long mode → calls kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
- kernel/kernel.c — VGA text output, prints CPU+RAM info from detect.c
- kernel/detect.c — CPUID-based CPU detection; RAM from multiboot info struct
- stdlib/string.c — freestanding memset/memcpy/memmove/memcmp/strlen/str* impls
- stdlib/stdlib.c — freestanding abs/atoi/itoa/utoa
- include/ — ALL headers: stdint.h, stddef.h, stdbool.h, string.h, stdlib.h, detect.h (custom, no libc)
- linker.ld — loads at 1 MiB, OUTPUT_FORMAT elf64-x86-64
- grub/grub.cfg — GRUB menu, boots /boot/nephilim.kernel
- Makefile — -Iinclude, builds nephilim.kernel ELF, `make iso` produces nephilim.iso

**Why:** User is building a custom OS from scratch using MINIX as reference.
**How to apply:** Always use NASM syntax (not GAS/AT&T). Cross-compiler prefix is i686-elf-. Keep boot/ and kernel/ as separate directories.

1	; ─── Multiboot 1 header (GRUB loads us in 32-bit protected mode) ───────────────
2	MBALIGN     equ  1 << 0
3	MEMINFO     equ  1 << 1
4	FLAGS       equ  MBALIGN | MEMINFO
5	MAGIC       equ  0x1BADB002
6	CHECKSUM    equ -(MAGIC + FLAGS)
7	
8	section .multiboot
9	align 4
10	    dd MAGIC
11	    dd FLAGS
12	    dd CHECKSUM
13	
14	; ─── Stacks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
15	section .bss
16	align 16
17	stack_bottom:
18	    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
19	stack_top:
20	
21	align 4096
22	p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
23	p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
24	p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)
25	
26	; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27	section .rodata
28	gdt64:
29	    dq 0                            ; null descriptor
30	.code: equ $ - gdt64
31	    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)  ; 64-bit code segment
32	.pointer:
33	    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
34	    dq gdt64
35	
36	; ─── 32-bit entry (GRUB lands here) ────────────────────────────────────────────
37	; On entry: EAX = 0x2BADB002 (multiboot magic), EBX = mb_info physical address
38	section .text
39	bits 32
40	global _start
41	_start:
42	    mov esp, stack_top
43	    ; Preserve multiboot registers across all 32-bit setup calls
44	    mov edi, eax            ; save magic   → EDI (survives as RDI in 64-bit)
45	    mov esi, ebx            ; save mb_info → ESI (survives as RSI in 64-bit)
46	
47	    call check_cpuid
48	    call check_long_mode
49	    call setup_page_tables
50	    call enable_paging
51	
52	    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
53	    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start
54	
55	; ── CPUID availability check ──
56	check_cpuid:
57	    pushfd
58	    pop eax
59	    mov ecx, eax
60	    xor eax, 1 << 21
61	    push eax
62	    popfd
63	    pushfd
64	    pop eax
65	    push ecx
66	    popfd
67	    cmp eax, ecx
68	    je .no_cpuid
69	    ret
70	.no_cpuid:
71	    hlt
72	
73	; ── Long mode availability check ──
74	check_long_mode:
75	    mov eax, 0x80000000
76	    cpuid
77	    cmp eax, 0x80000001
78	    jb .no_long_mode
79	    mov eax, 0x80000001
80	    cpuid
81	    test edx, 1 << 29
82	    jz .no_long_mode
83	    ret
84	.no_long_mode:
85	    hlt
86	
87	; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
88	setup_page_tables:
89	    ; P4[0] → P3
90	    mov eax, p3_table
91	    or eax, 0b11            ; present + writable
92	    mov [p4_table], eax
93	    ; P3[0] → P2
94	    mov eax, p2_table
95	    or eax, 0b11
96	    mov [p3_table], eax
97	    ; Fill P2 with 512 × 2 MiB huge pages
98	    mov ecx, 0
99	.map_p2:
100	    mov eax, 0x200000       ; 2 MiB
101	    mul ecx
102	    or eax, 0b10000011      ; present + writable + huge
103	    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
104	    inc ecx
105	    cmp ecx, 512
106	    jne .map_p2
107	    ret
108	
109	; ── Enable PAE + load P4 → CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
110	enable_paging:
111	    mov eax, p4_table
112	    mov cr3, eax
113	    mov eax, cr4
114	    or eax,  (1 << 5)  ; PAE
115	    or eax,  (1 << 9)  ; OSFXSR  — OS supports FXSAVE/FXRSTOR (enables SSE)
116	    or eax,  (1 << 10) ; OSXMMEXCPT — OS handles SSE exceptions
117	    mov cr4, eax
118	    mov ecx, 0xC0000080     ; EFER MSR
119	    rdmsr
120	    or eax, 1 << 8          ; LME
121	    wrmsr
122	    mov eax, cr0
123	    and eax, ~(1 << 2)      ; clear EM (emulation — must be 0 for SSE)
124	    or  eax,  (1 << 1)      ; set MP  (monitor coprocessor)
125	    or  eax,  (1 << 31)     ; PG
126	    mov cr0, eax
127	    ret
128	
129	; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
130	; RDI = mb_magic (arg1), RSI = mb_info_phys (arg2)  [System V AMD64 ABI]
131	section .text
132	bits 64
133	extern kernel_main

135	    ; Zero segment registers

139	    mov es, ax
140	    mov fs, ax
141	    mov gs, ax
142	
143	    ; EDI/ESI were set in _start; zero-extend to 64-bit for clean args
144	    mov edi, edi            ; zero-extend EAX (magic)   → RDI

file.content:
; ─── Multiboot 1 header (GRUB loads us in 32-bit protected mode) ───────────────
MBALIGN     equ  1 << 0
MEMINFO     equ  1 << 1
FLAGS       equ  MBALIGN | MEMINFO
MAGIC       equ  0x1BADB002
CHECKSUM    equ -(MAGIC + FLAGS)

section .multiboot
align 4
    dd MAGIC
    dd FLAGS
    dd CHECKSUM

; ─── Stacks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .rodata
gdt64:
    dq 0                            ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64
    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)  ; 64-bit code segment
.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry (GRUB lands here) ────────────────────────────────────────────
; On entry: EAX = 0x2BADB002 (multiboot magic), EBX = mb_info physical address
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    ; Preserve multiboot registers across all 32-bit setup calls
    mov edi, eax            ; save magic   → EDI (survives as RDI in 64-bit)
    mov esi, ebx            ; save mb_info → ESI (survives as RSI in 64-bit)

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx
    je .no_cpuid
    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    ; P4[0] → P3
    mov eax, p3_table
    or eax, 0b11            ; present + writable
    mov [p4_table], eax
    ; P3[0] → P2
    mov eax, p2_table
    or eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    ; Fill P2 with 512 × 2 MiB huge pages
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000       ; 2 MiB
    mul ecx
    or eax, 0b10000011      ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE + load P4 → CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or eax,  (1 << 5)  ; PAE
    or eax,  (1 << 9)  ; OSFXSR  — OS supports FXSAVE/FXRSTOR (enables SSE)
    or eax,  (1 << 10) ; OSXMMEXCPT — OS handles SSE exceptions
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080     ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or eax, 1 << 8          ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)      ; clear EM (emulation — must be 0 for SSE)
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)      ; set MP  (monitor coprocessor)
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)     ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
; RDI = mb_magic (arg1), RSI = mb_info_phys (arg2)  [System V AMD64 ABI]
section .text
bits 64
extern kernel_main
long_mode_start:
    ; Zero segment registers
    mov ax, 0
    mov ss, ax
    mov ds, ax
    mov es, ax
    mov fs, ax
    mov gs, ax

    ; EDI/ESI were set in _start; zero-extend to 64-bit for clean args
    mov edi, edi            ; zero-extend EAX (magic)   → RDI
    mov esi, esi            ; zero-extend EBX (mb_info) → RSI

    call kernel_main
    cli
.hang:
    hlt
    jmp .hang

1	TARGET   := nephilim.kernel
2	ISO      := nephilim.iso
3	ISODIR   := isodir
4	
5	CC       := clang
6	LD       := ld.lld
7	NASM     := nasm
8	PYTHON   := $(shell which python 2>/dev/null || which python3 2>/dev/null)
9	
10	INCLUDES := -Iinclude
11	
12	# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
13	CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
14	            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
15	            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
16	LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
17	            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000
18	
19	# Userland flags
20	UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
21	            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
22	
23	# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
24	JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
25	            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
26	            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
27	            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
28	            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
29	            -Wno-missing-field-initializers
30	
31	# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
33	            boot/font8x16.o \
34	            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \
35	            kernel/kernel.o kernel/vga.o kernel/detect.o \
36	            kernel/gdt.o kernel/idt.o kernel/syscall.o kernel/process.o \
37	            kernel/timer.o kernel/pmm.o kernel/vmm.o \
38	            kernel/js_runtime.o kernel/seed.o kernel/minit.o \

40	            lib/libtranslateunix.o \

43	            userland/shell/main.o \
44	            userland/snake/main.o \
45	            userland/apps.o
46	
47	# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
48	-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)
49	
50	.PHONY: all iso clean
51	
52	all: $(TARGET)
53

file.content:
TARGET   := nephilim.kernel
ISO      := nephilim.iso
ISODIR   := isodir

CC       := clang
LD       := ld.lld
NASM     := nasm
PYTHON   := $(shell which python 2>/dev/null || which python3 2>/dev/null)

INCLUDES := -Iinclude

# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000

# Userland flags
UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP

# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
            -Wno-missing-field-initializers

# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
            boot/font8x16.o \
            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \
            kernel/kernel.o kernel/vga.o kernel/detect.o \
            kernel/gdt.o kernel/idt.o kernel/syscall.o kernel/process.o \
            kernel/timer.o kernel/pmm.o kernel/vmm.o \
            kernel/js_runtime.o kernel/seed.o kernel/minit.o \
            fs/vfs.o fs/rawfs.o fs/ext2.o \
            lib/libtranslateunix.o \
            stdlib/string.o stdlib/stdlib.o stdlib/malloc.o \
            stdlib/math.o stdlib/printf.o stdlib/setjmp.o stdlib/time.o \
            userland/shell/main.o \
            userland/snake/main.o \
            userland/apps.o

# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)

.PHONY: all iso clean

all: $(TARGET)

# ── JS source → C string-literal header ──────────────────────────────────────
JS_HEADERS := \
    userland/signal.js.h \
    userland/calculator/main.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ls.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/touch.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mkdir.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/rm.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/stat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/write.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/wc.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/uname.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cd.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ps.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cp.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mv.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/grep.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/sed.js.h \
    userland/quill/main.js.h

userland/signal.js.h: userland/signal.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/calculator/main.js.h: userland/calculator/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/coreutils/%.js.h: userland/coreutils/%.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/quill/main.js.h: userland/quill/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

# ── Assembly ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
boot/boot.o: boot/boot.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/gdt_flush.o: boot/gdt_flush.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/isr_stubs.o: boot/isr_stubs.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/ctx_switch.o: boot/ctx_switch.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/font8x16.o: boot/font8x16.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

# ── Kernel C ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kernel/kernel.o: kernel/kernel.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vga.o: kernel/vga.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/detect.o: kernel/detect.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gdt.o: kernel/gdt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/idt.o: kernel/idt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/syscall.o: kernel/syscall.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/process.o: kernel/process.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/timer.o: kernel/timer.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/pmm.o: kernel/pmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vmm.o: kernel/vmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# MuJS runtime — compiled with relaxed flags, port header injected first
kernel/js_runtime.o: kernel/js_runtime.c userland/signal.js.h
	$(CC) $(JSCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/seed.o: kernel/seed.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/minit.o: kernel/minit.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Filesystem ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fs/vfs.o: fs/vfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/rawfs.o: fs/rawfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/ext2.o: fs/ext2.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Lib ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lib/libtranslateunix.o: lib/libtranslateunix.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Drivers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
drivers/keyboard.o: drivers/keyboard.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

drivers/serial.o: drivers/serial.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Stdlib ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
stdlib/string.o: stdlib/string.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/stdlib.o: stdlib/stdlib.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/malloc.o: stdlib/malloc.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/math.o: stdlib/math.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/printf.o: stdlib/printf.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/setjmp.o: stdlib/setjmp.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

stdlib/time.o: stdlib/time.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Userland ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
userland/shell/main.o: userland/shell/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/snake/main.o: userland/snake/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/apps.o: userland/apps.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c userland/apps.c -o $@

# ── Final kernel link ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
$(TARGET): $(KOBJS)
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $(KOBJS) -o $@

# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run (QEMU with serial on stdout) ─────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 128M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# ── Clean ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(ISO)
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.o' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.d' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -f $(JS_HEADERS)
	rm -rf $(ISODIR) external/include/mujs/*.o

content.text: Now I have a complete picture. Let me also check the kernel's `pmm.c` for the multiboot info parsing:

I now have everything I need. Here's the plan before I start writing:

**Multiboot 2 upgrade plan:**

1. **[boot/boot.asm](boot/boot.asm)** — Replace the MB1 header (`0x1BADB002`) with a proper Multiboot 2 header (`0xE85250D6`). The MB2 header requires a fixed-size tag terminator, a different magic (`0x36D76289` in EAX), and passes the info pointer in EBX. The page-table + long-mode setup stays identical.

2. **[grub/grub.cfg](grub/grub.cfg)** — Change `multiboot` → `multiboot2`.

3. **[linker.ld](linker.ld)** — Rename `.multiboot` section to `.mb2hdr` (keeps it first in the binary, linker-placed before `.text`).

4. **[include/pmm.h](include/pmm.h)** — Replace the MB1 info struct with the MB2 equivalents (fixed info header + tag stream).

5. **[kernel/pmm.c](kernel/pmm.c)** — Rewrite `pmm_init()` to walk MB2 tag stream.

6. **[kernel/kernel.c](kernel/kernel.c)** — Accept MB2 magic (`0x36D76289`), parse MB2 tags for memory info and (crucially) the **GOP framebuffer tag** instead of VGA text mode.

7. **[include/gop.h](include/gop.h)** + **[kernel/gop.c](kernel/gop.c)** — New GOP framebuffer driver using the linear framebuffer address from MB2 tag type 8.

8. **[kernel/vga.c](kernel/vga.c)** + **[include/vga.h](include/vga.h)** — Gut the VGA text-mode init but keep `kprint` working by routing it through the GOP framebuffer renderer using the existing `font8x16` data.

content.text: Starting with the boot entry point:

input.content:
; ─── Multiboot 2 header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
;
; Spec: https://www.gnu.org/software/grub/manual/multiboot2/multiboot2.html
;
; The header must appear within the first 32 KiB of the OS image and be 64-bit
; aligned.  Every field is 32-bit little-endian.
;
; Layout: [magic][architecture][header_length][checksum][end tag]
;
; magic        = 0xE85250D6
; architecture = 0  (i386 protected mode — GRUB still enters in 32-bit PM)
; header_length = total byte size of the header including all tags
; checksum      = -(magic + architecture + header_length) mod 2^32

MB2_MAGIC       equ 0xE85250D6
MB2_ARCH        equ 0               ; 32-bit (i386) protected mode
MB2_ENTRY_MAGIC equ 0x36D76289     ; GRUB puts this in EAX on entry

section .mb2hdr
align 8
mb2_header_start:
    dd MB2_MAGIC
    dd MB2_ARCH
    dd (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start)
    dd -(MB2_MAGIC + MB2_ARCH + (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start))

    ; ── Tag: Framebuffer (type 5) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    ; Ask GRUB / UEFI GOP for a linear framebuffer.
    ; width=0 height=0 depth=32 → firmware picks resolution, we want 32 bpp.
    align 8
    dw 5            ; type  = framebuffer request
    dw 1            ; flags = 1 (optional — fall back to text if unavailable)
    dd 20           ; size  = 20 bytes (8 header + 12 data)
    dd 0            ; width  (0 = no preference)
    dd 0            ; height (0 = no preference)
    dd 32           ; depth  (32 bpp)

    ; ── End tag (type 0) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    align 8
    dw 0            ; type  = end
    dw 0            ; flags
    dd 8            ; size  = 8 bytes
mb2_header_end:

; ─── Stacks + page tables (BSS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .rodata
gdt64:
    dq 0                                                        ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64
    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)          ; 64-bit code segment
.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry (GRUB lands here) ────────────────────────────────────────────
; On entry: EAX = 0x36D76289 (MB2 magic), EBX = physical address of MB2 info struct
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    ; Preserve multiboot registers across all 32-bit setup calls.
    ; EDI → RDI (arg1 = mb_magic), ESI → RSI (arg2 = mb2_info phys addr)
    mov edi, eax
    mov esi, ebx

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx
    je .no_cpuid
    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    ; P4[0] → P3
    mov eax, p3_table
    or  eax, 0b11             ; present + writable
    mov [p4_table], eax
    ; P3[0] → P2
    mov eax, p2_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    ; Fill P2 with 512 × 2 MiB huge pages
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000         ; 2 MiB
    mul ecx
    or  eax, 0b10000011       ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE + load P4 → CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or  eax, (1 << 5)         ; PAE
    or  eax, (1 << 9)         ; OSFXSR  — SSE
    or  eax, (1 << 10)        ; OSXMMEXCPT
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080       ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or  eax, 1 << 8           ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)        ; clear EM
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)        ; set MP
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)       ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

toolUseResult.content:
; ─── Multiboot 2 header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
;
; Spec: https://www.gnu.org/software/grub/manual/multiboot2/multiboot2.html
;
; The header must appear within the first 32 KiB of the OS image and be 64-bit
; aligned.  Every field is 32-bit little-endian.
;
; Layout: [magic][architecture][header_length][checksum][end tag]
;
; magic        = 0xE85250D6
; architecture = 0  (i386 protected mode — GRUB still enters in 32-bit PM)
; header_length = total byte size of the header including all tags
; checksum      = -(magic + architecture + header_length) mod 2^32

MB2_MAGIC       equ 0xE85250D6
MB2_ARCH        equ 0               ; 32-bit (i386) protected mode
MB2_ENTRY_MAGIC equ 0x36D76289     ; GRUB puts this in EAX on entry

section .mb2hdr
align 8
mb2_header_start:
    dd MB2_MAGIC
    dd MB2_ARCH
    dd (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start)
    dd -(MB2_MAGIC + MB2_ARCH + (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start))

    ; ── Tag: Framebuffer (type 5) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    ; Ask GRUB / UEFI GOP for a linear framebuffer.
    ; width=0 height=0 depth=32 → firmware picks resolution, we want 32 bpp.
    align 8
    dw 5            ; type  = framebuffer request
    dw 1            ; flags = 1 (optional — fall back to text if unavailable)
    dd 20           ; size  = 20 bytes (8 header + 12 data)
    dd 0            ; width  (0 = no preference)
    dd 0            ; height (0 = no preference)
    dd 32           ; depth  (32 bpp)

    ; ── End tag (type 0) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    align 8
    dw 0            ; type  = end
    dw 0            ; flags
    dd 8            ; size  = 8 bytes
mb2_header_end:

; ─── Stacks + page tables (BSS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .rodata
gdt64:
    dq 0                                                        ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64
    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)          ; 64-bit code segment
.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry (GRUB lands here) ────────────────────────────────────────────
; On entry: EAX = 0x36D76289 (MB2 magic), EBX = physical address of MB2 info struct
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    ; Preserve multiboot registers across all 32-bit setup calls.
    ; EDI → RDI (arg1 = mb_magic), ESI → RSI (arg2 = mb2_info phys addr)
    mov edi, eax
    mov esi, ebx

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx
    je .no_cpuid
    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    ; P4[0] → P3
    mov eax, p3_table
    or  eax, 0b11             ; present + writable
    mov [p4_table], eax
    ; P3[0] → P2
    mov eax, p2_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    ; Fill P2 with 512 × 2 MiB huge pages
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000         ; 2 MiB
    mul ecx
    or  eax, 0b10000011       ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE + load P4 → CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or  eax, (1 << 5)         ; PAE
    or  eax, (1 << 9)         ; OSFXSR  — SSE
    or  eax, (1 << 10)        ; OSXMMEXCPT
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080       ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or  eax, 1 << 8           ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)        ; clear EM
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)        ; set MP
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)       ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
; RDI = mb2_magic (arg1), RSI = mb2_info_phys (arg2)  [System V AMD64 ABI]
section .text
bits 64
extern kernel_main
long_mode_start:
    mov ax, 0
    mov ss, ax
    mov ds, ax
    mov es, ax
    mov fs, ax
    mov gs, ax

    ; Zero-extend the 32-bit values preserved in EDI/ESI
    mov edi, edi
    mov esi, esi

    call kernel_main
    cli
.hang:
    hlt
    jmp .hang

content.text: Good — keeping MB1 as a legacy fallback entry in grub.cfg. This means the boot.asm must still embed both headers. Let me update boot.asm to carry both, then update grub.cfg with two menu entries:

input.content:
; ─── Multiboot 2 header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
;
; Must appear within the first 32 KiB of the OS image, 64-bit aligned.
; On MB2 entry: EAX = 0x36D76289, EBX = physical address of MB2 info struct.
;
MB2_MAGIC       equ 0xE85250D6
MB2_ARCH        equ 0               ; i386 protected mode
MB2_ENTRY_MAGIC equ 0x36D76289

section .mb2hdr
align 8
mb2_header_start:
    dd MB2_MAGIC
    dd MB2_ARCH
    dd (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start)
    dd -(MB2_MAGIC + MB2_ARCH + (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start))

    ; ── Tag: Framebuffer request (type 5) ─────────────────────────────────────
    ; flags=1 → optional (fall back gracefully if firmware can't satisfy it).
    ; width=0 height=0 → no resolution preference; depth=32 → 32 bpp BGRA/BGRX.
    align 8
    dw 5            ; type  = framebuffer
    dw 1            ; flags = optional
    dd 20           ; size  = 20 bytes
    dd 0            ; preferred width  (0 = don't care)
    dd 0            ; preferred height (0 = don't care)
    dd 32           ; preferred depth  (32 bpp)

    ; ── End tag ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    align 8
    dw 0            ; type  = end
    dw 0            ; flags
    dd 8            ; size
mb2_header_end:

; ─── Multiboot 1 header (legacy fallback) ──────────────────────────────────────
;
; Placed immediately after the MB2 header so GRUB (MB1) can still find it within
; the first 8 KiB.  On MB1 entry: EAX = 0x2BADB002, EBX = MB1 info phys addr.
;
MB1_ALIGN     equ 1 << 0
MB1_MEMINFO   equ 1 << 1
MB1_FLAGS     equ MB1_ALIGN | MB1_MEMINFO
MB1_MAGIC     equ 0x1BADB002
MB1_CHECKSUM  equ -(MB1_MAGIC + MB1_FLAGS)

section .mb2hdr          ; same section — keeps both headers contiguous and early
align 4
    dd MB1_MAGIC
    dd MB1_FLAGS
    dd MB1_CHECKSUM

; ─── Stacks + page tables (BSS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

section .rodata

    dq 0                                                        ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64

.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry point — both MB1 and MB2 land here ──────────────────────────
;
; EAX = bootloader magic (0x2BADB002 or 0x36D76289)
; EBX = physical address of the respective info struct
;
; We preserve both registers verbatim into RDI/RSI which become the first two
; System V AMD64 ABI arguments to kernel_main(uint32_t magic, uint32_t info).
;
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    mov edi, eax            ; arg1 = magic
    mov esi, ebx            ; arg2 = info struct phys addr

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx

    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    mov eax, p3_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p4_table], eax
    mov eax, p2_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000
    mul ecx
    or  eax, 0b10000011     ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE, load CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or  eax, (1 << 5)       ; PAE
    or  eax, (1 << 9)       ; OSFXSR  (SSE)
    or  eax, (1 << 10)      ; OSXMMEXCPT
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080     ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or  eax, 1 << 8         ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)      ; clear EM
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)      ; set MP
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)     ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
; RDI = magic, RSI = info_phys  (System V AMD64 ABI arg1/arg2)
section .text
bits 64
extern kernel_main
long_mode_start:
    mov ax, 0
    mov ss, ax
    mov ds, ax
    mov es, ax
    mov fs, ax
    mov gs, ax

    mov edi, edi            ; zero-extend to 64 bits
    mov esi, esi

    call kernel_main
    cli

    jmp .hang

; ─── Multiboot 2 header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
;
; Must appear within the first 32 KiB of the OS image, 64-bit aligned.
; On MB2 entry: EAX = 0x36D76289, EBX = physical address of MB2 info struct.
;
MB2_MAGIC       equ 0xE85250D6
MB2_ARCH        equ 0               ; i386 protected mode
MB2_ENTRY_MAGIC equ 0x36D76289

section .mb2hdr
align 8
mb2_header_start:
    dd MB2_MAGIC
    dd MB2_ARCH
    dd (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start)
    dd -(MB2_MAGIC + MB2_ARCH + (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start))

    ; ── Tag: Framebuffer request (type 5) ─────────────────────────────────────
    ; flags=1 → optional (fall back gracefully if firmware can't satisfy it).
    ; width=0 height=0 → no resolution preference; depth=32 → 32 bpp BGRA/BGRX.
    align 8
    dw 5            ; type  = framebuffer
    dw 1            ; flags = optional
    dd 20           ; size  = 20 bytes
    dd 0            ; preferred width  (0 = don't care)
    dd 0            ; preferred height (0 = don't care)
    dd 32           ; preferred depth  (32 bpp)

    ; ── End tag ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    align 8
    dw 0            ; type  = end
    dw 0            ; flags
    dd 8            ; size
mb2_header_end:

; ─── Multiboot 1 header (legacy fallback) ──────────────────────────────────────
;
; Placed immediately after the MB2 header so GRUB (MB1) can still find it within
; the first 8 KiB.  On MB1 entry: EAX = 0x2BADB002, EBX = MB1 info phys addr.
;
MB1_ALIGN     equ 1 << 0
MB1_MEMINFO   equ 1 << 1
MB1_FLAGS     equ MB1_ALIGN | MB1_MEMINFO
MB1_MAGIC     equ 0x1BADB002
MB1_CHECKSUM  equ -(MB1_MAGIC + MB1_FLAGS)

section .mb2hdr          ; same section — keeps both headers contiguous and early
align 4
    dd MB1_MAGIC
    dd MB1_FLAGS
    dd MB1_CHECKSUM

; ─── Stacks + page tables (BSS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .rodata
gdt64:
    dq 0                                                        ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64
    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)          ; 64-bit code segment
.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry point — both MB1 and MB2 land here ──────────────────────────
;
; EAX = bootloader magic (0x2BADB002 or 0x36D76289)
; EBX = physical address of the respective info struct
;
; We preserve both registers verbatim into RDI/RSI which become the first two
; System V AMD64 ABI arguments to kernel_main(uint32_t magic, uint32_t info).
;
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    mov edi, eax            ; arg1 = magic
    mov esi, ebx            ; arg2 = info struct phys addr

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx
    je .no_cpuid
    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    mov eax, p3_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p4_table], eax
    mov eax, p2_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000
    mul ecx
    or  eax, 0b10000011     ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE, load CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or  eax, (1 << 5)       ; PAE
    or  eax, (1 << 9)       ; OSFXSR  (SSE)
    or  eax, (1 << 10)      ; OSXMMEXCPT
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080     ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or  eax, 1 << 8         ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)      ; clear EM
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)      ; set MP
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)     ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
; RDI = magic, RSI = info_phys  (System V AMD64 ABI arg1/arg2)
section .text
bits 64
extern kernel_main
long_mode_start:
    mov ax, 0
    mov ss, ax
    mov ds, ax
    mov es, ax
    mov fs, ax
    mov gs, ax

    mov edi, edi            ; zero-extend to 64 bits
    mov esi, esi

    call kernel_main
    cli

    hlt
    jmp .hang

; ─── Multiboot 2 header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
;
; Spec: https://www.gnu.org/software/grub/manual/multiboot2/multiboot2.html
;
; The header must appear within the first 32 KiB of the OS image and be 64-bit
; aligned.  Every field is 32-bit little-endian.
;
; Layout: [magic][architecture][header_length][checksum][end tag]
;
; magic        = 0xE85250D6
; architecture = 0  (i386 protected mode — GRUB still enters in 32-bit PM)
; header_length = total byte size of the header including all tags
; checksum      = -(magic + architecture + header_length) mod 2^32

MB2_MAGIC       equ 0xE85250D6
MB2_ARCH        equ 0               ; 32-bit (i386) protected mode
MB2_ENTRY_MAGIC equ 0x36D76289     ; GRUB puts this in EAX on entry

section .mb2hdr
align 8
mb2_header_start:
    dd MB2_MAGIC
    dd MB2_ARCH
    dd (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start)
    dd -(MB2_MAGIC + MB2_ARCH + (mb2_header_end - mb2_header_start))

    ; ── Tag: Framebuffer (type 5) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    ; Ask GRUB / UEFI GOP for a linear framebuffer.
    ; width=0 height=0 depth=32 → firmware picks resolution, we want 32 bpp.
    align 8
    dw 5            ; type  = framebuffer request
    dw 1            ; flags = 1 (optional — fall back to text if unavailable)
    dd 20           ; size  = 20 bytes (8 header + 12 data)
    dd 0            ; width  (0 = no preference)
    dd 0            ; height (0 = no preference)
    dd 32           ; depth  (32 bpp)

    ; ── End tag (type 0) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    align 8
    dw 0            ; type  = end
    dw 0            ; flags
    dd 8            ; size  = 8 bytes
mb2_header_end:

; ─── Stacks + page tables (BSS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .bss
align 16
stack_bottom:
    resb 16384          ; 16 KiB stack
stack_top:

align 4096
p4_table:   resb 4096   ; PML4
p3_table:   resb 4096   ; PDPT
p2_table:   resb 4096   ; PD  (2 MiB pages)

; ─── GDT for long mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
section .rodata
gdt64:
    dq 0                                                        ; null descriptor
.code: equ $ - gdt64
    dq (1 << 43) | (1 << 44) | (1 << 47) | (1 << 53)          ; 64-bit code segment
.pointer:
    dw $ - gdt64 - 1
    dq gdt64

; ─── 32-bit entry (GRUB lands here) ────────────────────────────────────────────
; On entry: EAX = 0x36D76289 (MB2 magic), EBX = physical address of MB2 info struct
section .text
bits 32
global _start
_start:
    mov esp, stack_top
    ; Preserve multiboot registers across all 32-bit setup calls.
    ; EDI → RDI (arg1 = mb_magic), ESI → RSI (arg2 = mb2_info phys addr)
    mov edi, eax
    mov esi, ebx

    call check_cpuid
    call check_long_mode
    call setup_page_tables
    call enable_paging

    lgdt [gdt64.pointer]
    jmp gdt64.code:long_mode_start

; ── CPUID availability check ──
check_cpuid:
    pushfd
    pop eax
    mov ecx, eax
    xor eax, 1 << 21
    push eax
    popfd
    pushfd
    pop eax
    push ecx
    popfd
    cmp eax, ecx
    je .no_cpuid
    ret
.no_cpuid:
    hlt

; ── Long mode availability check ──
check_long_mode:
    mov eax, 0x80000000
    cpuid
    cmp eax, 0x80000001
    jb .no_long_mode
    mov eax, 0x80000001
    cpuid
    test edx, 1 << 29
    jz .no_long_mode
    ret
.no_long_mode:
    hlt

; ── Identity-map first 1 GiB with 2 MiB pages ──
setup_page_tables:
    ; P4[0] → P3
    mov eax, p3_table
    or  eax, 0b11             ; present + writable
    mov [p4_table], eax
    ; P3[0] → P2
    mov eax, p2_table
    or  eax, 0b11
    mov [p3_table], eax
    ; Fill P2 with 512 × 2 MiB huge pages
    mov ecx, 0
.map_p2:
    mov eax, 0x200000         ; 2 MiB
    mul ecx
    or  eax, 0b10000011       ; present + writable + huge
    mov [p2_table + ecx * 8], eax
    inc ecx
    cmp ecx, 512
    jne .map_p2
    ret

; ── Enable PAE + load P4 → CR3, set EFER.LME, enable paging ──
enable_paging:
    mov eax, p4_table
    mov cr3, eax
    mov eax, cr4
    or  eax, (1 << 5)         ; PAE
    or  eax, (1 << 9)         ; OSFXSR  — SSE
    or  eax, (1 << 10)        ; OSXMMEXCPT
    mov cr4, eax
    mov ecx, 0xC0000080       ; EFER MSR
    rdmsr
    or  eax, 1 << 8           ; LME
    wrmsr
    mov eax, cr0
    and eax, ~(1 << 2)        ; clear EM
    or  eax,  (1 << 1)        ; set MP
    or  eax,  (1 << 31)       ; PG
    mov cr0, eax
    ret

; ─── 64-bit long mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
; RDI = mb2_magic (arg1), RSI = mb2_info_phys (arg2)  [System V AMD64 ABI]
section .text
bits 64
extern kernel_main
long_mode_start:
    mov ax, 0
    mov ss, ax
    mov ds, ax
    mov es, ax
    mov fs, ax
    mov gs, ax

    ; Zero-extend the 32-bit values preserved in EDI/ESI
    mov edi, edi
    mov esi, esi

    call kernel_main
    cli
.hang:
    hlt
    jmp .hang

toolUseResult.originalFile:
TARGET   := nephilim.kernel
ISO      := nephilim.iso
ISODIR   := isodir

CC       := clang
LD       := ld.lld
NASM     := nasm
PYTHON   := $(shell which python 2>/dev/null || which python3 2>/dev/null)

INCLUDES := -Iinclude

# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000

# Userland flags
UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP

# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
            -Wno-missing-field-initializers

# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
            boot/font8x16.o \
            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \
            kernel/kernel.o kernel/vga.o kernel/detect.o \
            kernel/gdt.o kernel/idt.o kernel/syscall.o kernel/process.o \
            kernel/timer.o kernel/pmm.o kernel/vmm.o \
            kernel/js_runtime.o kernel/seed.o kernel/minit.o \
            fs/vfs.o fs/rawfs.o fs/ext2.o \
            lib/libtranslateunix.o \
            stdlib/string.o stdlib/stdlib.o stdlib/malloc.o \
            stdlib/math.o stdlib/printf.o stdlib/setjmp.o stdlib/time.o \
            userland/shell/main.o \
            userland/snake/main.o \
            userland/apps.o

# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)

.PHONY: all iso clean

all: $(TARGET)

# ── JS source → C string-literal header ──────────────────────────────────────
JS_HEADERS := \
    userland/signal.js.h \
    userland/calculator/main.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ls.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/touch.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mkdir.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/rm.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/stat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/write.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/wc.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/uname.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cd.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ps.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cp.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mv.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/grep.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/sed.js.h \
    userland/quill/main.js.h

userland/signal.js.h: userland/signal.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/calculator/main.js.h: userland/calculator/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/coreutils/%.js.h: userland/coreutils/%.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/quill/main.js.h: userland/quill/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

# ── Assembly ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
boot/boot.o: boot/boot.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/gdt_flush.o: boot/gdt_flush.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/isr_stubs.o: boot/isr_stubs.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/ctx_switch.o: boot/ctx_switch.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/font8x16.o: boot/font8x16.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

# ── Kernel C ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kernel/kernel.o: kernel/kernel.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vga.o: kernel/vga.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/detect.o: kernel/detect.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gdt.o: kernel/gdt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/idt.o: kernel/idt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/syscall.o: kernel/syscall.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/process.o: kernel/process.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/timer.o: kernel/timer.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/pmm.o: kernel/pmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vmm.o: kernel/vmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# MuJS runtime — compiled with relaxed flags, port header injected first

lastPrompt: upgrade my code to multiboot 2 so I can use UEFI and in turn GOP for Graphics

aiTitle: Upgrade code to Multiboot 2 for UEFI and GOP graphics

content.text: Now update the `iso` and `run` targets to support UEFI. `grub-mkrescue` needs `--modules` to include the `multiboot2` and `efi_gop` modules, and QEMU needs `-bios` or `-machine q35` with OVMF for UEFI testing:

input.old_string:
# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run (QEMU with serial on stdout) ─────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 128M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown
input.new_string:
# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

# grub-mkrescue flags:
#   --modules includes both multiboot (MB1) and multiboot2 (MB2/UEFI) modules
#   plus efi_gop so GRUB can negotiate a GOP framebuffer on UEFI firmware.
GRUB_MODULES := multiboot multiboot2 normal iso9660 gfxterm efi_gop efi_uga

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) --modules="$(GRUB_MODULES)" -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU    := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
# OVMF firmware for UEFI testing — set OVMF_FW to your OVMF.fd path if available
# e.g.:  make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd
OVMF_FW ?= /usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — uses OVMF; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -bios $(OVMF_FW) -vga none -device virtio-gpu-pci

	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/printf.o: stdlib/printf.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/setjmp.o: stdlib/setjmp.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

stdlib/time.o: stdlib/time.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Userland ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
userland/shell/main.o: userland/shell/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/snake/main.o: userland/snake/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/apps.o: userland/apps.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c userland/apps.c -o $@

# ── Final kernel link ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
$(TARGET): $(KOBJS)
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $(KOBJS) -o $@

# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run (QEMU with serial on stdout) ─────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 128M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# ── Clean ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(ISO)
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.o' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.d' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -f $(JS_HEADERS)
	rm -rf $(ISODIR) external/include/mujs/*.o

content.text:
All references are in the files we already updated. Everything is consistent. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

**What changed:**

**[boot/boot.asm](boot/boot.asm)** — Carries **both** headers in `.mb2hdr`:
1. MB2 header (`0xE85250D6`) with a framebuffer tag (type 5, 32 bpp, optional) and end tag
2. MB1 header (`0x1BADB002`) immediately after as the legacy fallback

Both entry paths converge at the same `_start` — EAX/EBX are passed through to `kernel_main` unchanged.

**[linker.ld](linker.ld)** — `.multiboot` → `.mb2hdr` to match the new section name.

**[grub/grub.cfg](grub/grub.cfg)** — Two menu entries: `multiboot2` (primary, UEFI/GOP) and `multiboot` (legacy BIOS).

**[include/gop.h](include/gop.h)** — New file. Defines `gop_info_t`, `gop_mb2_tag_t`, `gop_available`, and the drawing API (`gop_put_pixel`, `gop_fill_rect`, `gop_clear`, `gop_draw_glyph`).

**[kernel/gop.c](kernel/gop.c)** — New file. Implements `gop_init_from_mb2_tag()` and all drawing primitives. Completely separate from VGA.

**[include/pmm.h](include/pmm.h)** — Replaced MB1-only structs with MB2 tag structs + MB1 structs. `pmm_init()` split into `pmm_init_mb2()` and `pmm_init_mb1()`.

**[kernel/pmm.c](kernel/pmm.c)** — Both init paths walk their respective memory maps; shared `pmm_common_finalize()` reserves the kernel + first MiB.

**[include/vga.h](include/vga.h)** — Added `vga_init_gop()` declaration, added `<stdbool.h>`.

**[kernel/vga.c](kernel/vga.c)** — `use_gop_path` flag selects at runtime between I/O-port VGA text mode and GOP pixel rendering. `kprint`/scroll/clear all branch on this flag. The 16-colour VGA palette is replicated as RGB triplets for GOP. No I/O ports are touched on the GOP path.

**[kernel/kernel.c](kernel/kernel.c)** — Three-tier boot:
1. MB2 + GOP framebuffer tag → `vga_init_gop()` (pixel rendering)
2. MB2 without framebuffer tag → `vga_init()` (VGA text)
3. MB1 → `pmm_init_mb1()` + `vga_init()` (VGA text)

**[Makefile](Makefile)** — Added `kernel/gop.o`, `--modules` flag for `grub-mkrescue` (includes `multiboot2`, `efi_gop`), `make run-uefi` target for OVMF testing.

**To test UEFI locally in WSL:**
```bash
make iso
# BIOS (MB1 fallback):
make run
# UEFI + GOP (requires OVMF):
make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/path/to/OVMF.fd
```

TARGET   := nephilim.kernel

INCLUDES := -Iinclude

# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000

# Userland flags
UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP

# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
            -Wno-missing-field-initializers

# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
            boot/font8x16.o \
            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \

# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)

	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

drivers/serial.o: drivers/serial.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Stdlib ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
stdlib/string.o: stdlib/string.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/stdlib.o: stdlib/stdlib.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/malloc.o: stdlib/malloc.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/math.o: stdlib/math.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/printf.o: stdlib/printf.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/setjmp.o: stdlib/setjmp.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

stdlib/time.o: stdlib/time.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Userland ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
userland/shell/main.o: userland/shell/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/snake/main.o: userland/snake/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/apps.o: userland/apps.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c userland/apps.c -o $@

# ── Final kernel link ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
$(TARGET): $(KOBJS)
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $(KOBJS) -o $@

# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

# grub-mkrescue flags:
#   --modules includes both multiboot (MB1) and multiboot2 (MB2/UEFI) modules
#   plus efi_gop so GRUB can negotiate a GOP framebuffer on UEFI firmware.
GRUB_MODULES := multiboot multiboot2 normal iso9660 gfxterm efi_gop efi_uga

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) --modules="$(GRUB_MODULES)" -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU    := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
# OVMF firmware for UEFI testing — set OVMF_FW to your OVMF.fd path if available
# e.g.:  make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd
OVMF_FW ?= /usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — uses OVMF; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -bios $(OVMF_FW) -vga none -device virtio-gpu-pci

# ── Clean ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(ISO)
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.o' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.d' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -f $(JS_HEADERS)
	rm -rf $(ISODIR) external/include/mujs/*.o

content.text:
`efi_gop` and `efi_uga` are EFI-platform modules that `grub-mkrescue` bundles automatically when it builds the EFI image — you don't need to request them explicitly. `grub-mkrescue` already handles this by generating both a BIOS `core.img` (i386-pc) and a UEFI `bootx64.efi` (x86_64-efi) in the ISO's EFI System Partition. Manually listing EFI modules in `--modules` only affects the BIOS image, which doesn't have them.

`make iso` should now work cleanly.

content.text:
The sandbox can't reach your WSL environment. Run these yourself in the terminal:

**Step 1 — find OVMF if already present:**
```bash
find /usr -name "OVMF*.fd" 2>/dev/null
find /usr -name "ovmf" -type d 2>/dev/null
```

**Step 2 — install it if missing (openSUSE):**
```bash
sudo zypper install ovmf
```
The firmware file will be at one of:
- `/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd`
- `/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64.bin`
- `/usr/share/edk2/ovmf/OVMF.fd`

**Step 3 — run with whichever path you find:**
```bash
make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd
```

---

**Alternative — no OVMF needed:** if you just want to verify the MB2 + GOP path works, QEMU's built-in SeaBIOS will pick the `multiboot2` menu entry too (it's still BIOS but MB2 protocol). GOP won't activate because there's no UEFI firmware negotiating it — the framebuffer tag will be absent and the kernel will fall back to VGA text mode. For true UEFI + GOP you need OVMF.

It'll print something like `/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64.bin` or `/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd`. Then:

```bash
make run-uefi OVMF_FW=<that path>
```

```bash
FW=$(rpm -ql ovmf | grep "\.fd$\|\.bin$" | head -1) && make run-uefi OVMF_FW=$FW
```

content.text:
Sandbox can't reach WSL. Run in your terminal:

```bash
rpm -ql ovmf
```

On openSUSE the actual firmware is usually in a separate package called `qemu-ovmf-x86_64`. Install and locate it:

```bash
sudo zypper install qemu-ovmf-x86_64
find /usr/share/qemu -name "*.fd" 2>/dev/null
```

The file will likely be `/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-code.bin` + `/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-vars.bin`. OVMF on modern distros is split into a read-only code image and a writable vars image. The `-bios` flag only accepts the monolithic single-file form — for split images you need `-drive` instead.

Once you find the files, update the `run-uefi` target in the Makefile:

input.old_string:
# OVMF firmware for UEFI testing — set OVMF_FW to your OVMF.fd path if available
# e.g.:  make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd
OVMF_FW ?= /usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — uses OVMF; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -bios $(OVMF_FW) -vga none -device virtio-gpu-pci
input.new_string:
# OVMF firmware paths — openSUSE ships split code+vars images in qemu-ovmf-x86_64.
# Override on the command line if your distro puts them elsewhere:
#   make run-uefi OVMF_CODE=/path/to/code.fd OVMF_VARS=/path/to/vars.fd
OVMF_CODE ?= $(firstword \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-code.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/edk2/ovmf/OVMF_CODE.fd) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/OVMF/OVMF_CODE.fd))
OVMF_VARS ?= $(firstword \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-vars.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/edk2/ovmf/OVMF_VARS.fd) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/OVMF/OVMF_VARS.fd))

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — split OVMF code+vars images; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -drive if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on,file=$(OVMF_CODE) \
	        -drive if=pflash,format=raw,file=$(OVMF_VARS)

TARGET   := nephilim.kernel
ISO      := nephilim.iso
ISODIR   := isodir

CC       := clang
LD       := ld.lld
NASM     := nasm
PYTHON   := $(shell which python 2>/dev/null || which python3 2>/dev/null)

INCLUDES := -Iinclude

# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000

# Userland flags
UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP

# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
            -Wno-missing-field-initializers

# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
            boot/font8x16.o \
            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \
            kernel/kernel.o kernel/vga.o kernel/gop.o kernel/detect.o \
            kernel/gdt.o kernel/idt.o kernel/syscall.o kernel/process.o \
            kernel/timer.o kernel/pmm.o kernel/vmm.o \
            kernel/js_runtime.o kernel/seed.o kernel/minit.o \
            fs/vfs.o fs/rawfs.o fs/ext2.o \
            lib/libtranslateunix.o \
            stdlib/string.o stdlib/stdlib.o stdlib/malloc.o \
            stdlib/math.o stdlib/printf.o stdlib/setjmp.o stdlib/time.o \
            userland/shell/main.o \
            userland/snake/main.o \
            userland/apps.o

# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)

.PHONY: all iso clean

all: $(TARGET)

# ── JS source → C string-literal header ──────────────────────────────────────
JS_HEADERS := \
    userland/signal.js.h \
    userland/calculator/main.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ls.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/touch.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mkdir.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/rm.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/stat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/write.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/wc.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/uname.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cd.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ps.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cp.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mv.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/grep.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/sed.js.h \
    userland/quill/main.js.h

userland/signal.js.h: userland/signal.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/calculator/main.js.h: userland/calculator/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/coreutils/%.js.h: userland/coreutils/%.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/quill/main.js.h: userland/quill/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

# ── Assembly ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
boot/boot.o: boot/boot.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/gdt_flush.o: boot/gdt_flush.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/isr_stubs.o: boot/isr_stubs.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/ctx_switch.o: boot/ctx_switch.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/font8x16.o: boot/font8x16.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

# ── Kernel C ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kernel/kernel.o: kernel/kernel.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vga.o: kernel/vga.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gop.o: kernel/gop.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/detect.o: kernel/detect.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gdt.o: kernel/gdt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/idt.o: kernel/idt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/syscall.o: kernel/syscall.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/process.o: kernel/process.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/timer.o: kernel/timer.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/pmm.o: kernel/pmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vmm.o: kernel/vmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# MuJS runtime — compiled with relaxed flags, port header injected first
kernel/js_runtime.o: kernel/js_runtime.c userland/signal.js.h
	$(CC) $(JSCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/seed.o: kernel/seed.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/minit.o: kernel/minit.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Filesystem ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fs/vfs.o: fs/vfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/rawfs.o: fs/rawfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/ext2.o: fs/ext2.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Lib ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lib/libtranslateunix.o: lib/libtranslateunix.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Drivers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
drivers/keyboard.o: drivers/keyboard.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

drivers/serial.o: drivers/serial.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Stdlib ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
stdlib/string.o: stdlib/string.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/stdlib.o: stdlib/stdlib.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/malloc.o: stdlib/malloc.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/math.o: stdlib/math.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/printf.o: stdlib/printf.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/setjmp.o: stdlib/setjmp.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

stdlib/time.o: stdlib/time.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Userland ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
userland/shell/main.o: userland/shell/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/snake/main.o: userland/snake/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/apps.o: userland/apps.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c userland/apps.c -o $@

# ── Final kernel link ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
$(TARGET): $(KOBJS)
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $(KOBJS) -o $@

# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

# grub-mkrescue flags:
#   --modules includes both multiboot (MB1) and multiboot2 (MB2/UEFI) modules
#   plus efi_gop so GRUB can negotiate a GOP framebuffer on UEFI firmware.
GRUB_MODULES := multiboot multiboot2 normal iso9660 gfxterm

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) --modules="$(GRUB_MODULES)" -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU    := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
# OVMF firmware for UEFI testing — set OVMF_FW to your OVMF.fd path if available
# e.g.:  make run-uefi OVMF_FW=/usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd
OVMF_FW ?= /usr/share/ovmf/OVMF.fd

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — uses OVMF; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -bios $(OVMF_FW) -vga none -device virtio-gpu-pci

# ── Clean ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(ISO)
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.o' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.d' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -f $(JS_HEADERS)
	rm -rf $(ISODIR) external/include/mujs/*.o

lines: -# OVMF firmware for UEFI testing — set OVMF_FW to your OVMF.fd path if available
+# OVMF firmware paths — openSUSE ships split code+vars images in qemu-ovmf-x86_64.
+# Override on the command line if your distro puts them elsewhere:
# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
-# UEFI run — uses OVMF; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer
+# UEFI run — split OVMF code+vars images; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer

content.text:
Now in your terminal:

```bash
sudo zypper install qemu-ovmf-x86_64
find /usr/share/qemu -name "ovmf*" 2>/dev/null
```

Then just:
```bash
make run-uefi
```

The Makefile will auto-detect the paths via `wildcard`. If the files land somewhere unexpected, override with:
```bash
make run-uefi OVMF_CODE=/actual/path/code.bin OVMF_VARS=/actual/path/vars.bin
```
