TARGET   := nephilim.kernel
ISO      := nephilim.iso
ISODIR   := isodir

CC       := clang
LD       := ld.lld
NASM     := nasm
PYTHON   := $(shell which python 2>/dev/null || which python3 2>/dev/null)

INCLUDES := -Iinclude

# Kernel flags: target is x86-64 — SSE/AVX available and enabled
CFLAGS   := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP
LDFLAGS  := -m elf_x86_64 -nostdlib -T linker.ld \
            --no-dynamic-linker -z max-page-size=0x1000

# Userland flags
UCFLAGS  := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 -Wall -Wextra \
            $(INCLUDES) -MMD -MP

# MuJS flags — relax warnings for third-party code
JSCFLAGS := --target=x86_64-elf -std=gnu99 -ffreestanding -O2 \
            -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel \
            $(INCLUDES) -Iexternal/include/mujs \
            -Wno-unused-function -Wno-unused-variable \
            -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-implicit-fallthrough \
            -Wno-missing-field-initializers

# ── Kernel objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KOBJS    := boot/boot.o boot/gdt_flush.o boot/isr_stubs.o boot/ctx_switch.o \
            boot/font8x16.o \
            drivers/keyboard.o drivers/serial.o \
            kernel/kernel.o kernel/vga.o kernel/gop.o kernel/detect.o \
            kernel/gdt.o kernel/idt.o kernel/syscall.o kernel/process.o \
            kernel/timer.o kernel/pmm.o kernel/vmm.o \
            kernel/js_runtime.o kernel/seed.o kernel/minit.o \
            fs/vfs.o fs/rawfs.o fs/ext2.o \
            lib/libtranslateunix.o \
            stdlib/string.o stdlib/stdlib.o stdlib/malloc.o \
            stdlib/math.o stdlib/printf.o stdlib/setjmp.o stdlib/time.o \
            userland/shell/main.o \
            userland/snake/main.o \
            userland/apps.o

# Auto-generated dependency files (produced by -MMD -MP)
-include $(KOBJS:.o=.d)

.PHONY: all iso clean

all: $(TARGET)

# ── JS source → C string-literal header ──────────────────────────────────────
JS_HEADERS := \
    userland/signal.js.h \
    userland/calculator/main.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ls.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/touch.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mkdir.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/rm.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/stat.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/write.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/wc.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/uname.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cd.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/ps.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/cp.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/mv.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/grep.js.h \
    userland/coreutils/sed.js.h \
    userland/quill/main.js.h

userland/signal.js.h: userland/signal.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/calculator/main.js.h: userland/calculator/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/coreutils/%.js.h: userland/coreutils/%.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

userland/quill/main.js.h: userland/quill/main.js
	$(PYTHON) tools/js2ch.py $< > $@

# ── Assembly ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
boot/boot.o: boot/boot.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/gdt_flush.o: boot/gdt_flush.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/isr_stubs.o: boot/isr_stubs.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/ctx_switch.o: boot/ctx_switch.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

boot/font8x16.o: boot/font8x16.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

# ── Kernel C ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kernel/kernel.o: kernel/kernel.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vga.o: kernel/vga.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gop.o: kernel/gop.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/detect.o: kernel/detect.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/gdt.o: kernel/gdt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/idt.o: kernel/idt.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/syscall.o: kernel/syscall.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/process.o: kernel/process.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/timer.o: kernel/timer.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/pmm.o: kernel/pmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/vmm.o: kernel/vmm.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# MuJS runtime — compiled with relaxed flags, port header injected first
kernel/js_runtime.o: kernel/js_runtime.c userland/signal.js.h
	$(CC) $(JSCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/seed.o: kernel/seed.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

kernel/minit.o: kernel/minit.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Filesystem ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fs/vfs.o: fs/vfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/rawfs.o: fs/rawfs.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

fs/ext2.o: fs/ext2.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Lib ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lib/libtranslateunix.o: lib/libtranslateunix.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Drivers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
drivers/keyboard.o: drivers/keyboard.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

drivers/serial.o: drivers/serial.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Stdlib ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
stdlib/string.o: stdlib/string.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/stdlib.o: stdlib/stdlib.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/malloc.o: stdlib/malloc.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/math.o: stdlib/math.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/printf.o: stdlib/printf.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

stdlib/setjmp.o: stdlib/setjmp.asm
	$(NASM) -f elf64 $< -o $@

stdlib/time.o: stdlib/time.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# ── Userland ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
userland/shell/main.o: userland/shell/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/snake/main.o: userland/snake/main.c
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

userland/apps.o: userland/apps.c $(JS_HEADERS)
	$(CC) $(UCFLAGS) -c userland/apps.c -o $@

# ── Final kernel link ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
$(TARGET): $(KOBJS)
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $(KOBJS) -o $@

# ── ISO ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRUB_MKRESCUE := $(shell which grub2-mkrescue 2>/dev/null || which grub-mkrescue 2>/dev/null)

# grub-mkrescue flags:
#   --modules includes both multiboot (MB1) and multiboot2 (MB2/UEFI) modules
#   plus efi_gop so GRUB can negotiate a GOP framebuffer on UEFI firmware.
GRUB_MODULES := multiboot multiboot2 normal iso9660 all_video

iso: $(TARGET)
	mkdir -p $(ISODIR)/boot/grub
	cp $(TARGET) $(ISODIR)/boot/$(TARGET)
	cp grub/grub.cfg $(ISODIR)/boot/grub/grub.cfg
	$(GRUB_MKRESCUE) --modules="$(GRUB_MODULES)" -o $(ISO) $(ISODIR)

# ── Run ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QEMU    := $(shell which qemu-system-x86_64 2>/dev/null)
# OVMF firmware paths — openSUSE ships split code+vars images in qemu-ovmf-x86_64.
# Override on the command line if your distro puts them elsewhere:
#   make run-uefi OVMF_CODE=/path/to/code.fd OVMF_VARS=/path/to/vars.fd
OVMF_CODE ?= $(firstword \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-4m-code.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-code.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/edk2/ovmf/OVMF_CODE.fd) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/OVMF/OVMF_CODE.fd))
OVMF_VARS ?= $(firstword \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-4m-vars.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-vars.bin) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/edk2/ovmf/OVMF_VARS.fd) \
    $(wildcard /usr/share/OVMF/OVMF_VARS.fd))

# BIOS legacy run (Multiboot 1 fallback menu entry)
run: $(ISO)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown

# UEFI run — split OVMF code+vars images; boots via Multiboot 2 + GOP framebuffer.
# Vars are copied to a temp file so QEMU never writes back to the system image.
OVMF_VARS_TMP := /tmp/nephilim-ovmf-vars.bin
run-uefi: $(ISO)
	cp $(OVMF_VARS) $(OVMF_VARS_TMP)
	$(QEMU) -cdrom $(ISO) -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
	        -drive if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on,file=$(OVMF_CODE) \
	        -drive if=pflash,format=raw,file=$(OVMF_VARS_TMP)

# ── Clean ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(ISO)
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.o' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find boot kernel stdlib userland drivers fs lib -name '*.d' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -f $(JS_HEADERS)
	rm -rf $(ISODIR) external/include/mujs/*.o

1	# NephilimOS
2	
3	A MINIX-inspired, bare-metal x86_64 operating system built from scratch — with a JavaScript userland and a C snake game.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## What It Is
8	
9	NephilimOS is a 64-bit hobby kernel that boots via GRUB Multiboot 1, runs a cooperative scheduler, mounts an ext2 filesystem from a RAM disk, and executes user-facing apps written in JavaScript via an embedded MuJS interpreter. All userland runs at Ring 1 through an `int $0x80` syscall interface. A native C snake game runs directly as a Ring-1 process with direct VGA access.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Feature Summary
14	
15	### Boot & Hardware
16	- Multiboot 1 compliant — boots via GRUB on real x86_64 or QEMU
17	- 32-bit protected mode → 64-bit long mode transition in assembly
18	- Identity-mapped first 1 GiB via 2 MiB huge pages (PML4 → PDPT → PD)
19	- VGA text-mode driver (80×25, colour, custom 8×16 bitmap font)
20	- PS/2 keyboard driver — scancode set 1, shift/ctrl modifiers, extended keys (arrows, F-keys)
21	- PIT timer at 100 Hz (IRQ0) — monotonic tick + second counter
22	- 8259 PIC remapped to vectors 32–47
23	
24	### Kernel Subsystems
25	- GDT with Ring 0 / Ring 1 descriptors and a 64-bit TSS
26	- IDT with 256 gates; CPU exceptions print vector + RIP and halt
27	- `int 0x80` syscall interface (Ring 0 and Ring 1 callable)
28	- Cooperative multi-process scheduler (FCFS + SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties)
29	- `proc_create`, `proc_fork`, `proc_destroy`, `proc_switch` process lifecycle
30	- **minit** — miniature init: lightweight process supervisor that restarts watched processes up to 3 times on death
31	
32	### Memory
33	- First-fit free-list heap allocator (`malloc` / `free` / `realloc`) with block coalescing
34	- 64 MB heap in `.bss` (NOBITS — not stored in the ELF binary)
35	- PMM physical frame allocator (tracks free 4K pages from multiboot memory map)
36	- VMM 4-level paging infrastructure
37	
38	### Filesystem
39	- VFS mount layer with path walk and fd table
40	- ext2 filesystem (block groups, inodes, directory entries, bitmaps)
41	- RawFS in-memory block device as ext2 backing store
42	- File syscalls: open / close / read / write / seek / stat / fstat / readdir / mkdir / unlink / creat
43	- Standard directory seeding at boot: `/tmp`, `/home`, `/bin`, `/etc`
44	
45	### JavaScript Userland
46	- MuJS interpreter embedded in the kernel (freestanding port)
47	- Ring-1 shell dispatches named apps via `SYS_JS_EXEC`
48	- Apps embedded as C string literals, executed in kernel MuJS context
49	- JS filesystem bindings: `fsOpen`, `fsRead`, `fsWrite`, `fsClose`, `fsReaddir`, `fsStat`, `fsMkdir`, `fsUnlink`, `fsCreat`
50	- JS terminal bindings: `print`, `println`, `readline`, `getChar`, `setCursor`, `setColor`, `screenWidth`, `screenHeight`, `clearScreen`, `printAt`
51	- JS path bindings: `chdir`, `getcwd`
52	- `__cwd` and `__args` preamble injected into every app execution
53	
54	### Native C Snake Game
55	- Runs as a Ring-1 builtin — no MuJS involved
56	- Direct VGA writes for zero-overhead rendering
57	- Blue snake (`@` head, `o` body) on black; magenta apple (`*`)
58	- 50×20 play area with right-panel HUD showing current score and best score
59	- Highscore persisted to `/tmp/snake_hi` across sessions (plain decimal text file)
60	- "New best!" message on the game-over screen when a record is broken
61	- Speed increases at scores 10, 20, 35
62	
63	### Standard Library
64	- Freestanding libc: `string`, `stdlib`, `stdio` (printf/snprintf), `math`, `setjmp`, `time`
65	- POSIX-compatible time: `gmtime_r`, `mktime`, `strftime`, `clock_gettime`
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Project Structure
70	
71	```
72	NephilimOS/
73	├── boot/
74	│   ├── boot.asm          # Multiboot entry; paging setup; long-mode transition
75	│   ├── gdt_flush.asm     # Reload segment registers + far-return to new CS
76	│   ├── isr_stubs.asm     # 256 ISR stubs; uniform int_frame_t layout
77	│   ├── ctx_switch.asm    # Low-level context switch (callee-saved regs)
78	│   └── font8x16.asm      # 8×16 VGA bitmap font data
79	├── kernel/
80	│   ├── kernel.c          # kernel_main; subsystem init; shell spawn; idle loop
81	│   ├── vga.c             # VGA text-mode driver; kprint, kprint_char, vga_goto
82	│   ├── detect.c          # CPUID CPU detection; RAM from multiboot info
83	│   ├── gdt.c             # GDT + TSS setup
84	│   ├── idt.c             # IDT init; int_handler; IRQ dispatch; syscall vector
85	│   ├── timer.c           # PIC remap; PIT 100 Hz; timer_irq_handler; timer_gettime
86	│   ├── syscall.c         # syscall_dispatch; all sys_* implementations; kernel_cwd
87	│   ├── process.c         # PCB; scheduler; proc_create/fork/destroy/switch
88	│   ├── pmm.c             # Physical memory manager (frame allocator)
89	│   ├── vmm.c             # Virtual memory manager (4-level paging)
90	│   ├── js_runtime.c      # MuJS engine init; all JS bindings
91	│   ├── seed.c            # initramfs: seeds /bin/* from embedded JS source
92	│   └── minit.c           # miniature init — process supervisor
93	├── drivers/
94	│   ├── keyboard.c        # PS/2 keyboard; scancode→KEY_*; keyboard_getkey()
95	│   └── serial.c          # Serial port output (QEMU debug console)
96	├── fs/
97	│   ├── ext2.c            # ext2 implementation (block groups, inodes, bitmaps)
98	│   ├── rawfs.c           # Raw in-memory block device + ext2 mount wrapper
99	│   └── vfs.c             # VFS mount table + path walk + fd table
100	├── lib/
101	│   └── libtranslateunix.c # Unix-like path helpers for VFS
102	├── stdlib/
103	│   ├── string.c          # memset/cpy/move/cmp, strlen, str*
104	│   ├── stdlib.c          # abs, atoi, itoa, utoa
105	│   ├── malloc.c          # First-fit free-list allocator with coalescing
106	│   ├── math.c            # Freestanding math (sin, cos, sqrt, log, exp, …)
107	│   ├── printf.c          # Freestanding printf / snprintf / vsnprintf
108	│   ├── setjmp.asm        # setjmp / longjmp
109	│   └── time.c            # POSIX time: gmtime_r, mktime, strftime, format_time
110	├── userland/
111	│   ├── shell/
112	│   │   └── main.c        # Ring-1 shell; line editing; history; app dispatch
113	│   ├── snake/
114	│   │   └── main.c        # Native C snake game; VGA + syscalls; highscore I/O
115	│   ├── apps.c            # JS app registry; cwd+args preamble injection
116	│   ├── calculator/
117	│   │   └── main.js       # Expression evaluator (MuJS)
118	│   ├── coreutils/
119	│   │   ├── ls.js         # List directory
120	│   │   ├── cat.js        # Print file contents
121	│   │   ├── touch.js      # Create empty file
122	│   │   ├── mkdir.js      # Create directory
123	│   │   ├── rm.js         # Remove file
124	│   │   ├── stat.js       # Show file metadata
125	│   │   ├── write.js      # Write text to file
126	│   │   ├── wc.js         # Count lines/words/bytes
127	│   │   ├── uname.js      # Show system information
128	│   │   ├── cd.js         # Change directory (path normalisation + chdir binding)
129	│   │   ├── ps.js         # List running processes
130	│   │   ├── cp.js         # Copy file
131	│   │   ├── mv.js         # Move / rename file
132	│   │   ├── grep.js       # Search lines matching pattern
133	│   │   └── sed.js        # Substitute pattern in file output
134	│   └── quill/
135	│       └── main.js       # Full-screen text editor (nano-like)
136	├── include/              # All public headers
137	├── external/
138	│   └── include/mujs/     # MuJS JS engine (vendored); mujs_port.h shims libc
139	├── grub/
140	│   └── grub.cfg          # GRUB menu entry
141	├── linker.ld             # Kernel linker script; loads at 1 MiB; elf64-x86-64
142	├── tools/
143	│   └── js2ch.py          # Converts .js source to C string-literal .js.h header
144	└── Makefile
145	```
146	
147	---
148	
149	## Prerequisites
150	
151	All build steps run inside **WSL2**. The toolchain uses **Clang/LLVM** — no GCC cross-compiler required.
152	
153	### openSUSE (Tumbleweed / Leap)
154	
155	```bash
156	sudo zypper install clang llvm lld nasm
157	sudo zypper install grub2-x86_64-efi xorriso
158	sudo zypper install qemu-x86
159	```
160	
161	### Ubuntu / Debian
162	
163	```bash
164	sudo apt install clang lld nasm grub-pc-bin grub-common xorriso qemu-system-x86
165	```
166	
167	### Arch Linux
168	
169	```bash
170	sudo pacman -S clang lld nasm grub xorriso qemu-system-x86
171	```
172	
173	---
174	
175	## Build
176	
177	```bash
178	# Compile and link the kernel ELF
179	make
180	
181	# Build a bootable ISO image
182	make iso
183	
184	# Clean all build artifacts
185	make clean
186	```
187	
188	Outputs:
189	
190	| File | Description |
191	|------|-------------|
192	| `nephilim.kernel` | ELF64 kernel binary |
193	| `nephilim.iso` | Bootable ISO (GRUB + kernel) |
194	
195	---
196	
197	## Run
198	
199	```bash
200	# Minimal boot
201	qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso
202	
203	# Recommended: 256 MB RAM
204	qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -vga std
205	
206	# With serial output mirrored to host terminal
207	qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown
208	```
209	
210	---
211	
212	## Boot Flow
213	
214	```
215	GRUB (Multiboot 1)
216	  └─ _start  [32-bit protected mode]
217	       ├─ Check CPUID / long-mode support
218	       ├─ Build identity-mapped page tables (PML4→PDPT→PD, 2 MiB huge pages)
219	       ├─ Enable PAE + EFER.LME, enable paging
220	       ├─ Load 64-bit GDT, far-jump to long mode
221	       └─ kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
222	            ├─ serial_init / vga_init / vga_clear
223	            ├─ gdt_init       — Ring 0/1 segments + 64-bit TSS
224	            ├─ idt_init       — 256 interrupt gates
225	            ├─ timer_init     — remap 8259 PIC; PIT → 100 Hz
226	            ├─ sti            — enable hardware interrupts
227	            ├─ syscall_init   — patch IDT[0x80] to DPL=1
228	            ├─ keyboard_init  — PS/2 scancode set 1
229	            ├─ process_init   — clear PCB table; init scheduler
230	            ├─ detect_cpu / detect RAM from multiboot
231	            ├─ pmm_init / vmm_init
232	            ├─ vfs_init / rawfs+ext2 mount /
233	            ├─ seed /tmp /home /bin /etc + initramfs_seed()
234	            ├─ minit_init
235	            ├─ proc_create(shell_main, RING_SERVICE) → proc_wake
236	            ├─ minit_watch(shell_main, "shell", shell)
237	            └─ sched_pick → proc_switch → shell_main
238	                 idle loop: minit_tick() + hlt
239	```
240	
241	---
242	
243	## Shell
244	
245	The Ring-1 shell (`userland/shell/main.c`) provides full line editing and history navigation.
246	
247	### Built-in Commands
248	
249	| Command | Description |
250	|---------|-------------|
251	| `help` | Show available commands |
252	| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
253	| `echo <text>` | Print text |
254	| `pwd` | Print working directory |
255	| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
256	| `pid` | Show current process PID |
257	| `reboot` | Reboot the system |
258	| `shutdown` | Power off |
259	| `snake` | Native C snake game |
260	
261	### JS Coreutils
262	
263	| Command | Description |
264	|---------|-------------|
265	| `cd [path]` | Change directory (supports `~`, `.`, `..`) |
266	| `ls [path]` | List directory contents |
267	| `cat <file>` | Print file contents |
268	| `touch <file>` | Create empty file |
269	| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory |
270	| `rm <file>` | Remove file |
271	| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file |
272	| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move / rename file |
273	| `stat <file>` | Show file metadata |
274	| `write <file> <text>` | Write text to file |
275	| `wc <file>` | Count lines / words / bytes |
276	| `grep <pat> <file>` | Print lines matching pattern |
277	| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute pattern in file output |
278	| `uname` | Show system information |
279	| `ps` | List running processes |
280	| `calculator` | Expression evaluator |
281	| `quill [file]` | Full-screen text editor |
282	
283	### Key Bindings
284	
285	| Key | Action |
286	|-----|--------|
287	| `Up` / `Down` | Navigate history |
288	| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor within line |
289	| `Home` / `End` | Jump to start / end of line |
290	| `Backspace` | Delete character left of cursor |
291	| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |
292	
293	---
294	
295	## Snake Game
296	
297	Type `snake` at the shell prompt to launch.
298	
299	| Control | Action |
300	|---------|--------|
301	| `W` / `↑` | Move up |
302	| `S` / `↓` | Move down |
303	| `A` / `←` | Move left |
304	| `D` / `→` | Move right |
305	| `P` | Pause / resume |
306	| `Q` | Quit |
307	
308	The play area is 50×20 cells. The snake is bright blue; the apple is magenta. Speed increases at scores 10, 20, and 35.
309	
310	The highscore is saved as a plain decimal integer to `/tmp/snake_hi` after each game. The HUD displays both the current score and the all-time best. The game-over screen shows "New best!" when the record is broken.
311	
312	---
313	
314	## Syscall Interface
315	
316	Invoked via `int $0x80`. Arguments: `rax`=number, `rdi`, `rsi`, `rdx`. Return in `rax`.
317	
318	| # | Name | Arguments | Description |
319	|---|------|-----------|-------------|
320	| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | `rdi`=str, `rsi`=len | Print string to VGA |
321	| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | Clear screen |
322	| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | Print newline |
323	| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | `rdi`=addr | Read 64-bit physical address |
324	| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy CPU model string |
325	| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | ACPI/QEMU power off |
326	| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | PS/2 controller reset |
327	| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | Voluntary CPU yield |
328	| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | Current process PID |
329	| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | `rdi`=entry, `rsi`=name, `rdx`=priority | Create + wake process |
330	| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | Fork current process |
331	| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | `rdi`=code | Terminate calling process |
332	| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | `rdi`=pid | Destroy process by PID |
333	| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | `rdi`=pid | Block until PID exits |
334	| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | `rdi`=ticks | Sleep for N scheduler ticks |
335	| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | `rdi`=pid, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=size | Get process name |
336	| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | Blocking ASCII keyboard read |
337	| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | `rdi`=source_ptr | Run JS string via MuJS |
338	| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | Seconds since boot |
339	| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | `rdi`=`*timespec`, `rsi`=clk_id | Fill timespec |
340	| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=flags, `rdx`=mode | Open file; returns fd |
341	| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | `rdi`=fd | Close file |
342	| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Read bytes |
343	| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Write bytes |
344	| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=offset, `rdx`=whence | Seek file |
345	| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by path |
346	| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by fd |
347	| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=index, `rdx`=dirent_ptr | Read directory entry |
348	| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Make directory |
349	| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | `rdi`=path | Unlink file |
350	| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Create file; returns fd |
351	| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | Blocking read; returns `KEY_*` or ASCII |
352	| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | `rdi`=path | Change kernel working directory |
353	| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy kernel cwd to buffer |
354	| 34 | `SYS_PS` | `rdi`=`proc_snap_t*`, `rsi`=max | Fill snapshot array; returns count |
355	| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | Non-blocking key read; -1 if no key pending |
356	
357	---
358	
359	## Toolchain
360	
361	| Tool | Role |
362	|------|------|
363	| `clang --target=x86_64-elf` | Freestanding C compiler |
364	| `ld.lld` | ELF64 linker |
365	| `nasm -f elf64` | Assembler (all `.asm` files) |
366	| `grub-mkrescue` | ISO image builder |
367	| `qemu-system-x86_64` | Emulator |
368	| `python tools/js2ch.py` | JS → C string-literal header |
369	
370	Key compiler flags: `--target=x86_64-elf -ffreestanding -O2 -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel`
371	
372	---
373	
374	## References
375	
376	- [OSDev Wiki](https://wiki.osdev.org/)
377	- [MINIX 3](https://www.minix3.org/)
378	- [Intel SDM Vol. 3A](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/articles/technical/intel-sdm.html)
379	- [Multiboot Specification](https://www.gnu.org/software/grub/manual/multiboot/multiboot.html)
380	- [MuJS](https://mujs.com/) — lightweight JavaScript interpreter
381

# NephilimOS

A MINIX-inspired, bare-metal x86_64 operating system built from scratch — with a JavaScript userland and a C snake game.

---

## What It Is

NephilimOS is a 64-bit hobby kernel that boots via GRUB Multiboot 1, runs a cooperative scheduler, mounts an ext2 filesystem from a RAM disk, and executes user-facing apps written in JavaScript via an embedded MuJS interpreter. All userland runs at Ring 1 through an `int $0x80` syscall interface. A native C snake game runs directly as a Ring-1 process with direct VGA access.

---

## Feature Summary

### Boot & Hardware
- Multiboot 1 compliant — boots via GRUB on real x86_64 or QEMU
- 32-bit protected mode → 64-bit long mode transition in assembly
- Identity-mapped first 1 GiB via 2 MiB huge pages (PML4 → PDPT → PD)
- VGA text-mode driver (80×25, colour, custom 8×16 bitmap font)
- PS/2 keyboard driver — scancode set 1, shift/ctrl modifiers, extended keys (arrows, F-keys)
- PIT timer at 100 Hz (IRQ0) — monotonic tick + second counter
- 8259 PIC remapped to vectors 32–47

### Kernel Subsystems
- GDT with Ring 0 / Ring 1 descriptors and a 64-bit TSS
- IDT with 256 gates; CPU exceptions print vector + RIP and halt
- `int 0x80` syscall interface (Ring 0 and Ring 1 callable)
- Cooperative multi-process scheduler (FCFS + SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties)
- `proc_create`, `proc_fork`, `proc_destroy`, `proc_switch` process lifecycle
- **minit** — miniature init: lightweight process supervisor that restarts watched processes up to 3 times on death

### Memory
- First-fit free-list heap allocator (`malloc` / `free` / `realloc`) with block coalescing
- 64 MB heap in `.bss` (NOBITS — not stored in the ELF binary)
- PMM physical frame allocator (tracks free 4K pages from multiboot memory map)
- VMM 4-level paging infrastructure

### Filesystem
- VFS mount layer with path walk and fd table
- ext2 filesystem (block groups, inodes, directory entries, bitmaps)
- RawFS in-memory block device as ext2 backing store
- File syscalls: open / close / read / write / seek / stat / fstat / readdir / mkdir / unlink / creat
- Standard directory seeding at boot: `/tmp`, `/home`, `/bin`, `/etc`

### JavaScript Userland
- MuJS interpreter embedded in the kernel (freestanding port)
- Ring-1 shell dispatches named apps via `SYS_JS_EXEC`
- Apps embedded as C string literals, executed in kernel MuJS context
- JS filesystem bindings: `fsOpen`, `fsRead`, `fsWrite`, `fsClose`, `fsReaddir`, `fsStat`, `fsMkdir`, `fsUnlink`, `fsCreat`
- JS terminal bindings: `print`, `println`, `readline`, `getChar`, `setCursor`, `setColor`, `screenWidth`, `screenHeight`, `clearScreen`, `printAt`
- JS path bindings: `chdir`, `getcwd`
- `__cwd` and `__args` preamble injected into every app execution

### Native C Snake Game
- Runs as a Ring-1 builtin — no MuJS involved
- Direct VGA writes for zero-overhead rendering
- Blue snake (`@` head, `o` body) on black; magenta apple (`*`)
- 50×20 play area with right-panel HUD showing current score and best score
- Highscore persisted to `/tmp/snake_hi` across sessions (plain decimal text file)
- "New best!" message on the game-over screen when a record is broken
- Speed increases at scores 10, 20, 35

### Standard Library
- Freestanding libc: `string`, `stdlib`, `stdio` (printf/snprintf), `math`, `setjmp`, `time`
- POSIX-compatible time: `gmtime_r`, `mktime`, `strftime`, `clock_gettime`

---

## Project Structure

```
NephilimOS/
├── boot/
│   ├── boot.asm          # Multiboot entry; paging setup; long-mode transition
│   ├── gdt_flush.asm     # Reload segment registers + far-return to new CS
│   ├── isr_stubs.asm     # 256 ISR stubs; uniform int_frame_t layout
│   ├── ctx_switch.asm    # Low-level context switch (callee-saved regs)
│   └── font8x16.asm      # 8×16 VGA bitmap font data
├── kernel/
│   ├── kernel.c          # kernel_main; subsystem init; shell spawn; idle loop
│   ├── vga.c             # VGA text-mode driver; kprint, kprint_char, vga_goto
│   ├── detect.c          # CPUID CPU detection; RAM from multiboot info
│   ├── gdt.c             # GDT + TSS setup
│   ├── idt.c             # IDT init; int_handler; IRQ dispatch; syscall vector
│   ├── timer.c           # PIC remap; PIT 100 Hz; timer_irq_handler; timer_gettime
│   ├── syscall.c         # syscall_dispatch; all sys_* implementations; kernel_cwd
│   ├── process.c         # PCB; scheduler; proc_create/fork/destroy/switch
│   ├── pmm.c             # Physical memory manager (frame allocator)
│   ├── vmm.c             # Virtual memory manager (4-level paging)
│   ├── js_runtime.c      # MuJS engine init; all JS bindings
│   ├── seed.c            # initramfs: seeds /bin/* from embedded JS source
│   └── minit.c           # miniature init — process supervisor
├── drivers/
│   ├── keyboard.c        # PS/2 keyboard; scancode→KEY_*; keyboard_getkey()
│   └── serial.c          # Serial port output (QEMU debug console)
├── fs/
│   ├── ext2.c            # ext2 implementation (block groups, inodes, bitmaps)
│   ├── rawfs.c           # Raw in-memory block device + ext2 mount wrapper
│   └── vfs.c             # VFS mount table + path walk + fd table
├── lib/
│   └── libtranslateunix.c # Unix-like path helpers for VFS
├── stdlib/
│   ├── string.c          # memset/cpy/move/cmp, strlen, str*
│   ├── stdlib.c          # abs, atoi, itoa, utoa
│   ├── malloc.c          # First-fit free-list allocator with coalescing
│   ├── math.c            # Freestanding math (sin, cos, sqrt, log, exp, …)
│   ├── printf.c          # Freestanding printf / snprintf / vsnprintf
│   ├── setjmp.asm        # setjmp / longjmp
│   └── time.c            # POSIX time: gmtime_r, mktime, strftime, format_time
├── userland/
│   ├── shell/
│   │   └── main.c        # Ring-1 shell; line editing; history; app dispatch
│   ├── snake/
│   │   └── main.c        # Native C snake game; VGA + syscalls; highscore I/O
│   ├── apps.c            # JS app registry; cwd+args preamble injection
│   ├── calculator/
│   │   └── main.js       # Expression evaluator (MuJS)
│   ├── coreutils/
│   │   ├── ls.js         # List directory
│   │   ├── cat.js        # Print file contents
│   │   ├── touch.js      # Create empty file
│   │   ├── mkdir.js      # Create directory
│   │   ├── rm.js         # Remove file
│   │   ├── stat.js       # Show file metadata
│   │   ├── write.js      # Write text to file
│   │   ├── wc.js         # Count lines/words/bytes
│   │   ├── uname.js      # Show system information
│   │   ├── cd.js         # Change directory (path normalisation + chdir binding)
│   │   ├── ps.js         # List running processes
│   │   ├── cp.js         # Copy file
│   │   ├── mv.js         # Move / rename file
│   │   ├── grep.js       # Search lines matching pattern
│   │   └── sed.js        # Substitute pattern in file output
│   └── quill/
│       └── main.js       # Full-screen text editor (nano-like)
├── include/              # All public headers
├── external/
│   └── include/mujs/     # MuJS JS engine (vendored); mujs_port.h shims libc
├── grub/
│   └── grub.cfg          # GRUB menu entry
├── linker.ld             # Kernel linker script; loads at 1 MiB; elf64-x86-64
├── tools/
│   └── js2ch.py          # Converts .js source to C string-literal .js.h header
└── Makefile
```

---

## Prerequisites

All build steps run inside **WSL2**. The toolchain uses **Clang/LLVM** — no GCC cross-compiler required.

### openSUSE (Tumbleweed / Leap)

```bash
sudo zypper install clang llvm lld nasm
sudo zypper install grub2-x86_64-efi xorriso
sudo zypper install qemu-x86
```

### Ubuntu / Debian

```bash
sudo apt install clang lld nasm grub-pc-bin grub-common xorriso qemu-system-x86
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
sudo pacman -S clang lld nasm grub xorriso qemu-system-x86
```

---

## Build

```bash
# Compile and link the kernel ELF
make

# Build a bootable ISO image
make iso

# Clean all build artifacts
make clean
```

Outputs:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `nephilim.kernel` | ELF64 kernel binary |
| `nephilim.iso` | Bootable ISO (GRUB + kernel) |

---

## Run

```bash
# Minimal boot
qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso

# Recommended: 256 MB RAM
qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -vga std

# With serial output mirrored to host terminal
qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown
```

---

## Boot Flow

```
GRUB (Multiboot 1)
  └─ _start  [32-bit protected mode]
       ├─ Check CPUID / long-mode support
       ├─ Build identity-mapped page tables (PML4→PDPT→PD, 2 MiB huge pages)
       ├─ Enable PAE + EFER.LME, enable paging
       ├─ Load 64-bit GDT, far-jump to long mode
       └─ kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
            ├─ serial_init / vga_init / vga_clear
            ├─ gdt_init       — Ring 0/1 segments + 64-bit TSS
            ├─ idt_init       — 256 interrupt gates
            ├─ timer_init     — remap 8259 PIC; PIT → 100 Hz
            ├─ sti            — enable hardware interrupts
            ├─ syscall_init   — patch IDT[0x80] to DPL=1
            ├─ keyboard_init  — PS/2 scancode set 1
            ├─ process_init   — clear PCB table; init scheduler
            ├─ detect_cpu / detect RAM from multiboot
            ├─ pmm_init / vmm_init
            ├─ vfs_init / rawfs+ext2 mount /
            ├─ seed /tmp /home /bin /etc + initramfs_seed()
            ├─ minit_init
            ├─ proc_create(shell_main, RING_SERVICE) → proc_wake
            ├─ minit_watch(shell_main, "shell", shell)
            └─ sched_pick → proc_switch → shell_main
                 idle loop: minit_tick() + hlt
```

---

## Shell

The Ring-1 shell (`userland/shell/main.c`) provides full line editing and history navigation.

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `help` | Show available commands |
| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
| `echo <text>` | Print text |
| `pwd` | Print working directory |
| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
| `pid` | Show current process PID |
| `reboot` | Reboot the system |
| `shutdown` | Power off |
| `snake` | Native C snake game |

### JS Coreutils

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `cd [path]` | Change directory (supports `~`, `.`, `..`) |
| `ls [path]` | List directory contents |
| `cat <file>` | Print file contents |
| `touch <file>` | Create empty file |
| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory |
| `rm <file>` | Remove file |
| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file |
| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move / rename file |
| `stat <file>` | Show file metadata |
| `write <file> <text>` | Write text to file |
| `wc <file>` | Count lines / words / bytes |
| `grep <pat> <file>` | Print lines matching pattern |
| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute pattern in file output |
| `uname` | Show system information |
| `ps` | List running processes |
| `calculator` | Expression evaluator |
| `quill [file]` | Full-screen text editor |

### Key Bindings

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Up` / `Down` | Navigate history |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor within line |
| `Home` / `End` | Jump to start / end of line |
| `Backspace` | Delete character left of cursor |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |

---

## Snake Game

Type `snake` at the shell prompt to launch.

| Control | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `W` / `↑` | Move up |
| `S` / `↓` | Move down |
| `A` / `←` | Move left |
| `D` / `→` | Move right |
| `P` | Pause / resume |
| `Q` | Quit |

The play area is 50×20 cells. The snake is bright blue; the apple is magenta. Speed increases at scores 10, 20, and 35.

The highscore is saved as a plain decimal integer to `/tmp/snake_hi` after each game. The HUD displays both the current score and the all-time best. The game-over screen shows "New best!" when the record is broken.

---

## Syscall Interface

Invoked via `int $0x80`. Arguments: `rax`=number, `rdi`, `rsi`, `rdx`. Return in `rax`.

| # | Name | Arguments | Description |
|---|------|-----------|-------------|
| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | `rdi`=str, `rsi`=len | Print string to VGA |
| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | Clear screen |
| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | Print newline |
| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | `rdi`=addr | Read 64-bit physical address |
| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy CPU model string |
| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | ACPI/QEMU power off |
| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | PS/2 controller reset |
| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | Voluntary CPU yield |
| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | Current process PID |
| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | `rdi`=entry, `rsi`=name, `rdx`=priority | Create + wake process |
| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | Fork current process |
| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | `rdi`=code | Terminate calling process |
| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | `rdi`=pid | Destroy process by PID |
| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | `rdi`=pid | Block until PID exits |
| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | `rdi`=ticks | Sleep for N scheduler ticks |
| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | `rdi`=pid, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=size | Get process name |
| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | Blocking ASCII keyboard read |
| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | `rdi`=source_ptr | Run JS string via MuJS |
| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | Seconds since boot |
| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | `rdi`=`*timespec`, `rsi`=clk_id | Fill timespec |
| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=flags, `rdx`=mode | Open file; returns fd |
| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | `rdi`=fd | Close file |
| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Read bytes |
| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Write bytes |
| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=offset, `rdx`=whence | Seek file |
| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by path |
| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by fd |
| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=index, `rdx`=dirent_ptr | Read directory entry |
| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Make directory |
| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | `rdi`=path | Unlink file |
| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Create file; returns fd |
| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | Blocking read; returns `KEY_*` or ASCII |
| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | `rdi`=path | Change kernel working directory |
| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy kernel cwd to buffer |
| 34 | `SYS_PS` | `rdi`=`proc_snap_t*`, `rsi`=max | Fill snapshot array; returns count |
| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | Non-blocking key read; -1 if no key pending |

---

## Toolchain

| Tool | Role |
|------|------|
| `clang --target=x86_64-elf` | Freestanding C compiler |
| `ld.lld` | ELF64 linker |
| `nasm -f elf64` | Assembler (all `.asm` files) |
| `grub-mkrescue` | ISO image builder |
| `qemu-system-x86_64` | Emulator |
| `python tools/js2ch.py` | JS → C string-literal header |

Key compiler flags: `--target=x86_64-elf -ffreestanding -O2 -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel`

---

## References

- [OSDev Wiki](https://wiki.osdev.org/)
- [MINIX 3](https://www.minix3.org/)
- [Intel SDM Vol. 3A](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/articles/technical/intel-sdm.html)
- [Multiboot Specification](https://www.gnu.org/software/grub/manual/multiboot/multiboot.html)
- [MuJS](https://mujs.com/) — lightweight JavaScript interpreter

1	# NephilimOS — Technical Documentation
2	
3	Full reference for kernel internals, the JavaScript userland, and the syscall ABI.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Table of Contents
8	
9	1. [Boot Sequence](#1-boot-sequence)
10	2. [Memory Layout](#2-memory-layout)
11	3. [Kernel Subsystems](#3-kernel-subsystems)
12	   - 3.1 [VGA Driver](#31-vga-driver)
13	   - 3.2 [GDT](#32-gdt)
14	   - 3.3 [IDT & Exceptions](#33-idt--exceptions)
15	   - 3.4 [PIC & PIT Timer](#34-pic--pit-timer)
16	   - 3.5 [PS/2 Keyboard](#35-ps2-keyboard)
17	   - 3.6 [Process Manager & Scheduler](#36-process-manager--scheduler)
18	   - 3.7 [PMM & VMM](#37-pmm--vmm)
19	   - 3.8 [minit — Process Supervisor](#38-minit--process-supervisor)
20	4. [Syscall Interface](#4-syscall-interface)
21	5. [Filesystem Stack](#5-filesystem-stack)
22	   - 5.1 [VFS Layer](#51-vfs-layer)
23	   - 5.2 [ext2 Implementation](#52-ext2-implementation)
24	   - 5.3 [RawFS Block Device](#53-rawfs-block-device)
25	   - 5.4 [initramfs Seed](#54-initramfs-seed)
26	6. [Heap Allocator](#6-heap-allocator)
27	7. [JavaScript Userland](#7-javascript-userland)
28	   - 7.1 [MuJS Integration](#71-mujs-integration)
29	   - 7.2 [JS Bindings Reference](#72-js-bindings-reference)
30	   - 7.3 [App Registry & Dispatch](#73-app-registry--dispatch)
31	   - 7.4 [Cwd & Args Preamble](#74-cwd--args-preamble)
32	8. [Shell](#8-shell)
33	   - 8.1 [Built-in Commands](#81-built-in-commands)
34	   - 8.2 [Line Editing](#82-line-editing)
35	   - 8.3 [History Ring](#83-history-ring)
36	9. [JS Apps Reference](#9-js-apps-reference)
37	   - 9.1 [coreutils](#91-coreutils)
38	   - 9.2 [calculator](#92-calculator)
39	   - 9.3 [quill](#93-quill)
40	10. [Snake Game](#10-snake-game)
41	11. [Keyboard Key Codes](#11-keyboard-key-codes)
42	12. [Linker Script](#12-linker-script)
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 1. Boot Sequence
47	
48	`boot/boot.asm` is the Multiboot 1 entry point, loaded at physical address `0x100000` by GRUB.
49	
50	```
51	[32-bit protected mode]
52	_start:
53	  1. Verify CPUID support (toggle ID flag in EFLAGS)
54	  2. Verify long-mode support (CPUID leaf 0x80000001, bit 29)
55	  3. Build identity-mapped page tables:
56	       PML4[0] → PDPT
57	       PDPT[0] → PD
58	       PD[0..511] → 2 MiB huge pages (covers 0..1 GiB)
59	  4. Load PML4 into CR3
60	  5. Set CR4.PAE, CR4.OSFXSR, CR4.OSXMMEXCPT
61	  6. Set EFER.LME via MSR 0xC0000080
62	  7. Set CR0.PG (enable paging → activates long mode)
63	  8. Load 64-bit GDT stub
64	  9. Far-jump to long_mode_start (CS selector 0x08)
65	
66	[64-bit long mode]
67	long_mode_start:
68	  10. Zero BSS segment
69	  11. Call kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
70	```
71	
72	`kernel_main` initialises every subsystem in dependency order before creating the shell process and entering the idle loop.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 2. Memory Layout
77	
78	### Physical Address Space (at boot)
79	
80	| Range | Contents |
81	|-------|----------|
82	| `0x000000 – 0x0FFFFF` | BIOS / legacy (not used) |
83	| `0x100000 – ~` | Kernel ELF loaded here by GRUB |
84	| Above kernel | Free RAM (tracked by PMM) |
85	
86	### Virtual Address Space (kernel)
87	
88	| Symbol | Address | Description |
89	|--------|---------|-------------|
90	| `.text` start | `0x100000` | Kernel code |
91	| `.data` / `.rodata` | after `.text` | Kernel data |
92	| `.bss` | after `.data` | Zero-init + heap |
93	| `_heap_start` | inside `.bss` | Start of allocator arena |
94	| `_heap_end` | `_heap_start + 64 MiB` | End of allocator arena |
95	
96	The heap is declared inside `.bss` (NOBITS ELF section) so it occupies no space in the binary on disk.
97	
98	### Linker-Exported Symbols
99	
100	```c
101	extern char _heap_start[];   /* first byte of heap arena */
102	extern char _heap_end[];     /* one past last byte       */
103	```
104	
105	Both used by `stdlib/malloc.c` to initialise the free-list.
106	
107	---
108	
109	## 3. Kernel Subsystems
110	
111	### 3.1 VGA Driver
112	
113	**File:** `kernel/vga.c`, `include/vga.h`
114	
115	Mode: 80×25 text, standard VGA MMIO at `0xB8000`. Cell format: `[attribute][char]` (2 bytes).
116	
117	```c
118	void vga_init(void);               /* set 80×25, upload font, set cursor style */
119	void vga_clear(void);              /* fill screen with spaces, colour 0x07     */
120	void kprint(const char *s);        /* print null-terminated string             */
121	void kprint_char(char c);          /* print single character                   */
122	void kprint_uint64(uint64_t n);    /* print decimal integer                    */
123	void vga_goto(size_t col, size_t row); /* move hardware cursor + update col/row */
124	```
125	
126	**Font:** 8×16 glyphs in `boot/font8x16.asm`. Uploaded to VGA plane 2 (0xA0000) at boot. Cell height set to 16 rows (register 0x09 = 0x0F), cursor at scanlines 14–15.
127	
128	**Colour:** `vga_color` global (`uint8_t`), set via `VGA_COLOR(fg, bg)` macro. JS apps can change it with the `setColor` binding.
129	
130	---
131	
132	### 3.2 GDT
133	
134	**File:** `kernel/gdt.c`, `include/gdt.h`
135	
136	| Index | Selector | Ring | Type |
137	|-------|----------|------|------|
138	| 0 | `0x00` | — | Null |
139	| 1 | `0x08` | 0 | 64-bit code |
140	| 2 | `0x10` | 0 | Data |
141	| 3 | `0x18` | 1 | 64-bit code (userland) |
142	| 4 | `0x20` | 1 | Data (userland) |
143	| 5–6 | `0x28` | 0 | 64-bit TSS (2 descriptors) |
144	
145	`gdt_flush()` (in `boot/gdt_flush.asm`) reloads all segment registers and performs a far-return to update CS.
146	
147	---
148	
149	### 3.3 IDT & Exceptions
150	
151	**File:** `kernel/idt.c`, `boot/isr_stubs.asm`
152	
153	256 interrupt gates, all installed at `idt_init()`. The stub macro in `isr_stubs.asm` pushes a uniform `int_frame_t` layout (error code + vector number) then calls the C handler.
154	
155	CPU exceptions (vectors 0–31) print the vector number and faulting RIP to VGA, then `hlt`.
156	
157	Hardware IRQs (vectors 32–47) are dispatched through the IRQ handler table. IRQ0 drives the timer; IRQ1 drives the keyboard.
158	
159	`int $0x80` (vector 128) is patched to DPL=1 by `syscall_init()` so Ring-1 code can invoke it without a GPF.
160	
161	---
162	
163	### 3.4 PIC & PIT Timer
164	
165	**File:** `kernel/timer.c`
166	
167	- 8259A PIC remapped: master → vectors 32–39, slave → vectors 40–47.
168	- PIT channel 0 programmed to 100 Hz (divisor 11932).
169	- `timer_ticks` and `timer_seconds` globals incremented by IRQ0 handler.
170	- `timer_gettime()` fills a `struct timespec` from tick counters.
171	
172	---
173	
174	### 3.5 PS/2 Keyboard
175	
176	**File:** `drivers/keyboard.c`, `include/keyboard.h`
177	
178	**`keyboard_getchar()`** — blocks until a printable ASCII key, returns `char`.
179	
180	**`keyboard_getkey()`** — blocks until any key, returns an `int`:
181	- Printable ASCII → returns the ASCII code directly.
182	- Extended keys (E0-prefixed scancodes) → returns a `KEY_*` constant (≥ 256).
183	- Ctrl+letter → returns 1–26.
184	- F1–F10 → returns `KEY_F1`–`KEY_F10`.
185	
186	See [Section 10](#10-keyboard-key-codes) for the full `KEY_*` table.
187	
188	---
189	
190	### 3.6 Process Manager & Scheduler
191	
192	**File:** `kernel/process.c`, `include/process.h`, `include/sched.h`
193	
194	Each process has a `process_t` PCB:
195	
196	```c
197	typedef struct process {
198	    uint64_t   rsp;             /* saved stack pointer (context switch) */
199	    uint64_t   rip;             /* entry point                          */
200	    uint32_t   pid;
201	    proc_state_t state;         /* PROC_UNUSED, READY, RUNNING, BLOCKED, ZOMBIE */
202	    uint8_t    ring;            /* RING_KERNEL=0, RING_SERVICE=1        */
203	    uint8_t    priority;
204	    uint32_t   quantum;
205	    char       name[PROC_NAME_LEN];
206	    /* … scheduling metadata … */
207	} process_t;
208	```
209	
210	**Scheduler:** Cooperative. `sched_pick()` selects the next runnable process (SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties). `proc_switch()` performs a context switch via `boot/ctx_switch.asm` (saves/restores callee-saved registers + RSP).
211	
212	**Key functions:**
213	
214	```c
215	process_t *proc_create(void (*entry)(void), const char *name, uint8_t ring, uint32_t quantum, uint32_t priority);
216	void        proc_wake(process_t *p);
217	void        proc_switch(process_t *p);
218	process_t  *proc_by_pid(uint32_t pid);   /* returns NULL if not found */
219	void        proc_destroy(process_t *p);
220	```
221	
222	---
223	
224	### 3.7 PMM & VMM
225	
226	**File:** `kernel/pmm.c`, `kernel/vmm.c`
227	
228	**PMM** tracks 4K physical frames using a bitmap derived from the Multiboot memory map. `pmm_alloc_frame()` returns a free physical page; `pmm_free_frame()` returns it to the pool.
229	
230	**VMM** initialises 4-level paging and records the initial CR3. Used as the foundation for future per-process address spaces.
231	
232	---
233	
234	### 3.8 minit — Process Supervisor
235	
236	**File:** `kernel/minit.c`, `include/minit.h`
237	
238	minit is NephilimOS's lightweight init. It is not a separate process — it runs inside the kernel idle loop, adding zero process overhead.
239	
240	**Configuration:**
241	
242	```c
243	#define MINIT_MAX_WATCH  16   /* max supervised processes */
244	#define MINIT_MAX_RETRY   3   /* restart attempts before giving up */
245	```
246	
247	**Watch table entry:**
248	
249	```c
250	typedef struct {
251	    void       (*entry)(void);      /* entry function used to re-spawn */
252	    char         name[PROC_NAME_LEN];
253	    uint32_t     pid;               /* PID of currently-running child  */
254	    uint8_t      retries;           /* restart attempts so far         */
255	    uint8_t      active;            /* 1 = slot in use                 */
256	} watch_t;
257	```
258	
259	**API:**
260	
261	```c
262	void minit_init(void);
263	int  minit_watch(void (*entry)(void), const char *name, process_t *p);
264	void minit_tick(void);   /* call from idle loop on each hlt iteration */
265	```
266	
267	**Behaviour per tick:**
268	1. For each active slot, call `proc_by_pid(w->pid)`.
269	2. If the process is alive (`state != PROC_UNUSED && state != PROC_ZOMBIE`), skip.
270	3. If dead and `retries < MINIT_MAX_RETRY`: increment retries, log the attempt, call `proc_create` + `proc_wake`, update `w->pid`.
271	4. If `retries >= MINIT_MAX_RETRY`: log "giving up", clear the slot.
272	
273	Currently the shell (`shell_main`) is the only watched process.
274	
275	---

277	## 4. Syscall Interface
278	
279	**File:** `kernel/syscall.c`, `include/syscall.h`
280	
281	Invoked via `int $0x80`. Convention:
282	
283	| Register | Role |
284	|----------|------|
285	| `rax` | Syscall number (in); return value (out) |
286	| `rdi` | Argument 1 |
287	| `rsi` | Argument 2 |
288	| `rdx` | Argument 3 |
289	
290	Inline helpers in `include/syscall.h`:
291	
292	```c
293	int64_t syscall0(uint64_t num);
294	int64_t syscall1(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1);
295	int64_t syscall2(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1, uint64_t a2);
296	int64_t syscall3(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1, uint64_t a2, uint64_t a3);
297	```
298	
299	**Full syscall table:**
300	
301	| # | Name | `rdi` | `rsi` | `rdx` | Returns |
302	|---|------|-------|-------|-------|---------|
303	| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | str ptr | len (0=null-term) | — | 0 |
304	| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | — | — | 0 |
305	| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | — | — | 0 |
306	| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | phys addr | — | — | uint64 |
307	| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | buf ptr | buf size | — | 0 |
308	| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | — | — | — |
309	| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | — | — | — |
310	| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | — | — | 0 |
311	| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | — | — | pid |
312	| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | entry fn | name ptr | priority | pid |
313	| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | — | — | child pid / 0 |
314	| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | exit code | — | — | — |
315	| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | pid | — | — | 0 / -1 |
316	| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | pid | — | — | 0 |
317	| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | ticks | — | — | 0 |
318	| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | pid | buf ptr | size | 0 / -1 |
319	| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | — | — | ASCII char |
320	| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | source ptr | — | — | 0 |
321	| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | — | — | seconds |
322	| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | timespec ptr | clock id | — | 0 |
323	| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | path | flags | mode | fd / -1 |
324	| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | fd | — | — | 0 / -1 |
325	| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | fd | buf ptr | len | bytes / -1 |
326	| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | fd | buf ptr | len | bytes / -1 |
327	| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | fd | offset | whence | new offset |
328	| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | path | stat ptr | — | 0 / -1 |
329	| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | fd | stat ptr | — | 0 / -1 |
330	| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | fd | index | dirent ptr | 0 / -1 |
331	| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | path | mode | — | 0 / -1 |
332	| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | path | — | — | 0 / -1 |
333	| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | path | mode | — | fd / -1 |
334	| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | — | — | `KEY_*` or ASCII |
335	| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | path | — | — | 0 / -1 |
336	| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | buf ptr | size | — | 0 |
337	| 34 | `SYS_PS` | snap buf ptr | max_entries | — | count of live procs |
338	| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | — | — | `KEY_*`/ASCII or -1 if none |
339	
340	**Return codes (negative):**
341	
342	| Code | Meaning |
343	|------|---------|
344	| `ESYS_BADNUM` (-1) | Unknown syscall number |
345	| `ESYS_BADARG` (-2) | Invalid argument |
346	| `ESYS_NOMEM` (-3) | Process table full |
347	| `ESYS_NOPROC` (-4) | No such PID |
348	| `ESYS_PERM` (-5) | Privilege violation |

351	
352	## 5. Filesystem Stack
353	
354	```
355	JS app / shell
356	     │  (path string)
357	     ▼
358	  VFS layer  (vfs.c)
359	     │  (vfs_driver_t interface)
360	     ▼
361	  RawFS  (rawfs.c)
362	     │  (block read/write)
363	     ▼
364	  ext2  (ext2.c)
365	     │  (in-memory byte array)
366	     ▼
367	  nephilim_disk_image[]  (declared in rawfs.c)
368	```
369	
370	### 5.1 VFS Layer
371	
372	**File:** `fs/vfs.c`, `include/fs.h`
373	
374	Single mount table entry for `/`. Translates POSIX-style paths to driver calls.
375	
376	```c
377	int vfs_mount(const char *path, vfs_driver_t *drv, void *ctx, size_t size);
378	int vfs_open(const char *path, int flags);
379	int vfs_close(int fd);
380	ssize_t vfs_read(int fd, void *buf, size_t len);
381	ssize_t vfs_write(int fd, const void *buf, size_t len);
382	int vfs_stat(const char *path, struct stat *st);
383	int vfs_readdir(int fd, int index, struct dirent *de);
384	int vfs_mkdir(const char *path, int mode);
385	int vfs_unlink(const char *path);
386	int vfs_creat(const char *path, int mode);
387	```
388	
389	### 5.2 ext2 Implementation
390	
391	**File:** `fs/ext2.c`
392	
393	Block size: 1024 bytes. Single block group layout (the entire disk is one group).
394	
395	**Block layout within the group:**
396	
397	| Block | Contents |
398	|-------|----------|
399	| 0 | (unused — Multiboot convention) |
400	| 1 | Superblock |
401	| 2 | Block Group Descriptor (BGD) |
402	| 3 | Block bitmap |
403	| 4 | Inode bitmap |
404	| 5 … 5+N-1 | Inode table (N = ceil(max_inodes × 128 / 1024)) |
405	| 5+N … end | Data blocks |
406	
407	The BGD is written at block 2, bitmaps at blocks 3 and 4. This avoids the overlap that occurred when bitmaps were placed at `gbase+1` (same block as the BGD table).
408	
409	Overhead for the allocator: `5 + inode_table_blocks`.
410	
411	### 5.3 RawFS Block Device
412	
413	**File:** `fs/rawfs.c`, `include/rawfs.h`
414	
415	`nephilim_disk_image[]` is a static byte array (`RAWFS_IMAGE_SIZE` bytes). `nephilim_disk` wraps it with a pointer and size. `rawfs_driver` is the `vfs_driver_t` that delegates to ext2.
416	
417	### 5.4 initramfs Seed
418	
419	**File:** `kernel/seed.c`
420	
421	Called once at boot after the filesystem is mounted. Writes JS app source files to `/bin/`:
422	
423	| Path | Source |
424	|------|--------|
425	| `/bin/ls` | `userland/coreutils/ls.js` |
426	| `/bin/cat` | `userland/coreutils/cat.js` |
427	| `/bin/touch` | `userland/coreutils/touch.js` |
428	| `/bin/mkdir` | `userland/coreutils/mkdir.js` |
429	| `/bin/rm` | `userland/coreutils/rm.js` |
430	| `/bin/stat` | `userland/coreutils/stat.js` |
431	| `/bin/write` | `userland/coreutils/write.js` |
432	| `/bin/wc` | `userland/coreutils/wc.js` |
433	| `/bin/uname` | `userland/coreutils/uname.js` |
434	| `/bin/cd` | `userland/coreutils/cd.js` |
435	| `/bin/calculator` | `userland/calculator/main.js` |
436	| `/bin/quill` | `userland/quill/main.js` |
437	| `/bin/ps` | `userland/coreutils/ps.js` |
438	| `/bin/cp` | `userland/coreutils/cp.js` |
439	| `/bin/mv` | `userland/coreutils/mv.js` |
440	| `/bin/grep` | `userland/coreutils/grep.js` |

443	JS sources are converted to C string literals at build time by `tools/js2ch.py` and `#include`d as `.js.h` headers.

447	## 5.5 libtranslateunix — POSIX Shim
448	
449	**File:** `lib/libtranslateunix.c`, `include/libtranslateunix.h`
450	
451	Translates standard POSIX calls into VFS operations. Intended for porting Unix software without modifying its source. All functions are prefixed `unix_*`; macro aliases in the header let callers use the bare POSIX names.
452	
453	**File I/O:**
454	
455	| Function | POSIX equivalent | Notes |
456	|----------|-----------------|-------|
457	| `unix_open(path, flags, mode)` | `open()` | Maps O_* flags to vfs_open |
458	| `unix_close(fd)` | `close()` | |
459	| `unix_read(fd, buf, len)` | `read()` | Returns bytes read |
460	| `unix_write(fd, buf, len)` | `write()` | |
461	| `unix_lseek(fd, offset, whence)` | `lseek()` | |
462	| `unix_stat(path, st)` | `stat()` | Fills `struct stat` |
463	| `unix_fstat(fd, st)` | `fstat()` | |
464	| `unix_unlink(path)` | `unlink()` | |
465	| `unix_mkdir(path, mode)` | `mkdir()` | |
466	| `unix_rmdir(path)` | `rmdir()` | Delegates to vfs_unlink |
467	| `unix_creat(path, mode)` | `creat()` | |
468	| `unix_rename(old, new)` | `rename()` | Copy+delete; no atomic dirent move |
469	| `unix_access(path, mode)` | `access()` | Checks existence only |
470	| `unix_truncate(path, len)` | `truncate()` | Open with O_TRUNC then close |
471	| `unix_ftruncate(fd, len)` | `ftruncate()` | Returns ENOTSUPP (not yet in VFS) |
472	| `unix_chmod(path, mode)` | `chmod()` | No-op stub (permissions not enforced) |
473	| `unix_fchmod(fd, mode)` | `fchmod()` | No-op stub |
474	
475	**Working directory:**
476	
477	| Function | POSIX equivalent | Notes |
478	|----------|-----------------|-------|
479	| `unix_chdir(path)` | `chdir()` | Validates via VFS, updates `kernel_cwd` |
480	| `unix_getcwd(buf, size)` | `getcwd()` | Copies `kernel_cwd` to caller buffer |
481	| `unix_realpath(path, resolved)` | `realpath()` | Joins relative paths to `kernel_cwd`; no symlink resolution |
482	
483	**Directory iteration:**
484	
485	| Function | POSIX equivalent |
486	|----------|-----------------|
487	| `unix_opendir(path)` | `opendir()` |
488	| `unix_closedir(dir)` | `closedir()` |
489	| `unix_readdir(dir)` | `readdir()` — returns `struct dirent *` |
490	| `unix_rewinddir(dir)` | `rewinddir()` |
491

493	
494	## 6. Heap Allocator
495	
496	**File:** `stdlib/malloc.c`
497	
498	First-fit free-list allocator with immediate coalescing.
499	
500	**Block header (16 bytes):**
501	
502	```c
503	typedef struct block {
504	    size_t        size;    /* usable payload bytes (not including header) */
505	    uint32_t      free;    /* 1 = free, 0 = allocated                     */
506	    uint32_t      _pad;
507	    struct block *next;    /* next block in the list (NULL = end)          */
508	} block_t;
509	```
510	
511	**`malloc(n)`:** Walks the free list, finds the first block where `b->size >= n`. If the remainder after splitting is ≥ 32 bytes (header + 16 bytes minimum payload), splits the block. Otherwise allocates the whole block.
512	
513	**`free(ptr)`:** Marks the block free, then calls `coalesce()` which merges adjacent free blocks in a single linear pass.
514	
515	**`realloc(ptr, 0)`:** Equivalent to `free(ptr)`.  
516	**`realloc(ptr, n)`:** If the existing block is large enough, returns `ptr` unchanged. Otherwise `malloc` + `memcpy` + `free`.
517	
518	**Arena:** `_heap_start` to `_heap_end` (64 MiB). Initialised by `arena_init()` which creates a single free block covering the whole range. `arena_init()` is called lazily on the first `malloc`.
519	
520	MuJS compatibility: MuJS GC calls `realloc(ptr, 0)` to release memory. The free-list allocator handles this correctly; the former bump allocator did not, causing OOM after ~8 editor keystrokes.
521	
522	---
523	
524	## 7. JavaScript Userland
525	
526	### 7.1 MuJS Integration
527	
528	**File:** `kernel/js_runtime.c`, `external/include/mujs/`
529	
530	MuJS is compiled freestanding against `mujs_port.h` which maps `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` to the kernel allocator and stubs out POSIX I/O. The interpreter runs in Ring 0 kernel context during `SYS_JS_EXEC`.
531	
532	`js_runtime_run(const char *source)` creates a fresh `js_State`, registers all bindings, injects the signal prelude, evaluates the app source string, and destroys the state. Each app invocation is isolated.
533	
534	### 7.1.1 Signal Library
535	
536	**File:** `userland/signal.js` — injected automatically into every JS state before the app source runs. No import needed.
537	
538	A minimal reactive state library. All four primitives are available as globals.
539	
540	**`signal(initialValue)`**
541	
542	```javascript
543	var count = signal(0);
544	count.get();          // read current value
545	count.peek();         // read without being tracked (safe inside effects)
546	count.set(n);         // write; notifies subscribers (deferred during batch)
547	count.sub(fn);        // subscribe: fn(newValue, oldValue) called on change
548	```
549	
550	**`computed(fn)`**
551	
552	Read-only signal whose value is derived from other signals. Recomputes lazily on `.get()` when marked stale.
553	
554	```javascript
555	var label = computed(function() {
556	    return dirty.get() ? '[modified]' : '';
557	});
558	label.get();    // recomputes if dirty changed since last call
559	label.peek();   // returns cached value without recompute
560	```
561	
562	**`effect(signals, fn)`**
563	
564	Runs `fn` immediately, then re-runs it whenever any signal in the array changes.
565	
566	```javascript
567	effect([cx, cy], function() {
568	    drawStatusBar();
569	});
570	```
571	
572	**`batch(fn)`**
573	
574	Defers all subscriber notifications until `fn` returns. Each subscriber fires at most once per batch, with the final value. Essential when multiple signals change together.
575	
576	```javascript
577	batch(function() {
578	    cx.set(0);
579	    cy.set(cy.get() - 1);
580	    dirty.set(true);
581	});
582	// all three subscribers fire here, each exactly once
583	```
584	
585	Batches nest correctly — inner batches join the outer one.
586	
587	### 7.2 JS Bindings Reference
588	
589	All functions are available globally inside any JS app.
590	
591	**Output:**
592	
593	| Function | Description |
594	|----------|-------------|
595	| `print(s)` | Print string without newline |
596	| `println(s)` | Print string + newline |
597	
598	**Input:**
599	
600	| Function | Description |
601	|----------|-------------|
602	| `readline()` | Block until Enter; return line string |
603	| `getChar()` | Block until key; return int (`KEY_*` or ASCII code) |
604	| `getKeyNonBlock()` | Return key code immediately; -1 if no key pending |
605	| `getTicks()` | Return PIT tick counter (100 Hz, monotonically increasing) |
606	
607	**Filesystem:**
608	
609	| Function | Description |
610	|----------|-------------|
611	| `fsOpen(path, mode)` | Open file; mode `"r"` or `"w"`; returns fd (int) |
612	| `fsClose(fd)` | Close fd |
613	| `fsRead(fd, len)` | Read up to len bytes; returns string or null |
614	| `fsWrite(fd, s)` | Write string to fd |
615	| `fsReaddir(fd, index)` | Return `{name, ino}` object or null |
616	| `fsStat(path)` | Return `{size, mode, ino}` or null |
617	| `fsMkdir(path)` | Create directory |
618	| `fsUnlink(path)` | Remove file |
619	| `fsCreat(path)` | Create file; returns fd |
620	
621	**Terminal:**
622	
623	| Function | Description |
624	|----------|-------------|
625	| `clearScreen()` | Clear VGA screen |
626	| `setCursor(col, row)` | Move hardware cursor to (col, row) |
627	| `setColor(fg, bg)` | Set VGA colour (0–15 each) |
628	| `printAt(col, row, s)` | Print string at specific screen position |
629	| `screenWidth()` | Returns 80 |
630	| `screenHeight()` | Returns 25 |
631	
632	**Path / CWD:**
633	
634	| Function | Description |
635	|----------|-------------|
636	| `chdir(path)` | Change kernel cwd; returns 0 or -1 |
637	| `getcwd()` | Returns current working directory string |
638	
639	**Process info:**
640	
641	| Function | Description |
642	|----------|-------------|
643	| `getProcessList()` | Returns array of `{pid, name, state, ring, priority}` objects for all live processes |
644	
645	State values: 0=unused, 1=embryo, 2=ready, 3=running, 4=blocked, 5=zombie. Ring values: 0=kernel, 1=service.
646	
647	### 7.3 App Registry & Dispatch
648	
649	**File:** `userland/apps.c`, `include/apps.h`
650	
651	```c
652	typedef struct {
653	    const char *name;      /* command typed at the shell */
654	    const char *binname;   /* filename under /bin */
655	    const char *fallback;  /* embedded JS source (if /bin read fails) */
656	} app_t;
657	```
658	
659	`app_run(const char *cmdline, const char *cwd)` searches `apps[]` for the command, reads `/bin/<binname>` via VFS, prepends the cwd+args preamble, then calls `SYS_JS_EXEC`. If the file cannot be read (e.g. during early boot), it falls back to the embedded string literal. Returns 1 if the app was found, 0 if not.
660	
661	Aliases are provided for convenience: `calc` → `calculator`, `chdir` → `cd`.
662	
663	### 7.4 Cwd & Args Preamble
664	
665	Before the app source is executed, `build_args_preamble()` prepends:
666	
667	```javascript
668	var __cwd="<current directory>";
669	var __args=["arg1","arg2",...];
670	```
671	
672	Apps access these globals:
673	
674	```javascript
675	var cwd  = typeof __cwd  !== 'undefined' ? __cwd  : '/';
676	var args = typeof __args !== 'undefined' ? __args : [];
677	```
678	
679	`__cwd` is the shell's current directory (synced from `kernel_cwd` via `SYS_GETCWD`).  
680	`__args` contains the command-line tokens after the command name itself.
681

684	## 8. Shell
685	
686	**File:** `userland/shell/main.c`
687	
688	Runs as a Ring-1 process (`RING_SERVICE`). All I/O goes through syscalls.
689	
690	### 8.1 Built-in Commands
691	
692	| Command | Description |
693	|---------|-------------|
694	| `help` | Show available commands |
695	| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
696	| `echo <text>` | Print text |
697	| `pwd` | Print working directory |
698	| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
699	| `pid` | Show current process PID |
700	| `reboot` | Reboot the system |
701	| `shutdown` | Power off |
702	| `snake` | Native C snake game |
703	
704	### 8.2 Line Editing
705	
706	`sh_readline()` maintains a `line[]` buffer and a `cursor` position. Supported operations:
707	
708	| Key | Behaviour |
709	|-----|-----------|
710	| Printable ASCII | Insert at cursor, shift right |
711	| `Backspace` | Delete left of cursor, shift left |
712	| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor one position |
713	| `Home` / `End` | Jump to beginning / end |
714	| `Enter` | Finalise line |
715	| `Ctrl+C` | Clear line, return empty |
716	| `Up` / `Down` | History navigation (see below) |
717	
718	Mid-line insert and delete are both supported with correct terminal re-rendering.
719	
720	### 8.3 History Ring
721	
722	32-entry circular buffer. Consecutive duplicates are suppressed.
723	
724	```
725	hist[32][256]    — ring buffer of stored lines
726	hist_head        — index where next entry will be written
727	hist_count       — number of valid entries (capped at 32)
728	```
729	
730	`hist_push(line)` — adds a non-empty, non-duplicate line.  
731	`hist_get(offset)` — `offset=1` = most recent, `offset=2` = previous, etc.
732	
733	While browsing history with Up, the current live input is saved to `saved[]` and restored on Down back to position 0.

740	
741	| App | Usage | Notes |
742	|-----|-------|-------|
743	| `ls [path]` | List directory | Defaults to cwd; shows files and directories |
744	| `cat <file>` | Print file | Reads and prints entire file |
745	| `touch <file>` | Create empty file | Uses `fsCreat` |
746	| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory | Uses `fsMkdir` |
747	| `rm <file>` | Remove file | Uses `fsUnlink` |
748	| `stat <file>` | Metadata | Shows size, mode, inode |
749	| `write <file> <text>` | Write to file | Overwrites; `write myfile hello world` |
750	| `wc <file>` | Count | Reports lines, words, bytes |
751	| `uname` | System info | CPU model + kernel name |
752	| `cd [path]` | Change directory | Expands `~` to `/home`; resolves `.` and `..`; calls `chdir()` |
753	| `ps` | Process list | Shows PID, ring, state, name for all live processes |
754	| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file | If dst is a directory, copies into it |
755	| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move/rename file | Copy + unlink; directory dest supported |
756	| `grep <pat> <file>` | Search lines | Supports `.` `*` `^` `$` `[abc]` `[^abc]`; multi-file prints `file:line` |
757	| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute | Replaces first (or all with `g`) occurrence of pattern per line; prints result |
758	
759	**`cd` path resolution:**
760	1. No argument → `/home`
761	2. `~` prefix → replaced with `/home`
762	3. Absolute path → used directly
763	4. Relative path → joined to `getcwd()`, then `.`/`..` normalised

769	### 9.3 quill
770	
771	Full-screen text editor. Launched as `quill [filename]`.
772	
773	**Layout (80×25):**
774	
775	| Row | Contents |
776	|-----|----------|
777	| 0 | Title bar (filename, `[modified]` flag) |
778	| 1–23 | Text editing area (23 visible lines) |
779	| 24 | Status bar (cursor position, key hints) |
780	
781	**Key bindings:**
782	
783	| Key | Action |
784	|-----|--------|
785	| `Up` / `Down` | Move cursor row |
786	| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor column |
787	| `Home` | Jump to start of line |
788	| `End` | Jump to end of line |
789	| `Backspace` | Delete character left |
790	| `Del` (KEY_DEL) | Delete character under cursor |
791	| `Enter` | Insert newline / split line |
792	| `Ctrl+S` | Save file |
793	| `Ctrl+Q` | Quit (prompts if modified) |
794	| `Ctrl+X` | Force quit without saving |
795	
796	Rows beyond the end of the file are shown as `~` (nano/vim convention). Vertical scrolling is supported when the file exceeds 23 lines.
797	
798	If launched without a filename, operates as a scratch buffer (prompts for filename on save).
799	
800	---
801	
802	## 10. Keyboard Key Codes
803	
804	Defined in `include/keyboard.h`. Returned by `keyboard_getkey()` and `SYS_GETKEY` / JS `getChar()`.
805	
806	| Constant | Value | Key |
807	|----------|-------|-----|
808	| `KEY_UP` | 0x100 | Up arrow |
809	| `KEY_DOWN` | 0x101 | Down arrow |
810	| `KEY_LEFT` | 0x102 | Left arrow |
811	| `KEY_RIGHT` | 0x103 | Right arrow |
812	| `KEY_HOME` | 0x104 | Home |
813	| `KEY_END` | 0x105 | End |
814	| `KEY_PGUP` | 0x106 | Page Up |
815	| `KEY_PGDN` | 0x107 | Page Down |
816	| `KEY_DEL` | 0x108 | Delete |
817	| `KEY_F1` | 0x110 | F1 |
818	| `KEY_F2` | 0x111 | F2 |
819	| … | … | … |
820	| `KEY_F10` | 0x119 | F10 |
821	
822	ASCII codes (0–127) are returned directly. Ctrl+letter returns 1–26 (`Ctrl+A` = 1, `Ctrl+C` = 3, `Ctrl+S` = 19, `Ctrl+Q` = 17, `Ctrl+X` = 24).
823	
824	---
825	
826	## 11. Linker Script
827	
828	**File:** `linker.ld`
829	
830	```
831	ENTRY(_start)
832	OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-x86-64)
833	OUTPUT_ARCH(i386:x86-64)
834	
835	SECTIONS {
836	    . = 1M;          /* load address: 1 MiB */
837	
838	    .text   : { *(.text*) }
839	    .rodata : { *(.rodata*) }
840	    .data   : { *(.data*)  }
841	
842	    .bss : ALIGN(4K) {
843	        *(COMMON)
844	        *(.bss)
845	        _heap_start = .;
846	        . += 64M;
847	        _heap_end   = .;
848	    }
849	}
850	```
851	
852	The heap is inside `.bss` (NOBITS). GRUB does not load NOBITS sections from the ELF file — the bootloader zeroes them. This keeps the on-disk kernel binary small even with a 64 MiB heap reservation.
853
