# NephilimOS — Technical Documentation

Full reference for kernel internals, the JavaScript userland, and the syscall ABI.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Boot Sequence](#1-boot-sequence)
2. [Memory Layout](#2-memory-layout)
3. [Kernel Subsystems](#3-kernel-subsystems)
   - 3.1 [VGA Driver](#31-vga-driver)
   - 3.2 [GDT](#32-gdt)
   - 3.3 [IDT & Exceptions](#33-idt--exceptions)
   - 3.4 [PIC & PIT Timer](#34-pic--pit-timer)
   - 3.5 [PS/2 Keyboard](#35-ps2-keyboard)
   - 3.6 [Process Manager & Scheduler](#36-process-manager--scheduler)
   - 3.7 [PMM & VMM](#37-pmm--vmm)
   - 3.8 [minit — Process Supervisor](#38-minit--process-supervisor)
4. [Syscall Interface](#4-syscall-interface)
5. [Filesystem Stack](#5-filesystem-stack)
   - 5.1 [VFS Layer](#51-vfs-layer)
   - 5.2 [ext2 Implementation](#52-ext2-implementation)
   - 5.3 [RawFS Block Device](#53-rawfs-block-device)
   - 5.4 [initramfs Seed](#54-initramfs-seed)
6. [Heap Allocator](#6-heap-allocator)
7. [JavaScript Userland](#7-javascript-userland)
   - 7.1 [MuJS Integration](#71-mujs-integration)
   - 7.2 [JS Bindings Reference](#72-js-bindings-reference)
   - 7.3 [App Registry & Dispatch](#73-app-registry--dispatch)
   - 7.4 [Cwd & Args Preamble](#74-cwd--args-preamble)
8. [Shell](#8-shell)
   - 8.1 [Built-in Commands](#81-built-in-commands)
   - 8.2 [Line Editing](#82-line-editing)
   - 8.3 [History Ring](#83-history-ring)
9. [JS Apps Reference](#9-js-apps-reference)
   - 9.1 [coreutils](#91-coreutils)
   - 9.2 [calculator](#92-calculator)
   - 9.3 [quill](#93-quill)
10. [Snake Game](#10-snake-game)
11. [Keyboard Key Codes](#11-keyboard-key-codes)
12. [Linker Script](#12-linker-script)

---

## 1. Boot Sequence

`boot/boot.asm` is the Multiboot 1 entry point, loaded at physical address `0x100000` by GRUB.

```
[32-bit protected mode]
_start:
  1. Verify CPUID support (toggle ID flag in EFLAGS)
  2. Verify long-mode support (CPUID leaf 0x80000001, bit 29)
  3. Build identity-mapped page tables:
       PML4[0] → PDPT
       PDPT[0] → PD
       PD[0..511] → 2 MiB huge pages (covers 0..1 GiB)
  4. Load PML4 into CR3
  5. Set CR4.PAE, CR4.OSFXSR, CR4.OSXMMEXCPT
  6. Set EFER.LME via MSR 0xC0000080
  7. Set CR0.PG (enable paging → activates long mode)
  8. Load 64-bit GDT stub
  9. Far-jump to long_mode_start (CS selector 0x08)

[64-bit long mode]
long_mode_start:
  10. Zero BSS segment
  11. Call kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
```

`kernel_main` initialises every subsystem in dependency order before creating the shell process and entering the idle loop.

---

## 2. Memory Layout

### Physical Address Space (at boot)

| Range | Contents |
|-------|----------|
| `0x000000 – 0x0FFFFF` | BIOS / legacy (not used) |
| `0x100000 – ~` | Kernel ELF loaded here by GRUB |
| Above kernel | Free RAM (tracked by PMM) |

### Virtual Address Space (kernel)

| Symbol | Address | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `.text` start | `0x100000` | Kernel code |
| `.data` / `.rodata` | after `.text` | Kernel data |
| `.bss` | after `.data` | Zero-init + heap |
| `_heap_start` | inside `.bss` | Start of allocator arena |
| `_heap_end` | `_heap_start + 64 MiB` | End of allocator arena |

The heap is declared inside `.bss` (NOBITS ELF section) so it occupies no space in the binary on disk.

### Linker-Exported Symbols

```c
extern char _heap_start[];   /* first byte of heap arena */
extern char _heap_end[];     /* one past last byte       */
```

Both used by `stdlib/malloc.c` to initialise the free-list.

---

## 3. Kernel Subsystems

### 3.1 VGA Driver

**File:** `kernel/vga.c`, `include/vga.h`

Mode: 80×25 text, standard VGA MMIO at `0xB8000`. Cell format: `[attribute][char]` (2 bytes).

```c
void vga_init(void);               /* set 80×25, upload font, set cursor style */
void vga_clear(void);              /* fill screen with spaces, colour 0x07     */
void kprint(const char *s);        /* print null-terminated string             */
void kprint_char(char c);          /* print single character                   */
void kprint_uint64(uint64_t n);    /* print decimal integer                    */
void vga_goto(size_t col, size_t row); /* move hardware cursor + update col/row */
```

**Font:** 8×16 glyphs in `boot/font8x16.asm`. Uploaded to VGA plane 2 (0xA0000) at boot. Cell height set to 16 rows (register 0x09 = 0x0F), cursor at scanlines 14–15.

**Colour:** `vga_color` global (`uint8_t`), set via `VGA_COLOR(fg, bg)` macro. JS apps can change it with the `setColor` binding.

---

### 3.2 GDT

**File:** `kernel/gdt.c`, `include/gdt.h`

| Index | Selector | Ring | Type |
|-------|----------|------|------|
| 0 | `0x00` | — | Null |
| 1 | `0x08` | 0 | 64-bit code |
| 2 | `0x10` | 0 | Data |
| 3 | `0x18` | 1 | 64-bit code (userland) |
| 4 | `0x20` | 1 | Data (userland) |
| 5–6 | `0x28` | 0 | 64-bit TSS (2 descriptors) |

`gdt_flush()` (in `boot/gdt_flush.asm`) reloads all segment registers and performs a far-return to update CS.

---

### 3.3 IDT & Exceptions

**File:** `kernel/idt.c`, `boot/isr_stubs.asm`

256 interrupt gates, all installed at `idt_init()`. The stub macro in `isr_stubs.asm` pushes a uniform `int_frame_t` layout (error code + vector number) then calls the C handler.

CPU exceptions (vectors 0–31) print the vector number and faulting RIP to VGA, then `hlt`.

Hardware IRQs (vectors 32–47) are dispatched through the IRQ handler table. IRQ0 drives the timer; IRQ1 drives the keyboard.

`int $0x80` (vector 128) is patched to DPL=1 by `syscall_init()` so Ring-1 code can invoke it without a GPF.

---

### 3.4 PIC & PIT Timer

**File:** `kernel/timer.c`

- 8259A PIC remapped: master → vectors 32–39, slave → vectors 40–47.
- PIT channel 0 programmed to 100 Hz (divisor 11932).
- `timer_ticks` and `timer_seconds` globals incremented by IRQ0 handler.
- `timer_gettime()` fills a `struct timespec` from tick counters.

---

### 3.5 PS/2 Keyboard

**File:** `drivers/keyboard.c`, `include/keyboard.h`

**`keyboard_getchar()`** — blocks until a printable ASCII key, returns `char`.

**`keyboard_getkey()`** — blocks until any key, returns an `int`:
- Printable ASCII → returns the ASCII code directly.
- Extended keys (E0-prefixed scancodes) → returns a `KEY_*` constant (≥ 256).
- Ctrl+letter → returns 1–26.
- F1–F10 → returns `KEY_F1`–`KEY_F10`.

See [Section 10](#10-keyboard-key-codes) for the full `KEY_*` table.

---

### 3.6 Process Manager & Scheduler

**File:** `kernel/process.c`, `include/process.h`, `include/sched.h`

Each process has a `process_t` PCB:

```c
typedef struct process {
    uint64_t   rsp;             /* saved stack pointer (context switch) */
    uint64_t   rip;             /* entry point                          */
    uint32_t   pid;
    proc_state_t state;         /* PROC_UNUSED, READY, RUNNING, BLOCKED, ZOMBIE */
    uint8_t    ring;            /* RING_KERNEL=0, RING_SERVICE=1        */
    uint8_t    priority;
    uint32_t   quantum;
    char       name[PROC_NAME_LEN];
    /* … scheduling metadata … */
} process_t;
```

**Scheduler:** Cooperative. `sched_pick()` selects the next runnable process (SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties). `proc_switch()` performs a context switch via `boot/ctx_switch.asm` (saves/restores callee-saved registers + RSP).

**Key functions:**

```c
process_t *proc_create(void (*entry)(void), const char *name, uint8_t ring, uint32_t quantum, uint32_t priority);
void        proc_wake(process_t *p);
void        proc_switch(process_t *p);
process_t  *proc_by_pid(uint32_t pid);   /* returns NULL if not found */
void        proc_destroy(process_t *p);
```

---

### 3.7 PMM & VMM

**File:** `kernel/pmm.c`, `kernel/vmm.c`

**PMM** tracks 4K physical frames using a bitmap derived from the Multiboot memory map. `pmm_alloc_frame()` returns a free physical page; `pmm_free_frame()` returns it to the pool.

**VMM** initialises 4-level paging and records the initial CR3. Used as the foundation for future per-process address spaces.

---

### 3.8 minit — Process Supervisor

**File:** `kernel/minit.c`, `include/minit.h`

minit is NephilimOS's lightweight init. It is not a separate process — it runs inside the kernel idle loop, adding zero process overhead.

**Configuration:**

```c
#define MINIT_MAX_WATCH  16   /* max supervised processes */
#define MINIT_MAX_RETRY   3   /* restart attempts before giving up */
```

**Watch table entry:**

```c
typedef struct {
    void       (*entry)(void);      /* entry function used to re-spawn */
    char         name[PROC_NAME_LEN];
    uint32_t     pid;               /* PID of currently-running child  */
    uint8_t      retries;           /* restart attempts so far         */
    uint8_t      active;            /* 1 = slot in use                 */
} watch_t;
```

**API:**

```c
void minit_init(void);
int  minit_watch(void (*entry)(void), const char *name, process_t *p);
void minit_tick(void);   /* call from idle loop on each hlt iteration */
```

**Behaviour per tick:**
1. For each active slot, call `proc_by_pid(w->pid)`.
2. If the process is alive (`state != PROC_UNUSED && state != PROC_ZOMBIE`), skip.
3. If dead and `retries < MINIT_MAX_RETRY`: increment retries, log the attempt, call `proc_create` + `proc_wake`, update `w->pid`.
4. If `retries >= MINIT_MAX_RETRY`: log "giving up", clear the slot.

Currently the shell (`shell_main`) is the only watched process.

---

## 4. Syscall Interface

**File:** `kernel/syscall.c`, `include/syscall.h`

Invoked via `int $0x80`. Convention:

| Register | Role |
|----------|------|
| `rax` | Syscall number (in); return value (out) |
| `rdi` | Argument 1 |
| `rsi` | Argument 2 |
| `rdx` | Argument 3 |

Inline helpers in `include/syscall.h`:

```c
int64_t syscall0(uint64_t num);
int64_t syscall1(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1);
int64_t syscall2(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1, uint64_t a2);
int64_t syscall3(uint64_t num, uint64_t a1, uint64_t a2, uint64_t a3);
```

**Full syscall table:**

| # | Name | `rdi` | `rsi` | `rdx` | Returns |
|---|------|-------|-------|-------|---------|
| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | str ptr | len (0=null-term) | — | 0 |
| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | — | — | 0 |
| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | — | — | 0 |
| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | phys addr | — | — | uint64 |
| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | buf ptr | buf size | — | 0 |
| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | — | — | — |
| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | — | — | — |
| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | — | — | 0 |
| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | — | — | pid |
| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | entry fn | name ptr | priority | pid |
| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | — | — | child pid / 0 |
| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | exit code | — | — | — |
| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | pid | — | — | 0 / -1 |
| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | pid | — | — | 0 |
| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | ticks | — | — | 0 |
| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | pid | buf ptr | size | 0 / -1 |
| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | — | — | ASCII char |
| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | source ptr | — | — | 0 |
| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | — | — | seconds |
| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | timespec ptr | clock id | — | 0 |
| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | path | flags | mode | fd / -1 |
| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | fd | — | — | 0 / -1 |
| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | fd | buf ptr | len | bytes / -1 |
| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | fd | buf ptr | len | bytes / -1 |
| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | fd | offset | whence | new offset |
| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | path | stat ptr | — | 0 / -1 |
| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | fd | stat ptr | — | 0 / -1 |
| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | fd | index | dirent ptr | 0 / -1 |
| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | path | mode | — | 0 / -1 |
| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | path | — | — | 0 / -1 |
| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | path | mode | — | fd / -1 |
| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | — | — | `KEY_*` or ASCII |
| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | path | — | — | 0 / -1 |
| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | buf ptr | size | — | 0 |
| 34 | `SYS_PS` | snap buf ptr | max_entries | — | count of live procs |
| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | — | — | `KEY_*`/ASCII or -1 if none |

**Return codes (negative):**

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `ESYS_BADNUM` (-1) | Unknown syscall number |
| `ESYS_BADARG` (-2) | Invalid argument |
| `ESYS_NOMEM` (-3) | Process table full |
| `ESYS_NOPROC` (-4) | No such PID |
| `ESYS_PERM` (-5) | Privilege violation |

---

## 5. Filesystem Stack

```
JS app / shell
     │  (path string)
     ▼
  VFS layer  (vfs.c)
     │  (vfs_driver_t interface)
     ▼
  RawFS  (rawfs.c)
     │  (block read/write)
     ▼
  ext2  (ext2.c)
     │  (in-memory byte array)
     ▼
  nephilim_disk_image[]  (declared in rawfs.c)
```

### 5.1 VFS Layer

**File:** `fs/vfs.c`, `include/fs.h`

Single mount table entry for `/`. Translates POSIX-style paths to driver calls.

```c
int vfs_mount(const char *path, vfs_driver_t *drv, void *ctx, size_t size);
int vfs_open(const char *path, int flags);
int vfs_close(int fd);
ssize_t vfs_read(int fd, void *buf, size_t len);
ssize_t vfs_write(int fd, const void *buf, size_t len);
int vfs_stat(const char *path, struct stat *st);
int vfs_readdir(int fd, int index, struct dirent *de);
int vfs_mkdir(const char *path, int mode);
int vfs_unlink(const char *path);
int vfs_creat(const char *path, int mode);
```

### 5.2 ext2 Implementation

**File:** `fs/ext2.c`

Block size: 1024 bytes. Single block group layout (the entire disk is one group).

**Block layout within the group:**

| Block | Contents |
|-------|----------|
| 0 | (unused — Multiboot convention) |
| 1 | Superblock |
| 2 | Block Group Descriptor (BGD) |
| 3 | Block bitmap |
| 4 | Inode bitmap |
| 5 … 5+N-1 | Inode table (N = ceil(max_inodes × 128 / 1024)) |
| 5+N … end | Data blocks |

The BGD is written at block 2, bitmaps at blocks 3 and 4. This avoids the overlap that occurred when bitmaps were placed at `gbase+1` (same block as the BGD table).

Overhead for the allocator: `5 + inode_table_blocks`.

### 5.3 RawFS Block Device

**File:** `fs/rawfs.c`, `include/rawfs.h`

`nephilim_disk_image[]` is a static byte array (`RAWFS_IMAGE_SIZE` bytes). `nephilim_disk` wraps it with a pointer and size. `rawfs_driver` is the `vfs_driver_t` that delegates to ext2.

### 5.4 initramfs Seed

**File:** `kernel/seed.c`

Called once at boot after the filesystem is mounted. Writes JS app source files to `/bin/`:

| Path | Source |
|------|--------|
| `/bin/ls` | `userland/coreutils/ls.js` |
| `/bin/cat` | `userland/coreutils/cat.js` |
| `/bin/touch` | `userland/coreutils/touch.js` |
| `/bin/mkdir` | `userland/coreutils/mkdir.js` |
| `/bin/rm` | `userland/coreutils/rm.js` |
| `/bin/stat` | `userland/coreutils/stat.js` |
| `/bin/write` | `userland/coreutils/write.js` |
| `/bin/wc` | `userland/coreutils/wc.js` |
| `/bin/uname` | `userland/coreutils/uname.js` |
| `/bin/cd` | `userland/coreutils/cd.js` |
| `/bin/calculator` | `userland/calculator/main.js` |
| `/bin/quill` | `userland/quill/main.js` |
| `/bin/ps` | `userland/coreutils/ps.js` |
| `/bin/cp` | `userland/coreutils/cp.js` |
| `/bin/mv` | `userland/coreutils/mv.js` |
| `/bin/grep` | `userland/coreutils/grep.js` |
| `/bin/sed` | `userland/coreutils/sed.js` |

JS sources are converted to C string literals at build time by `tools/js2ch.py` and `#include`d as `.js.h` headers.

---

## 5.5 libtranslateunix — POSIX Shim

**File:** `lib/libtranslateunix.c`, `include/libtranslateunix.h`

Translates standard POSIX calls into VFS operations. Intended for porting Unix software without modifying its source. All functions are prefixed `unix_*`; macro aliases in the header let callers use the bare POSIX names.

**File I/O:**

| Function | POSIX equivalent | Notes |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| `unix_open(path, flags, mode)` | `open()` | Maps O_* flags to vfs_open |
| `unix_close(fd)` | `close()` | |
| `unix_read(fd, buf, len)` | `read()` | Returns bytes read |
| `unix_write(fd, buf, len)` | `write()` | |
| `unix_lseek(fd, offset, whence)` | `lseek()` | |
| `unix_stat(path, st)` | `stat()` | Fills `struct stat` |
| `unix_fstat(fd, st)` | `fstat()` | |
| `unix_unlink(path)` | `unlink()` | |
| `unix_mkdir(path, mode)` | `mkdir()` | |
| `unix_rmdir(path)` | `rmdir()` | Delegates to vfs_unlink |
| `unix_creat(path, mode)` | `creat()` | |
| `unix_rename(old, new)` | `rename()` | Copy+delete; no atomic dirent move |
| `unix_access(path, mode)` | `access()` | Checks existence only |
| `unix_truncate(path, len)` | `truncate()` | Open with O_TRUNC then close |
| `unix_ftruncate(fd, len)` | `ftruncate()` | Returns ENOTSUPP (not yet in VFS) |
| `unix_chmod(path, mode)` | `chmod()` | No-op stub (permissions not enforced) |
| `unix_fchmod(fd, mode)` | `fchmod()` | No-op stub |

**Working directory:**

| Function | POSIX equivalent | Notes |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| `unix_chdir(path)` | `chdir()` | Validates via VFS, updates `kernel_cwd` |
| `unix_getcwd(buf, size)` | `getcwd()` | Copies `kernel_cwd` to caller buffer |
| `unix_realpath(path, resolved)` | `realpath()` | Joins relative paths to `kernel_cwd`; no symlink resolution |

**Directory iteration:**

| Function | POSIX equivalent |
|----------|-----------------|
| `unix_opendir(path)` | `opendir()` |
| `unix_closedir(dir)` | `closedir()` |
| `unix_readdir(dir)` | `readdir()` — returns `struct dirent *` |
| `unix_rewinddir(dir)` | `rewinddir()` |

---

## 6. Heap Allocator

**File:** `stdlib/malloc.c`

First-fit free-list allocator with immediate coalescing.

**Block header (16 bytes):**

```c
typedef struct block {
    size_t        size;    /* usable payload bytes (not including header) */
    uint32_t      free;    /* 1 = free, 0 = allocated                     */
    uint32_t      _pad;
    struct block *next;    /* next block in the list (NULL = end)          */
} block_t;
```

**`malloc(n)`:** Walks the free list, finds the first block where `b->size >= n`. If the remainder after splitting is ≥ 32 bytes (header + 16 bytes minimum payload), splits the block. Otherwise allocates the whole block.

**`free(ptr)`:** Marks the block free, then calls `coalesce()` which merges adjacent free blocks in a single linear pass.

**`realloc(ptr, 0)`:** Equivalent to `free(ptr)`.  
**`realloc(ptr, n)`:** If the existing block is large enough, returns `ptr` unchanged. Otherwise `malloc` + `memcpy` + `free`.

**Arena:** `_heap_start` to `_heap_end` (64 MiB). Initialised by `arena_init()` which creates a single free block covering the whole range. `arena_init()` is called lazily on the first `malloc`.

MuJS compatibility: MuJS GC calls `realloc(ptr, 0)` to release memory. The free-list allocator handles this correctly; the former bump allocator did not, causing OOM after ~8 editor keystrokes.

---

## 7. JavaScript Userland

### 7.1 MuJS Integration

**File:** `kernel/js_runtime.c`, `external/include/mujs/`

MuJS is compiled freestanding against `mujs_port.h` which maps `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` to the kernel allocator and stubs out POSIX I/O. The interpreter runs in Ring 0 kernel context during `SYS_JS_EXEC`.

`js_runtime_run(const char *source)` creates a fresh `js_State`, registers all bindings, injects the signal prelude, evaluates the app source string, and destroys the state. Each app invocation is isolated.

### 7.1.1 Signal Library

**File:** `userland/signal.js` — injected automatically into every JS state before the app source runs. No import needed.

A minimal reactive state library. All four primitives are available as globals.

**`signal(initialValue)`**

```javascript
var count = signal(0);
count.get();          // read current value
count.peek();         // read without being tracked (safe inside effects)
count.set(n);         // write; notifies subscribers (deferred during batch)
count.sub(fn);        // subscribe: fn(newValue, oldValue) called on change
```

**`computed(fn)`**

Read-only signal whose value is derived from other signals. Recomputes lazily on `.get()` when marked stale.

```javascript
var label = computed(function() {
    return dirty.get() ? '[modified]' : '';
});
label.get();    // recomputes if dirty changed since last call
label.peek();   // returns cached value without recompute
```

**`effect(signals, fn)`**

Runs `fn` immediately, then re-runs it whenever any signal in the array changes.

```javascript
effect([cx, cy], function() {
    drawStatusBar();
});
```

**`batch(fn)`**

Defers all subscriber notifications until `fn` returns. Each subscriber fires at most once per batch, with the final value. Essential when multiple signals change together.

```javascript
batch(function() {
    cx.set(0);
    cy.set(cy.get() - 1);
    dirty.set(true);
});
// all three subscribers fire here, each exactly once
```

Batches nest correctly — inner batches join the outer one.

### 7.2 JS Bindings Reference

All functions are available globally inside any JS app.

**Output:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `print(s)` | Print string without newline |
| `println(s)` | Print string + newline |

**Input:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `readline()` | Block until Enter; return line string |
| `getChar()` | Block until key; return int (`KEY_*` or ASCII code) |
| `getKeyNonBlock()` | Return key code immediately; -1 if no key pending |
| `getTicks()` | Return PIT tick counter (100 Hz, monotonically increasing) |

**Filesystem:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `fsOpen(path, mode)` | Open file; mode `"r"` or `"w"`; returns fd (int) |
| `fsClose(fd)` | Close fd |
| `fsRead(fd, len)` | Read up to len bytes; returns string or null |
| `fsWrite(fd, s)` | Write string to fd |
| `fsReaddir(fd, index)` | Return `{name, ino}` object or null |
| `fsStat(path)` | Return `{size, mode, ino}` or null |
| `fsMkdir(path)` | Create directory |
| `fsUnlink(path)` | Remove file |
| `fsCreat(path)` | Create file; returns fd |

**Terminal:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `clearScreen()` | Clear VGA screen |
| `setCursor(col, row)` | Move hardware cursor to (col, row) |
| `setColor(fg, bg)` | Set VGA colour (0–15 each) |
| `printAt(col, row, s)` | Print string at specific screen position |
| `screenWidth()` | Returns 80 |
| `screenHeight()` | Returns 25 |

**Path / CWD:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `chdir(path)` | Change kernel cwd; returns 0 or -1 |
| `getcwd()` | Returns current working directory string |

**Process info:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `getProcessList()` | Returns array of `{pid, name, state, ring, priority}` objects for all live processes |

State values: 0=unused, 1=embryo, 2=ready, 3=running, 4=blocked, 5=zombie. Ring values: 0=kernel, 1=service.

### 7.3 App Registry & Dispatch

**File:** `userland/apps.c`, `include/apps.h`

```c
typedef struct {
    const char *name;      /* command typed at the shell */
    const char *binname;   /* filename under /bin */
    const char *fallback;  /* embedded JS source (if /bin read fails) */
} app_t;
```

`app_run(const char *cmdline, const char *cwd)` searches `apps[]` for the command, reads `/bin/<binname>` via VFS, prepends the cwd+args preamble, then calls `SYS_JS_EXEC`. If the file cannot be read (e.g. during early boot), it falls back to the embedded string literal. Returns 1 if the app was found, 0 if not.

Aliases are provided for convenience: `calc` → `calculator`, `chdir` → `cd`.

### 7.4 Cwd & Args Preamble

Before the app source is executed, `build_args_preamble()` prepends:

```javascript
var __cwd="<current directory>";
var __args=["arg1","arg2",...];
```

Apps access these globals:

```javascript
var cwd  = typeof __cwd  !== 'undefined' ? __cwd  : '/';
var args = typeof __args !== 'undefined' ? __args : [];
```

`__cwd` is the shell's current directory (synced from `kernel_cwd` via `SYS_GETCWD`).  
`__args` contains the command-line tokens after the command name itself.

---

## 8. Shell

**File:** `userland/shell/main.c`

Runs as a Ring-1 process (`RING_SERVICE`). All I/O goes through syscalls.

### 8.1 Built-in Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `help` | Show available commands |
| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
| `echo <text>` | Print text |
| `pwd` | Print working directory |
| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
| `pid` | Show current process PID |
| `reboot` | Reboot the system |
| `shutdown` | Power off |
| `snake` | Native C snake game |

### 8.2 Line Editing

`sh_readline()` maintains a `line[]` buffer and a `cursor` position. Supported operations:

| Key | Behaviour |
|-----|-----------|
| Printable ASCII | Insert at cursor, shift right |
| `Backspace` | Delete left of cursor, shift left |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor one position |
| `Home` / `End` | Jump to beginning / end |
| `Enter` | Finalise line |
| `Ctrl+C` | Clear line, return empty |
| `Up` / `Down` | History navigation (see below) |

Mid-line insert and delete are both supported with correct terminal re-rendering.

### 8.3 History Ring

32-entry circular buffer. Consecutive duplicates are suppressed.

```
hist[32][256]    — ring buffer of stored lines
hist_head        — index where next entry will be written
hist_count       — number of valid entries (capped at 32)
```

`hist_push(line)` — adds a non-empty, non-duplicate line.  
`hist_get(offset)` — `offset=1` = most recent, `offset=2` = previous, etc.

While browsing history with Up, the current live input is saved to `saved[]` and restored on Down back to position 0.

---

## 9. JS Apps Reference

### 9.1 coreutils

| App | Usage | Notes |
|-----|-------|-------|
| `ls [path]` | List directory | Defaults to cwd; shows files and directories |
| `cat <file>` | Print file | Reads and prints entire file |
| `touch <file>` | Create empty file | Uses `fsCreat` |
| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory | Uses `fsMkdir` |
| `rm <file>` | Remove file | Uses `fsUnlink` |
| `stat <file>` | Metadata | Shows size, mode, inode |
| `write <file> <text>` | Write to file | Overwrites; `write myfile hello world` |
| `wc <file>` | Count | Reports lines, words, bytes |
| `uname` | System info | CPU model + kernel name |
| `cd [path]` | Change directory | Expands `~` to `/home`; resolves `.` and `..`; calls `chdir()` |
| `ps` | Process list | Shows PID, ring, state, name for all live processes |
| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file | If dst is a directory, copies into it |
| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move/rename file | Copy + unlink; directory dest supported |
| `grep <pat> <file>` | Search lines | Supports `.` `*` `^` `$` `[abc]` `[^abc]`; multi-file prints `file:line` |
| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute | Replaces first (or all with `g`) occurrence of pattern per line; prints result |

**`cd` path resolution:**
1. No argument → `/home`
2. `~` prefix → replaced with `/home`
3. Absolute path → used directly
4. Relative path → joined to `getcwd()`, then `.`/`..` normalised

### 9.2 calculator

Launched as `calculator`. Evaluates JavaScript expressions using `eval()` inside MuJS. Prompts for input in a loop; `quit` or `exit` to leave.

### 9.3 quill

Full-screen text editor. Launched as `quill [filename]`.

**Layout (80×25):**

| Row | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| 0 | Title bar (filename, `[modified]` flag) |
| 1–23 | Text editing area (23 visible lines) |
| 24 | Status bar (cursor position, key hints) |

**Key bindings:**

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Up` / `Down` | Move cursor row |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor column |
| `Home` | Jump to start of line |
| `End` | Jump to end of line |
| `Backspace` | Delete character left |
| `Del` (KEY_DEL) | Delete character under cursor |
| `Enter` | Insert newline / split line |
| `Ctrl+S` | Save file |
| `Ctrl+Q` | Quit (prompts if modified) |
| `Ctrl+X` | Force quit without saving |

Rows beyond the end of the file are shown as `~` (nano/vim convention). Vertical scrolling is supported when the file exceeds 23 lines.

If launched without a filename, operates as a scratch buffer (prompts for filename on save).

---

## 10. Keyboard Key Codes

Defined in `include/keyboard.h`. Returned by `keyboard_getkey()` and `SYS_GETKEY` / JS `getChar()`.

| Constant | Value | Key |
|----------|-------|-----|
| `KEY_UP` | 0x100 | Up arrow |
| `KEY_DOWN` | 0x101 | Down arrow |
| `KEY_LEFT` | 0x102 | Left arrow |
| `KEY_RIGHT` | 0x103 | Right arrow |
| `KEY_HOME` | 0x104 | Home |
| `KEY_END` | 0x105 | End |
| `KEY_PGUP` | 0x106 | Page Up |
| `KEY_PGDN` | 0x107 | Page Down |
| `KEY_DEL` | 0x108 | Delete |
| `KEY_F1` | 0x110 | F1 |
| `KEY_F2` | 0x111 | F2 |
| … | … | … |
| `KEY_F10` | 0x119 | F10 |

ASCII codes (0–127) are returned directly. Ctrl+letter returns 1–26 (`Ctrl+A` = 1, `Ctrl+C` = 3, `Ctrl+S` = 19, `Ctrl+Q` = 17, `Ctrl+X` = 24).

---

## 11. Linker Script

**File:** `linker.ld`

```
ENTRY(_start)
OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-x86-64)
OUTPUT_ARCH(i386:x86-64)

SECTIONS {
    . = 1M;          /* load address: 1 MiB */

    .text   : { *(.text*) }
    .rodata : { *(.rodata*) }
    .data   : { *(.data*)  }

    .bss : ALIGN(4K) {
        *(COMMON)
        *(.bss)
        _heap_start = .;
        . += 64M;
        _heap_end   = .;
    }
}
```

The heap is inside `.bss` (NOBITS). GRUB does not load NOBITS sections from the ELF file — the bootloader zeroes them. This keeps the on-disk kernel binary small even with a 64 MiB heap reservation.

content.text: Now I have the full picture. I'll rewrite both files with all the new additions: MB2/UEFI boot, GOP framebuffer, dual boot entries, 4 GiB identity map, VoiceBox audio driver, display syscalls, snake dual-renderer + colour schemes, improved shutdown/reboot.

input.content:
# NephilimOS

A MINIX-inspired, bare-metal x86_64 operating system built from scratch — with a JavaScript userland, a C snake game, GOP framebuffer graphics, and a multi-backend audio driver.

---

## What It Is

NephilimOS is a 64-bit hobby kernel that boots via GRUB Multiboot 2 (UEFI + GOP framebuffer) with a Multiboot 1 legacy fallback, runs a cooperative scheduler, mounts an ext2 filesystem from a RAM disk, and executes user-facing apps written in JavaScript via an embedded MuJS interpreter. All userland runs at Ring 1 through an `int $0x80` syscall interface. A native C snake game renders in full pixel graphics on the GOP path or falls back to ASCII on VGA text mode.

> **Note:** The Multiboot 1 / BIOS legacy boot path is present and selectable from the GRUB menu but is currently non-functional. UEFI + Multiboot 2 is the supported boot path.

---

## Feature Summary

### Boot & Hardware
- **Multiboot 2** (primary) — UEFI boot via GRUB with GOP framebuffer negotiation
- **Multiboot 1** (legacy fallback) — BIOS VGA text mode (menu entry present; fix pending)
- Dual-header boot binary — single kernel image supports both bootloaders
- 32-bit protected mode → 64-bit long mode transition in assembly
- Identity-mapped first **4 GiB** via 2 MiB huge pages (PML4 → PDPT → 4× PD) — covers GOP framebuffer at 0x80000000
- **GOP framebuffer driver** (`drivers/gop.c`) — pixel renderer with 8×16 font blitting, palette-to-RGB mapping, QEMU virtio-gpu quirk handling
- VGA text-mode driver (80×25, colour, custom 8×16 bitmap font) — fallback when GOP unavailable
- PS/2 keyboard driver — scancode set 1, shift/ctrl modifiers, extended keys
- PIT timer at 100 Hz (IRQ0) — monotonic tick + second counter
- 8259 PIC remapped to vectors 32–47
- **VoiceBox audio driver** — multi-backend audio with boot confirmation chime

### Kernel Subsystems
- GDT with Ring 0 / Ring 1 descriptors and a 64-bit TSS
- IDT with 256 gates; CPU exceptions print vector + RIP and halt
- `int 0x80` syscall interface (Ring 0 and Ring 1 callable)
- Cooperative multi-process scheduler (FCFS + SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties)
- `proc_create`, `proc_fork`, `proc_destroy`, `proc_switch` process lifecycle
- **minit** — miniature init: lightweight process supervisor that restarts watched processes up to 3 times on death. Supervises the shell and VoiceBox.

### Memory
- First-fit free-list heap allocator (`malloc` / `free` / `realloc`) with block coalescing
- 64 MB heap in `.bss` (NOBITS — not stored in the ELF binary)
- PMM physical frame allocator (tracks free 4K pages from multiboot memory map; supports both MB1 and MB2 memory maps)
- VMM 4-level paging infrastructure

### Filesystem
- VFS mount layer with path walk and fd table
- ext2 filesystem (block groups, inodes, directory entries, bitmaps)
- RawFS in-memory block device as ext2 backing store
- File syscalls: open / close / read / write / seek / stat / fstat / readdir / mkdir / unlink / creat
- Standard directory seeding at boot: `/tmp`, `/home`, `/bin`, `/etc`

### JavaScript Userland
- MuJS interpreter embedded in the kernel (freestanding port)
- Ring-1 shell dispatches named apps via `SYS_JS_EXEC`
- Apps embedded as C string literals, executed in kernel MuJS context
- JS filesystem bindings: `fsOpen`, `fsRead`, `fsWrite`, `fsClose`, `fsReaddir`, `fsStat`, `fsMkdir`, `fsUnlink`, `fsCreat`
- JS terminal bindings: `print`, `println`, `readline`, `getChar`, `setCursor`, `setColor`, `screenWidth`, `screenHeight`, `clearScreen`, `printAt`
- JS path bindings: `chdir`, `getcwd`
- `__cwd` and `__args` preamble injected into every app execution

### Native C Snake Game
- Dual renderer: **GOP path** (MB2/UEFI) renders full pixel graphics; **VGA path** (MB1/BIOS) uses ASCII characters
- GOP path: 20×20 px cells, large board, coloured rectangles, pixel font sidebar
- Two switchable colour schemes (`C` key): **Green snake / Red fruit** and **Blue snake / Purple fruit**
- VGA path: 50×20 play area, blue snake (`@`/`o`), magenta apple (`*`)
- Right-panel HUD showing score and best score on both paths
- Highscore persisted to `/tmp/snake_hi` across sessions
- Speed increases dynamically with score

### Audio — VoiceBox
- **SB16** (primary) — Sound Blaster 16 probe at I/O bases 0x220 / 0x240 / 0x260 / 0x280
- **AC97** (secondary) — Intel AC'97 codec probe via PCI scan (ICH, ICH0, ICH2, ICH3, ICH4, AMD)
- **ISA speaker** (fallback) — PIT channel 2 + port 0x61; always available
- **HDA** (experimental) — Intel HD Audio probe via PCI scan; detected and logged, no playback yet
- Boot confirmation chime plays once after shell initialisation
- `SYS_BEEP(freq_hz, duration_ms)` syscall exposed to userland
- Supervised by minit — restarted on crash

### Standard Library
- Freestanding libc: `string`, `stdlib`, `stdio` (printf/snprintf), `math`, `setjmp`, `time`
- POSIX-compatible time: `gmtime_r`, `mktime`, `strftime`, `clock_gettime`

---

## Project Structure

```
NephilimOS/
├── boot/
│   ├── boot.asm          # Dual MB2+MB1 headers; 4 GiB page tables; long-mode transition
│   ├── gdt_flush.asm     # Reload segment registers + far-return to new CS
│   ├── isr_stubs.asm     # 256 ISR stubs; uniform int_frame_t layout
│   ├── ctx_switch.asm    # Low-level context switch (callee-saved regs)
│   └── font8x16.asm      # 8×16 VGA/GOP bitmap font data
├── kernel/
│   ├── kernel.c          # kernel_main; MB2/MB1 detection; subsystem init; minit; idle loop
│   ├── vga.c             # Dual-path display driver: VGA text + GOP pixel rendering
│   ├── gop.c             # GOP framebuffer driver; glyph blitter; fill rect; palette convert
│   ├── detect.c          # CPUID CPU detection; RAM from multiboot info
│   ├── gdt.c             # GDT + TSS setup
│   ├── idt.c             # IDT init; int_handler; IRQ dispatch; syscall vector
│   ├── timer.c           # PIC remap; PIT 100 Hz; timer_irq_handler; timer_gettime
│   ├── syscall.c         # syscall_dispatch; all sys_* implementations; kernel_cwd
│   ├── process.c         # PCB; scheduler; proc_create/fork/destroy/switch
│   ├── pmm.c             # Physical memory manager (MB1 + MB2 init paths)
│   ├── vmm.c             # Virtual memory manager (4-level paging)
│   ├── js_runtime.c      # MuJS engine init; all JS bindings
│   ├── seed.c            # initramfs: seeds /bin/* from embedded JS source
│   └── minit.c           # miniature init — process supervisor
├── drivers/
│   ├── keyboard.c        # PS/2 keyboard; scancode→KEY_*; keyboard_getkey()
│   ├── serial.c          # Serial port output (QEMU debug console)
│   └── voicebox.c        # VoiceBox audio driver: SB16 / AC97 / ISA speaker / HDA
├── fs/
│   ├── ext2.c            # ext2 implementation (block groups, inodes, bitmaps)
│   ├── rawfs.c           # Raw in-memory block device + ext2 mount wrapper
│   └── vfs.c             # VFS mount table + path walk + fd table
├── lib/
│   └── libtranslateunix.c # Unix-like path helpers for VFS
├── stdlib/
│   ├── string.c          # memset/cpy/move/cmp, strlen, str*
│   ├── stdlib.c          # abs, atoi, itoa, utoa
│   ├── malloc.c          # First-fit free-list allocator with coalescing
│   ├── math.c            # Freestanding math (sin, cos, sqrt, log, exp, …)
│   ├── printf.c          # Freestanding printf / snprintf / vsnprintf
│   ├── setjmp.asm        # setjmp / longjmp
│   └── time.c            # POSIX time: gmtime_r, mktime, strftime, format_time
├── userland/
│   ├── shell/
│   │   └── main.c        # Ring-1 shell; line editing; history; app dispatch
│   ├── snake/
│   │   └── main.c        # Dual-renderer snake: GOP pixel + VGA ASCII; colour schemes
│   ├── apps.c            # JS app registry; cwd+args preamble injection
│   ├── calculator/
│   │   └── main.js       # Expression evaluator (MuJS)
│   ├── coreutils/
│   │   ├── ls.js         # List directory
│   │   ├── cat.js        # Print file contents
│   │   ├── touch.js      # Create empty file
│   │   ├── mkdir.js      # Create directory
│   │   ├── rm.js         # Remove file
│   │   ├── stat.js       # Show file metadata
│   │   ├── write.js      # Write text to file
│   │   ├── wc.js         # Count lines/words/bytes
│   │   ├── uname.js      # Show system information
│   │   ├── cd.js         # Change directory (path normalisation + chdir binding)
│   │   ├── ps.js         # List running processes
│   │   ├── cp.js         # Copy file
│   │   ├── mv.js         # Move / rename file
│   │   ├── grep.js       # Search lines matching pattern
│   │   └── sed.js        # Substitute pattern in file output
│   └── quill/
│       └── main.js       # Full-screen text editor (nano-like)
├── include/              # All public headers
│   ├── voicebox.h        # VoiceBox API + backend enum
│   ├── gop.h             # GOP framebuffer info + drawing API
│   └── syscall.h         # Syscall numbers + inline helpers + fb_info_t
├── external/
│   └── include/mujs/     # MuJS JS engine (vendored); mujs_port.h shims libc
├── grub/
│   └── grub.cfg          # GRUB menu: MB2/UEFI entry + MB1/BIOS legacy entry
├── linker.ld             # Kernel linker script; .mb2hdr section; loads at 1 MiB
├── tools/
│   └── js2ch.py          # Converts .js source to C string-literal .js.h header
└── Makefile
```

---

## Prerequisites

All build steps run inside **WSL2**. The toolchain uses **Clang/LLVM** — no GCC cross-compiler required.

### openSUSE (Tumbleweed / Leap)

```bash
sudo zypper install clang llvm lld nasm
sudo zypper install grub2-x86_64-efi xorriso
sudo zypper install qemu-x86 qemu-ovmf-x86_64
```

### Ubuntu / Debian

```bash
sudo apt install clang lld nasm grub-pc-bin grub-efi-amd64-bin grub-common xorriso qemu-system-x86 ovmf
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
sudo pacman -S clang lld nasm grub xorriso qemu-system-x86 edk2-ovmf
```

---

## Build

```bash
# Compile and link the kernel ELF
make

# Build a bootable ISO image
make iso

# Clean all build artifacts
make clean
```

Outputs:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `nephilim.kernel` | ELF64 kernel binary |
| `nephilim.iso` | Bootable ISO (GRUB + kernel) |

---

## Run

```bash
# UEFI / Multiboot 2 (recommended — GOP framebuffer)
make run-uefi

# BIOS / Multiboot 1 (legacy — VGA text mode, currently broken)
make run

# With serial debug output on host terminal
qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
  -drive if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on,file=/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-4m-code.bin \
  -drive if=pflash,format=raw,file=/tmp/nephilim-ovmf-vars.bin
```

---

## Boot Flow

```
GRUB menu
  ├─ "NephilimOS (Multiboot 2 / UEFI)"   ← primary
  │     insmod all_video
  │     set gfxpayload=keep
  │     multiboot2 /boot/nephilim.kernel
  │
  └─ "NephilimOS (Multiboot 1 / BIOS legacy)"   ← fallback (fix pending)
        multiboot /boot/nephilim.kernel

_start  [32-bit protected mode]
  ├─ Check CPUID / long-mode support
  ├─ Build identity-mapped page tables (4 GiB: PML4→PDPT→p2_table_0..3)
  ├─ Enable PAE + EFER.LME, enable paging
  ├─ Load 64-bit GDT, far-jump to long mode
  └─ kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
       ├─ serial_init
       ├─ MB2: parse framebuffer tag → gop_init → vga_init_gop
       │  MB1: vga_init (VGA text mode)
       ├─ vga_clear
       ├─ gdt_init / idt_init / timer_init / sti
       ├─ syscall_init / keyboard_init / process_init
       ├─ detect_cpu / pmm_init_mb2 or pmm_init_mb1 / vmm_init
       ├─ vfs_init / rawfs+ext2 mount / initramfs_seed
       ├─ minit_init
       ├─ proc_create(shell_main)  → proc_wake → minit_watch
       ├─ proc_create(voicebox_main) → proc_wake → minit_watch
       └─ sched_pick → proc_switch
            idle loop: minit_tick() + hlt

voicebox_main (Ring-1 process, supervised by minit):
  ├─ vb_init: probe SB16 → AC97 → HDA → ISA speaker
  ├─ Play single 1000 Hz / 120 ms confirmation chime
  └─ hlt forever
```

---

## Shell

The Ring-1 shell (`userland/shell/main.c`) provides full line editing and history navigation.

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `help` | Show available commands |
| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
| `echo <text>` | Print text |
| `pwd` | Print working directory |
| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
| `pid` | Show current process PID |
| `reboot` | Reboot (ACPI reset reg 0xCF9, then PS/2 fallback) |
| `shutdown` | Power off (ACPI PM1a 0x604, PIIX4 0xB004, microvm 0x4004) |
| `snake` | Native C snake game |

### JS Coreutils

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `cd [path]` | Change directory (supports `~`, `.`, `..`) |
| `ls [path]` | List directory contents |
| `cat <file>` | Print file contents |
| `touch <file>` | Create empty file |
| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory |
| `rm <file>` | Remove file |
| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file |
| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move / rename file |
| `stat <file>` | Show file metadata |
| `write <file> <text>` | Write text to file |
| `wc <file>` | Count lines / words / bytes |
| `grep <pat> <file>` | Print lines matching pattern |
| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute pattern in file output |
| `uname` | Show system information |
| `ps` | List running processes |
| `calculator` | Expression evaluator |
| `quill [file]` | Full-screen text editor |

### Key Bindings

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Up` / `Down` | Navigate history |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor within line |
| `Home` / `End` | Jump to start / end of line |
| `Backspace` | Delete character left of cursor |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |

---

## Snake Game

Type `snake` at the shell prompt to launch.

### GOP Path (UEFI / MB2)

Full pixel rendering on the GOP framebuffer. Cell size 20×20 px. Board sized to ~85% of screen height. Right sidebar shows score, controls, and current colour scheme.

| Control | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `W` / `↑` | Move up |
| `S` / `↓` | Move down |
| `A` / `←` | Move left |
| `D` / `→` | Move right |
| `P` | Pause / resume |
| `C` | Cycle colour scheme |
| `Q` | Quit |

**Colour schemes:**

| Scheme | Snake | Fruit |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Green / Red (default) | Bright green head, green body | Red |
| Blue / Purple | Cyan-blue head, blue body | Purple |

### VGA Path (BIOS / MB1)

ASCII character grid, 50×20 play area. Blue snake (`@` head, `o` body), magenta apple (`*`).

The highscore is saved as a plain decimal integer to `/tmp/snake_hi` after each game. Speed increases dynamically with score.

---

## VoiceBox Audio Driver

VoiceBox is a kernel audio driver launched as a supervised Ring-1 process by minit.

### Backend Priority

| Priority | Backend | Detection method |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| 1 (highest) | **SB16** | DSP reset probe at 0x220 / 0x240 / 0x260 / 0x280; version ≥ 4.x |
| 2 | **AC97** | PCI scan for Intel ICH / AMD AC97 vendor:device IDs |
| 3 | **ISA speaker** | Always available — PIT channel 2 + port 0x61 |
| 4 (stub) | **HDA** | PCI scan for Intel/AMD/NVIDIA HDA IDs; detected only, no playback |

All four probes always run at boot. Results are logged to the kernel console. The highest-priority detected backend becomes active; ISA speaker is the guaranteed fallback.

### API

```c
void         vb_beep(uint32_t freq_hz, uint32_t duration_ms);  /* play tone */
void         vb_stop(void);                                      /* stop tone */
vb_backend_t vb_active_backend(void);                           /* query backend */
const char  *vb_backend_name(void);                             /* "SB16", "AC97", … */
```

Userland access: `SYS_BEEP` syscall (number 41) — `rdi`=freq_hz, `rsi`=duration_ms.

---

## Syscall Interface

Invoked via `int $0x80`. Arguments: `rax`=number, `rdi`, `rsi`, `rdx`, `rcx`, `r8`. Return in `rax`.

| # | Name | Arguments | Description |
|---|------|-----------|-------------|
| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | `rdi`=str, `rsi`=len | Print string to display |
| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | Clear screen |
| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | Print newline |
| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | `rdi`=addr | Read 64-bit physical address |
| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy CPU model string |
| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | ACPI power off |
| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | System reset |
| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | Voluntary CPU yield |
| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | Current process PID |
| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | `rdi`=entry, `rsi`=name, `rdx`=priority | Create + wake process |
| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | Fork current process |
| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | `rdi`=code | Terminate calling process |
| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | `rdi`=pid | Destroy process by PID |
| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | `rdi`=pid | Block until PID exits |
| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | `rdi`=ticks | Sleep for N scheduler ticks |
| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | `rdi`=pid, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=size | Get process name |
| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | Blocking ASCII keyboard read |
| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | `rdi`=source_ptr | Run JS string via MuJS |
| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | Seconds since boot |
| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | `rdi`=`*timespec`, `rsi`=clk_id | Fill timespec |
| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=flags, `rdx`=mode | Open file; returns fd |
| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | `rdi`=fd | Close file |
| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Read bytes |
| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Write bytes |
| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=offset, `rdx`=whence | Seek file |
| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by path |
| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by fd |
| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=index, `rdx`=dirent_ptr | Read directory entry |
| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Make directory |
| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | `rdi`=path | Unlink file |
| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Create file; returns fd |
| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | Blocking read; returns `KEY_*` or ASCII |
| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | `rdi`=path | Change kernel working directory |
| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy kernel cwd to buffer |
| 34 | `SYS_PS` | `rdi`=`proc_snap_t*`, `rsi`=max | Fill process snapshot array |
| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | Non-blocking key read; -1 if no key pending |
| 36 | `SYS_DISPLAY_MODE` | — | Returns 0=VGA text, 1=GOP framebuffer |
| 37 | `SYS_PUTCELL` | `rdi`=col, `rsi`=row, `rdx`=packed | Draw char at (col,row) without moving cursor |
| 38 | `SYS_SETCURSOR` | `rdi`=col, `rsi`=row | Move logical cursor |
| 39 | `SYS_FB_INFO` | `rdi`=`fb_info_t*` | Fill framebuffer info struct |
| 40 | `SYS_FB_FILLRECT` | `rdi`=x, `rsi`=y, `rdx`=w, `rcx`=h, `r8`=rgb32 | Fill rectangle |
| 41 | `SYS_BEEP` | `rdi`=freq_hz, `rsi`=duration_ms | Play audio tone via VoiceBox |

---

## Toolchain

| Tool | Role |
|------|------|
| `clang --target=x86_64-elf` | Freestanding C compiler |
| `ld.lld` | ELF64 linker |
| `nasm -f elf64` | Assembler (all `.asm` files) |
| `grub-mkrescue` | ISO image builder |
| `qemu-system-x86_64` | Emulator |
| `python tools/js2ch.py` | JS → C string-literal header |

Key compiler flags: `--target=x86_64-elf -ffreestanding -O2 -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel`

---

## References

- [OSDev Wiki](https://wiki.osdev.org/)

- [MuJS](https://mujs.com/) — lightweight JavaScript interpreter

toolUseResult.content:
# NephilimOS

A MINIX-inspired, bare-metal x86_64 operating system built from scratch — with a JavaScript userland, a C snake game, GOP framebuffer graphics, and a multi-backend audio driver.

---

## What It Is

NephilimOS is a 64-bit hobby kernel that boots via GRUB Multiboot 2 (UEFI + GOP framebuffer) with a Multiboot 1 legacy fallback, runs a cooperative scheduler, mounts an ext2 filesystem from a RAM disk, and executes user-facing apps written in JavaScript via an embedded MuJS interpreter. All userland runs at Ring 1 through an `int $0x80` syscall interface. A native C snake game renders in full pixel graphics on the GOP path or falls back to ASCII on VGA text mode.

> **Note:** The Multiboot 1 / BIOS legacy boot path is present and selectable from the GRUB menu but is currently non-functional. UEFI + Multiboot 2 is the supported boot path.

---

## Feature Summary

### Boot & Hardware
- **Multiboot 2** (primary) — UEFI boot via GRUB with GOP framebuffer negotiation
- **Multiboot 1** (legacy fallback) — BIOS VGA text mode (menu entry present; fix pending)
- Dual-header boot binary — single kernel image supports both bootloaders
- 32-bit protected mode → 64-bit long mode transition in assembly
- Identity-mapped first **4 GiB** via 2 MiB huge pages (PML4 → PDPT → 4× PD) — covers GOP framebuffer at 0x80000000
- **GOP framebuffer driver** (`drivers/gop.c`) — pixel renderer with 8×16 font blitting, palette-to-RGB mapping, QEMU virtio-gpu quirk handling
- VGA text-mode driver (80×25, colour, custom 8×16 bitmap font) — fallback when GOP unavailable
- PS/2 keyboard driver — scancode set 1, shift/ctrl modifiers, extended keys
- PIT timer at 100 Hz (IRQ0) — monotonic tick + second counter
- 8259 PIC remapped to vectors 32–47
- **VoiceBox audio driver** — multi-backend audio with boot confirmation chime

### Kernel Subsystems
- GDT with Ring 0 / Ring 1 descriptors and a 64-bit TSS
- IDT with 256 gates; CPU exceptions print vector + RIP and halt
- `int 0x80` syscall interface (Ring 0 and Ring 1 callable)
- Cooperative multi-process scheduler (FCFS + SJF + priority aging + exhaustion penalties)
- `proc_create`, `proc_fork`, `proc_destroy`, `proc_switch` process lifecycle
- **minit** — miniature init: lightweight process supervisor that restarts watched processes up to 3 times on death. Supervises the shell and VoiceBox.

### Memory
- First-fit free-list heap allocator (`malloc` / `free` / `realloc`) with block coalescing
- 64 MB heap in `.bss` (NOBITS — not stored in the ELF binary)
- PMM physical frame allocator (tracks free 4K pages from multiboot memory map; supports both MB1 and MB2 memory maps)
- VMM 4-level paging infrastructure

### Filesystem
- VFS mount layer with path walk and fd table
- ext2 filesystem (block groups, inodes, directory entries, bitmaps)
- RawFS in-memory block device as ext2 backing store
- File syscalls: open / close / read / write / seek / stat / fstat / readdir / mkdir / unlink / creat
- Standard directory seeding at boot: `/tmp`, `/home`, `/bin`, `/etc`

### JavaScript Userland
- MuJS interpreter embedded in the kernel (freestanding port)
- Ring-1 shell dispatches named apps via `SYS_JS_EXEC`
- Apps embedded as C string literals, executed in kernel MuJS context
- JS filesystem bindings: `fsOpen`, `fsRead`, `fsWrite`, `fsClose`, `fsReaddir`, `fsStat`, `fsMkdir`, `fsUnlink`, `fsCreat`
- JS terminal bindings: `print`, `println`, `readline`, `getChar`, `setCursor`, `setColor`, `screenWidth`, `screenHeight`, `clearScreen`, `printAt`
- JS path bindings: `chdir`, `getcwd`
- `__cwd` and `__args` preamble injected into every app execution

### Native C Snake Game
- Dual renderer: **GOP path** (MB2/UEFI) renders full pixel graphics; **VGA path** (MB1/BIOS) uses ASCII characters
- GOP path: 20×20 px cells, large board, coloured rectangles, pixel font sidebar
- Two switchable colour schemes (`C` key): **Green snake / Red fruit** and **Blue snake / Purple fruit**
- VGA path: 50×20 play area, blue snake (`@`/`o`), magenta apple (`*`)
- Right-panel HUD showing score and best score on both paths
- Highscore persisted to `/tmp/snake_hi` across sessions
- Speed increases dynamically with score

### Audio — VoiceBox
- **SB16** (primary) — Sound Blaster 16 probe at I/O bases 0x220 / 0x240 / 0x260 / 0x280
- **AC97** (secondary) — Intel AC'97 codec probe via PCI scan (ICH, ICH0, ICH2, ICH3, ICH4, AMD)
- **ISA speaker** (fallback) — PIT channel 2 + port 0x61; always available
- **HDA** (experimental) — Intel HD Audio probe via PCI scan; detected and logged, no playback yet
- Boot confirmation chime plays once after shell initialisation
- `SYS_BEEP(freq_hz, duration_ms)` syscall exposed to userland
- Supervised by minit — restarted on crash

### Standard Library
- Freestanding libc: `string`, `stdlib`, `stdio` (printf/snprintf), `math`, `setjmp`, `time`
- POSIX-compatible time: `gmtime_r`, `mktime`, `strftime`, `clock_gettime`

---

## Project Structure

```
NephilimOS/
├── boot/
│   ├── boot.asm          # Dual MB2+MB1 headers; 4 GiB page tables; long-mode transition
│   ├── gdt_flush.asm     # Reload segment registers + far-return to new CS
│   ├── isr_stubs.asm     # 256 ISR stubs; uniform int_frame_t layout
│   ├── ctx_switch.asm    # Low-level context switch (callee-saved regs)
│   └── font8x16.asm      # 8×16 VGA/GOP bitmap font data
├── kernel/
│   ├── kernel.c          # kernel_main; MB2/MB1 detection; subsystem init; minit; idle loop
│   ├── vga.c             # Dual-path display driver: VGA text + GOP pixel rendering
│   ├── gop.c             # GOP framebuffer driver; glyph blitter; fill rect; palette convert
│   ├── detect.c          # CPUID CPU detection; RAM from multiboot info
│   ├── gdt.c             # GDT + TSS setup
│   ├── idt.c             # IDT init; int_handler; IRQ dispatch; syscall vector
│   ├── timer.c           # PIC remap; PIT 100 Hz; timer_irq_handler; timer_gettime
│   ├── syscall.c         # syscall_dispatch; all sys_* implementations; kernel_cwd
│   ├── process.c         # PCB; scheduler; proc_create/fork/destroy/switch
│   ├── pmm.c             # Physical memory manager (MB1 + MB2 init paths)
│   ├── vmm.c             # Virtual memory manager (4-level paging)
│   ├── js_runtime.c      # MuJS engine init; all JS bindings
│   ├── seed.c            # initramfs: seeds /bin/* from embedded JS source
│   └── minit.c           # miniature init — process supervisor
├── drivers/
│   ├── keyboard.c        # PS/2 keyboard; scancode→KEY_*; keyboard_getkey()
│   ├── serial.c          # Serial port output (QEMU debug console)
│   └── voicebox.c        # VoiceBox audio driver: SB16 / AC97 / ISA speaker / HDA
├── fs/
│   ├── ext2.c            # ext2 implementation (block groups, inodes, bitmaps)
│   ├── rawfs.c           # Raw in-memory block device + ext2 mount wrapper
│   └── vfs.c             # VFS mount table + path walk + fd table
├── lib/
│   └── libtranslateunix.c # Unix-like path helpers for VFS
├── stdlib/
│   ├── string.c          # memset/cpy/move/cmp, strlen, str*
│   ├── stdlib.c          # abs, atoi, itoa, utoa
│   ├── malloc.c          # First-fit free-list allocator with coalescing
│   ├── math.c            # Freestanding math (sin, cos, sqrt, log, exp, …)
│   ├── printf.c          # Freestanding printf / snprintf / vsnprintf
│   ├── setjmp.asm        # setjmp / longjmp
│   └── time.c            # POSIX time: gmtime_r, mktime, strftime, format_time
├── userland/
│   ├── shell/
│   │   └── main.c        # Ring-1 shell; line editing; history; app dispatch
│   ├── snake/
│   │   └── main.c        # Dual-renderer snake: GOP pixel + VGA ASCII; colour schemes
│   ├── apps.c            # JS app registry; cwd+args preamble injection
│   ├── calculator/
│   │   └── main.js       # Expression evaluator (MuJS)
│   ├── coreutils/
│   │   ├── ls.js         # List directory
│   │   ├── cat.js        # Print file contents
│   │   ├── touch.js      # Create empty file
│   │   ├── mkdir.js      # Create directory
│   │   ├── rm.js         # Remove file
│   │   ├── stat.js       # Show file metadata
│   │   ├── write.js      # Write text to file
│   │   ├── wc.js         # Count lines/words/bytes
│   │   ├── uname.js      # Show system information
│   │   ├── cd.js         # Change directory (path normalisation + chdir binding)
│   │   ├── ps.js         # List running processes
│   │   ├── cp.js         # Copy file
│   │   ├── mv.js         # Move / rename file
│   │   ├── grep.js       # Search lines matching pattern
│   │   └── sed.js        # Substitute pattern in file output
│   └── quill/
│       └── main.js       # Full-screen text editor (nano-like)
├── include/              # All public headers
│   ├── voicebox.h        # VoiceBox API + backend enum
│   ├── gop.h             # GOP framebuffer info + drawing API
│   └── syscall.h         # Syscall numbers + inline helpers + fb_info_t
├── external/
│   └── include/mujs/     # MuJS JS engine (vendored); mujs_port.h shims libc
├── grub/
│   └── grub.cfg          # GRUB menu: MB2/UEFI entry + MB1/BIOS legacy entry
├── linker.ld             # Kernel linker script; .mb2hdr section; loads at 1 MiB
├── tools/
│   └── js2ch.py          # Converts .js source to C string-literal .js.h header
└── Makefile
```

---

## Prerequisites

All build steps run inside **WSL2**. The toolchain uses **Clang/LLVM** — no GCC cross-compiler required.

### openSUSE (Tumbleweed / Leap)

```bash
sudo zypper install clang llvm lld nasm
sudo zypper install grub2-x86_64-efi xorriso
sudo zypper install qemu-x86 qemu-ovmf-x86_64
```

### Ubuntu / Debian

```bash
sudo apt install clang lld nasm grub-pc-bin grub-efi-amd64-bin grub-common xorriso qemu-system-x86 ovmf
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
sudo pacman -S clang lld nasm grub xorriso qemu-system-x86 edk2-ovmf
```

---

## Build

```bash
# Compile and link the kernel ELF
make

# Build a bootable ISO image
make iso

# Clean all build artifacts
make clean
```

Outputs:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `nephilim.kernel` | ELF64 kernel binary |
| `nephilim.iso` | Bootable ISO (GRUB + kernel) |

---

## Run

```bash
# UEFI / Multiboot 2 (recommended — GOP framebuffer)
make run-uefi

# BIOS / Multiboot 1 (legacy — VGA text mode, currently broken)
make run

# With serial debug output on host terminal
qemu-system-x86_64 -cdrom nephilim.iso -m 256M -serial stdio -no-reboot -no-shutdown \
  -drive if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on,file=/usr/share/qemu/ovmf-x86_64-4m-code.bin \
  -drive if=pflash,format=raw,file=/tmp/nephilim-ovmf-vars.bin
```

---

## Boot Flow

```
GRUB menu
  ├─ "NephilimOS (Multiboot 2 / UEFI)"   ← primary
  │     insmod all_video
  │     set gfxpayload=keep
  │     multiboot2 /boot/nephilim.kernel
  │
  └─ "NephilimOS (Multiboot 1 / BIOS legacy)"   ← fallback (fix pending)
        multiboot /boot/nephilim.kernel

_start  [32-bit protected mode]
  ├─ Check CPUID / long-mode support
  ├─ Build identity-mapped page tables (4 GiB: PML4→PDPT→p2_table_0..3)
  ├─ Enable PAE + EFER.LME, enable paging
  ├─ Load 64-bit GDT, far-jump to long mode
  └─ kernel_main(mb_magic, mb_info_phys)
       ├─ serial_init
       ├─ MB2: parse framebuffer tag → gop_init → vga_init_gop
       │  MB1: vga_init (VGA text mode)
       ├─ vga_clear
       ├─ gdt_init / idt_init / timer_init / sti
       ├─ syscall_init / keyboard_init / process_init
       ├─ detect_cpu / pmm_init_mb2 or pmm_init_mb1 / vmm_init
       ├─ vfs_init / rawfs+ext2 mount / initramfs_seed
       ├─ minit_init
       ├─ proc_create(shell_main)  → proc_wake → minit_watch
       ├─ proc_create(voicebox_main) → proc_wake → minit_watch
       └─ sched_pick → proc_switch
            idle loop: minit_tick() + hlt

voicebox_main (Ring-1 process, supervised by minit):
  ├─ vb_init: probe SB16 → AC97 → HDA → ISA speaker
  ├─ Play single 1000 Hz / 120 ms confirmation chime
  └─ hlt forever
```

---

## Shell

The Ring-1 shell (`userland/shell/main.c`) provides full line editing and history navigation.

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Description |

| `help` | Show available commands |

| `pwd` | Print working directory |

| `pid` | Show current process PID |
| `reboot` | Reboot (ACPI reset reg 0xCF9, then PS/2 fallback) |
| `shutdown` | Power off (ACPI PM1a 0x604, PIIX4 0xB004, microvm 0x4004) |
| `snake` | Native C snake game |

### JS Coreutils

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `cd [path]` | Change directory (supports `~`, `.`, `..`) |
| `ls [path]` | List directory contents |
| `cat <file>` | Print file contents |

| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory |

| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file |
| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move / rename file |
| `stat <file>` | Show file metadata |
| `write <file> <text>` | Write text to file |
| `wc <file>` | Count lines / words / bytes |
| `grep <pat> <file>` | Print lines matching pattern |
| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute pattern in file output |
| `uname` | Show system information |
| `ps` | List running processes |
| `calculator` | Expression evaluator |
| `quill [file]` | Full-screen text editor |

### Key Bindings

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Up` / `Down` | Navigate history |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor within line |
| `Home` / `End` | Jump to start / end of line |
| `Backspace` | Delete character left of cursor |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |

---

## Snake Game

Type `snake` at the shell prompt to launch.

### GOP Path (UEFI / MB2)

Full pixel rendering on the GOP framebuffer. Cell size 20×20 px. Board sized to ~85% of screen height. Right sidebar shows score, controls, and current colour scheme.

| Control | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `W` / `↑` | Move up |
| `S` / `↓` | Move down |
| `A` / `←` | Move left |
| `D` / `→` | Move right |
| `P` | Pause / resume |
| `C` | Cycle colour scheme |
| `Q` | Quit |

**Colour schemes:**

| Scheme | Snake | Fruit |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Green / Red (default) | Bright green head, green body | Red |
| Blue / Purple | Cyan-blue head, blue body | Purple |

### VGA Path (BIOS / MB1)

ASCII character grid, 50×20 play area. Blue snake (`@` head, `o` body), magenta apple (`*`).

The highscore is saved as a plain decimal integer to `/tmp/snake_hi` after each game. Speed increases dynamically with score.

---

## VoiceBox Audio Driver

VoiceBox is a kernel audio driver launched as a supervised Ring-1 process by minit.

### Backend Priority

| Priority | Backend | Detection method |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| 1 (highest) | **SB16** | DSP reset probe at 0x220 / 0x240 / 0x260 / 0x280; version ≥ 4.x |
| 2 | **AC97** | PCI scan for Intel ICH / AMD AC97 vendor:device IDs |
| 3 | **ISA speaker** | Always available — PIT channel 2 + port 0x61 |
| 4 (stub) | **HDA** | PCI scan for Intel/AMD/NVIDIA HDA IDs; detected only, no playback |

All four probes always run at boot. Results are logged to the kernel console. The highest-priority detected backend becomes active; ISA speaker is the guaranteed fallback.

### API

```c
void         vb_beep(uint32_t freq_hz, uint32_t duration_ms);  /* play tone */
void         vb_stop(void);                                      /* stop tone */
vb_backend_t vb_active_backend(void);                           /* query backend */
const char  *vb_backend_name(void);                             /* "SB16", "AC97", … */
```

Userland access: `SYS_BEEP` syscall (number 41) — `rdi`=freq_hz, `rsi`=duration_ms.

---

## Syscall Interface

Invoked via `int $0x80`. Arguments: `rax`=number, `rdi`, `rsi`, `rdx`, `rcx`, `r8`. Return in `rax`.

| # | Name | Arguments | Description |
|---|------|-----------|-------------|
| 0 | `SYS_PRINT` | `rdi`=str, `rsi`=len | Print string to display |
| 1 | `SYS_CLEAR` | — | Clear screen |
| 2 | `SYS_NEWLINE` | — | Print newline |
| 3 | `SYS_READ_MEM` | `rdi`=addr | Read 64-bit physical address |
| 4 | `SYS_CPU_MODEL` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy CPU model string |
| 5 | `SYS_SHUTDOWN` | — | ACPI power off |
| 6 | `SYS_REBOOT` | — | System reset |
| 7 | `SYS_YIELD` | — | Voluntary CPU yield |
| 8 | `SYS_GETPID` | — | Current process PID |
| 9 | `SYS_SPAWN` | `rdi`=entry, `rsi`=name, `rdx`=priority | Create + wake process |
| 10 | `SYS_FORK` | — | Fork current process |
| 11 | `SYS_EXIT` | `rdi`=code | Terminate calling process |
| 12 | `SYS_KILL` | `rdi`=pid | Destroy process by PID |
| 13 | `SYS_WAIT` | `rdi`=pid | Block until PID exits |
| 14 | `SYS_SLEEP` | `rdi`=ticks | Sleep for N scheduler ticks |
| 15 | `SYS_GETPNAME` | `rdi`=pid, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=size | Get process name |
| 16 | `SYS_GETCHAR` | — | Blocking ASCII keyboard read |
| 17 | `SYS_JS_EXEC` | `rdi`=source_ptr | Run JS string via MuJS |
| 18 | `SYS_TIME` | — | Seconds since boot |
| 19 | `SYS_CLOCK_GETTIME` | `rdi`=`*timespec`, `rsi`=clk_id | Fill timespec |
| 20 | `SYS_FS_OPEN` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=flags, `rdx`=mode | Open file; returns fd |
| 21 | `SYS_FS_CLOSE` | `rdi`=fd | Close file |
| 22 | `SYS_FS_READ` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Read bytes |
| 23 | `SYS_FS_WRITE` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=buf, `rdx`=len | Write bytes |
| 24 | `SYS_FS_SEEK` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=offset, `rdx`=whence | Seek file |
| 25 | `SYS_FS_STAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by path |
| 26 | `SYS_FS_FSTAT` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=stat_ptr | Stat by fd |
| 27 | `SYS_FS_READDIR` | `rdi`=fd, `rsi`=index, `rdx`=dirent_ptr | Read directory entry |
| 28 | `SYS_FS_MKDIR` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Make directory |
| 29 | `SYS_FS_UNLINK` | `rdi`=path | Unlink file |
| 30 | `SYS_FS_CREAT` | `rdi`=path, `rsi`=mode | Create file; returns fd |
| 31 | `SYS_GETKEY` | — | Blocking read; returns `KEY_*` or ASCII |
| 32 | `SYS_CHDIR` | `rdi`=path | Change kernel working directory |
| 33 | `SYS_GETCWD` | `rdi`=buf, `rsi`=size | Copy kernel cwd to buffer |
| 34 | `SYS_PS` | `rdi`=`proc_snap_t*`, `rsi`=max | Fill process snapshot array |
| 35 | `SYS_GETKEY_NB` | — | Non-blocking key read; -1 if no key pending |
| 36 | `SYS_DISPLAY_MODE` | — | Returns 0=VGA text, 1=GOP framebuffer |
| 37 | `SYS_PUTCELL` | `rdi`=col, `rsi`=row, `rdx`=packed | Draw char at (col,row) without moving cursor |
| 38 | `SYS_SETCURSOR` | `rdi`=col, `rsi`=row | Move logical cursor |
| 39 | `SYS_FB_INFO` | `rdi`=`fb_info_t*` | Fill framebuffer info struct |
| 40 | `SYS_FB_FILLRECT` | `rdi`=x, `rsi`=y, `rdx`=w, `rcx`=h, `r8`=rgb32 | Fill rectangle |
| 41 | `SYS_BEEP` | `rdi`=freq_hz, `rsi`=duration_ms | Play audio tone via VoiceBox |

---

## Toolchain

| Tool | Role |
|------|------|
| `clang --target=x86_64-elf` | Freestanding C compiler |
| `ld.lld` | ELF64 linker |
| `nasm -f elf64` | Assembler (all `.asm` files) |
| `grub-mkrescue` | ISO image builder |
| `qemu-system-x86_64` | Emulator |
| `python tools/js2ch.py` | JS → C string-literal header |

Key compiler flags: `--target=x86_64-elf -ffreestanding -O2 -mno-red-zone -mcmodel=kernel`

---
