## 5. Filesystem Stack

```
JS app / shell
     │  (path string)
     ▼
  VFS layer  (vfs.c)
     │  (vfs_driver_t interface)
     ▼
  RawFS  (rawfs.c)
     │  (block read/write)
     ▼
  ext2  (ext2.c)
     │  (in-memory byte array)
     ▼
  nephilim_disk_image[]  (declared in rawfs.c)
```

### 5.1 VFS Layer

**File:** `fs/vfs.c`, `include/fs.h`

Single mount table entry for `/`. Translates POSIX-style paths to driver calls.

```c
int vfs_mount(const char *path, vfs_driver_t *drv, void *ctx, size_t size);
int vfs_open(const char *path, int flags, uint16_t mode);
int vfs_close(int fd);
int vfs_read(int fd, void *buf, size_t len);
int vfs_write(int fd, const void *buf, size_t len);
int vfs_stat(const char *path, fs_stat_t *st);
int vfs_fstat(int fd, fs_stat_t *st);
int vfs_readdir(int fd, uint32_t index, fs_dirent_t *de);
int vfs_mkdir(const char *path, uint16_t mode);
int vfs_unlink(const char *path);
int vfs_creat(const char *path, uint16_t mode);
```

### 5.2 ext2 Implementation

**File:** `fs/ext2.c`

Block size: 1024 bytes. Single block group layout.

**Block layout:**

| Block | Contents |
|-------|----------|
| 0 | (unused) |
| 1 | Superblock |
| 2 | Block Group Descriptor (BGD) |
| 3 | Block bitmap |
| 4 | Inode bitmap |
| 5 … 5+N-1 | Inode table (N = ceil(max_inodes × 128 / 1024)) |
| 5+N … end | Data blocks |

### 5.3 RawFS Block Device

**File:** `fs/rawfs.c`, `include/rawfs.h`

`nephilim_disk_image[]` is a static byte array (`RAWFS_IMAGE_SIZE` bytes). `rawfs_driver` is the `vfs_driver_t` that delegates to ext2.

### 5.4 initramfs Seed

**File:** `kernel/seed.c`

Called once at boot after the filesystem is mounted. Writes JS app source files to `/bin/`:

| Path | Source |
|------|--------|
| `/bin/ls` | `userland/coreutils/ls.js` |
| `/bin/cat` | `userland/coreutils/cat.js` |
| `/bin/touch` | `userland/coreutils/touch.js` |
| `/bin/mkdir` | `userland/coreutils/mkdir.js` |
| `/bin/rm` | `userland/coreutils/rm.js` |
| `/bin/stat` | `userland/coreutils/stat.js` |
| `/bin/write` | `userland/coreutils/write.js` |
| `/bin/wc` | `userland/coreutils/wc.js` |
| `/bin/uname` | `userland/coreutils/uname.js` |
| `/bin/cd` | `userland/coreutils/cd.js` |
| `/bin/calculator` | `userland/calculator/main.js` |
| `/bin/quill` | `userland/quill/main.js` |
| `/bin/ps` | `userland/coreutils/ps.js` |

**File:** `lib/libtranslateunix.c`, `include/libtranslateunix.h`

Translates standard POSIX calls into VFS operations.

**File I/O:**

| `unix_open(path, flags, mode)` | `open()` | Maps O_* flags to vfs_open |
| `unix_close(fd)` | `close()` | |

## 6. Heap Allocator

**File:** `stdlib/malloc.c`

First-fit free-list allocator with immediate coalescing.

**Block header (16 bytes):**

```c
typedef struct block {
    size_t        size;    /* usable payload bytes */
    uint32_t      free;    /* 1 = free, 0 = allocated */
    uint32_t      _pad;
    struct block *next;
} block_t;
```

**`malloc(n)`:** First-fit walk; splits if remainder ≥ 32 bytes.

**`free(ptr)`:** Marks free, coalesces adjacent free blocks.

**`realloc(ptr, n)`:** In-place if block is large enough; otherwise malloc + memcpy + free.

**Arena:** `_heap_start` to `_heap_end` (64 MiB in `.bss`). Initialised lazily on first `malloc`.

---

## 7. JavaScript Userland

### 7.1 MuJS Integration

**File:** `kernel/js_runtime.c`, `external/include/mujs/`

MuJS is compiled freestanding against `mujs_port.h` which maps `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` to the kernel allocator. `js_runtime_run(const char *source)` creates a fresh `js_State`, registers all bindings, injects the signal prelude, evaluates the source, and destroys the state. Each app invocation is isolated.

#### 7.1.1 Signal Library

**File:** `userland/signal.js` — injected automatically into every JS state.

| Primitive | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `signal(v)` | Reactive value with `.get()`, `.set()`, `.peek()`, `.sub()` |
| `computed(fn)` | Read-only derived signal, lazy recompute |
| `effect(sigs, fn)` | Runs fn immediately; re-runs on signal change |
| `batch(fn)` | Defers notifications until fn returns; each subscriber fires at most once |

### 7.2 JS Bindings Reference

**Output:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `print(s)` | Print string without newline |
| `println(s)` | Print string + newline |

**Input:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `readline()` | Block until Enter; return line string |
| `getChar()` | Block until key; return int |
| `getKeyNonBlock()` | Return key code immediately; -1 if none |
| `getTicks()` | PIT tick counter (100 Hz) |

**Filesystem:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `fsOpen(path, mode)` | `"r"` or `"w"`; returns fd |
| `fsClose(fd)` | Close fd |
| `fsRead(fd, len)` | Read up to len bytes; returns string or null |
| `fsWrite(fd, s)` | Write string |
| `fsReaddir(fd, index)` | Returns `{name, ino}` or null |
| `fsStat(path)` | Returns `{size, mode, ino}` or null |
| `fsMkdir(path)` | Create directory |
| `fsUnlink(path)` | Remove file |
| `fsCreat(path)` | Create file; returns fd |

**Terminal:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `clearScreen()` | Clear display |
| `setCursor(col, row)` | Move hardware cursor |
| `setColor(fg, bg)` | Set VGA colour (0–15 each) |
| `printAt(col, row, s)` | Print at specific position |
| `screenWidth()` | Returns 80 |
| `screenHeight()` | Returns 25 |

**Path / CWD:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `chdir(path)` | Change kernel cwd |
| `getcwd()` | Returns cwd string |

**Process:**

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `getProcessList()` | Array of `{pid, name, state, ring, priority}` |

### 7.3 App Registry & Dispatch

**File:** `userland/apps.c`

`app_run(cmdline, cwd)` looks up the command, reads `/bin/<name>` via VFS, prepends the preamble, and calls `SYS_JS_EXEC`. Falls back to the embedded string literal if VFS read fails.

### 7.4 Cwd & Args Preamble

```javascript
var __cwd="<current directory>";
var __args=["arg1","arg2",...];
```

Prepended before every app execution. Apps read these as:

```javascript
var cwd  = typeof __cwd  !== 'undefined' ? __cwd  : '/';
var args = typeof __args !== 'undefined' ? __args : [];
```

---

## 8. Shell

**File:** `userland/shell/main.c`

Runs as a Ring-1 process (`RING_SERVICE`). Supervised by minit.

### 8.1 Built-in Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `help` | Show available commands |
| `clear` / `cls` | Clear the screen |
| `echo <text>` | Print text |
| `pwd` | Print working directory |
| `cpu` | Show CPU model string |
| `pid` | Show current process PID |
| `reboot` | System reset |
| `shutdown` | Power off |
| `snake` | Native C snake game |

### 8.2 Line Editing

| Key | Behaviour |
|-----|-----------|
| Printable ASCII | Insert at cursor, shift right |
| `Backspace` | Delete left of cursor |
| `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor |
| `Home` / `End` | Jump to beginning / end |
| `Enter` | Finalise line |
| `Ctrl+C` | Clear line |
| `Up` / `Down` | History navigation |

### 8.3 History Ring

32-entry circular buffer. Consecutive duplicates suppressed. Up/Down browsing saves and restores the live input.

---

## 9. JS Apps Reference

### 9.1 coreutils

| App | Usage | Notes |
|-----|-------|-------|
| `ls [path]` | List directory | Defaults to cwd |
| `cat <file>` | Print file | |
| `touch <file>` | Create empty file | |
| `mkdir <dir>` | Create directory | |
| `rm <file>` | Remove file | |
| `stat <file>` | Metadata | size, mode, inode |
| `write <file> <text>` | Write to file | Overwrites |
| `wc <file>` | Count | lines, words, bytes |
| `uname` | System info | CPU + kernel name |
| `cd [path]` | Change directory | `~` → `/home`; `.`/`..` resolved |
| `ps` | Process list | PID, ring, state, name |
| `cp <src> <dst>` | Copy file | |
| `mv <src> <dst>` | Move/rename | |
| `grep <pat> <file>` | Search lines | `.` `*` `^` `$` `[abc]` `[^abc]` |
| `sed s/p/r/[g] <file>` | Substitute | `g` flag replaces all occurrences |

### 9.2 calculator

Evaluates JavaScript expressions via `eval()` inside MuJS. `quit` or `exit` to leave.

### 9.3 quill

Full-screen text editor. `quill [filename]`.

**Layout (80×25):**

| Row | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| 0 | Title bar (filename, `[modified]` flag) |
| 1–23 | Text editing area |
| 24 | Status bar |

**Key bindings:**

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Up` / `Down` / `Left` / `Right` | Move cursor |
| `Home` / `End` | Start / end of line |
| `Backspace` | Delete left |
| `Del` | Delete under cursor |
| `Enter` | Insert newline |
| `Ctrl+S` | Save |
| `Ctrl+Q` | Quit (prompts if modified) |
| `Ctrl+X` | Force quit |

---

## 10. Snake Game

**File:** `userland/snake/main.c`

`snake_main()` calls `SYS_FB_INFO` at startup and dispatches to `snake_gop()` (GOP path) or `snake_vga()` (VGA path).

### GOP Path

Pixel renderer using `SYS_FB_FILLRECT` and `SYS_PUTCELL`.

**Geometry:** Cell size 20×20 px. Board fills ~85% of screen height. `G_cols ≈ G_rows × 1.5`. Right sidebar at `fb_width - 160`.

**Sidebar layout (pixel rows):**

| y | Content |
|---|---------|
| 32 | "SNAKE" title |
| 96 | "Score" label |
| 116 | Score value |
| 156 | "Best" label |
| 176 | Best value |
| 240 | "Controls:" heading |
| 264–324 | WASD/arrows, P pause, Q quit, C colors |
| 380 | Scheme indicator dot + name |

**Colour schemes:**

| Index | Head | Body | Fruit | Background |
|-------|------|------|-------|------------|
| 0 Green/Red | `#00FF88` | `#00CC44` | `#FF2233` | `#0D0D1A` |
| 1 Blue/Purple | `#44AAFF` | `#2266CC` | `#CC44FF` | `#080818` |

`C` key cycles schemes; `gop_repaint_all()` redraws the full screen.

### VGA Path

ASCII grid, `SYS_PUTCELL` for each cell. Board: 50×20 cells at screen position (1,2). HUD at column 55. Controls: WASD/arrows, P pause, Q quit.

---

## 11. Keyboard Key Codes

Defined in `include/keyboard.h`.

| Constant | Value | Key |
|----------|-------|-----|
| `KEY_UP` | 0x100 | Up arrow |
| `KEY_DOWN` | 0x101 | Down arrow |
| `KEY_LEFT` | 0x102 | Left arrow |
| `KEY_RIGHT` | 0x103 | Right arrow |
| `KEY_HOME` | 0x104 | Home |
| `KEY_END` | 0x105 | End |
| `KEY_PGUP` | 0x106 | Page Up |
| `KEY_PGDN` | 0x107 | Page Down |
| `KEY_DEL` | 0x108 | Delete |
| `KEY_F1`–`KEY_F10` | 0x110–0x119 | Function keys |

ASCII codes 0–127 returned directly. Ctrl+letter → 1–26.

---

## 12. Linker Script

**File:** `linker.ld`

```
ENTRY(_start)
OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-x86-64)

SECTIONS {
    . = 1M;

    .mb2hdr : { *(.mb2hdr) }   /* Multiboot 2 + MB1 headers — must be early */
    .text   : { *(.text*)  }
    .rodata : { *(.rodata*) }
    .data   : { *(.data*)  }

    .bss : ALIGN(4K) {
        *(COMMON)
        *(.bss)
        _heap_start = .;
        . += 64M;
        _heap_end   = .;
    }
}
```

The `.mb2hdr` section is placed first so GRUB can find the Multiboot headers within the required first 32 KiB. The heap lives in `.bss` (NOBITS) — the on-disk binary stays small.

---

## 8. Shell

lines: +Every JS app in NephilimOS is a plain `.js` file executed inside MuJS by `SYS_JS_EXEC`. The runtime is stateless — each invocation gets a fresh JS state — but the filesystem persists between runs.
+The conventional structure is an IIFE (immediately-invoked function expression) to keep all variables local and avoid polluting the global scope:
+`__cwd` and `__args` are injected by the shell/exec preamble (see §7.4). Guarding with `typeof` lets the script run safely even if executed directly via `SYS_JS_EXEC` without a preamble.
+Relative paths must be resolved against `__cwd` manually — MuJS has no concept of a working directory:
+Use this before every `fsOpen` / `fsStat` / `fsMkdir` call when the path might be relative.
+`fsRead` returns a string of up to `len` bytes, or `null` at EOF. Read in chunks — there is no guarantee the whole file arrives in one call.
+| `"w"` | Write; create if absent, truncate if present |
+| `"a"` | Append; create if absent, seek to end before every write |
+| `"r+"` | Read+write; file must exist; no truncation |
+Use `readline()` for line input and `getChar()` / `getKeyNonBlock()` for keypress-level control:
+For real-time apps (games, editors) poll `getKeyNonBlock()` and pace with `getTicks()` or `getGameTick()`:
+/* Draw a box at character cell (10, 5) */

+`vgaPoke(col, row, charCode, attr)` writes one cell. `attr` is a VGA attribute byte: low nibble = foreground colour (0–15), high nibble = background colour (0–7). On the GOP path the driver translates these to 24-bit RGB.
+`setColor(fg, bg)` sets the colour for subsequent `print` / `println` calls.
+`printAt(col, row, str)` prints a string starting at the given cell position without moving the global cursor.
+/* No direct JS binding — invoke via exec from the shell, or call from C.
+   JS apps cannot issue raw syscalls directly. */
+There is currently no `beep()` JS binding. Audio access from JS userland is planned but not yet exposed.
+/* hello.js — write a greeting, print it back */
+Or write it with quill, then run: `exec /home/USER NephilimOS`
+`exec` is a C shell built-in (not a JS app). It reads any `.js` file from the VFS, prepends the `__cwd`/`__args` preamble, and feeds the combined source to `SYS_JS_EXEC`.
+- `<path>` may be absolute (`/home/USER or relative to cwd.
+- There is no shebang support — the file must be valid JS.
+- Buffer limit: 16 KiB of source + 512 bytes of preamble headroom.
+To ship a script as a built-in app that the shell can invoke by name:
+4. Add an entry to the `apps[]` table in `apps.c`:
+5. Add an embedded source and seed entry in `kernel/seed.c` following the same `seed_myapp` pattern.
+6. The shell will now recognise `myapp` as a command, reading from `/bin/myapp` on disk with the embedded string as fallback.
